translations.Escala de Cornell - Depressão em Demênci: Cornell Scale for Depression in Dementia (CSDD)
translations.Calcule a Escala de Cornell para Depress: Calculate the Cornell Scale for Depression in Dementia (CSDD) and assess depressive symptoms in patients with cognitive impairment. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Escala de Cornell para Depressão em Demê: Cornell Scale for Depression in Dementia
translations.Avaliar a presença e gravidade de sintom: Assess the presence and severity of depressive symptoms in patients with cognitive impairment/dementia, based on 19 items grouped into 5 domains: mood signs, behavioral disturbance, physical signs, cyclic functions, and ideational disturbance.
translations.Indicada para rastreio de depressão em i: Indicated for screening depression in elderly with mild to severe dementia, especially when patient self-report is unreliable.
translations.Idosos com demência ou comprometimento c: Elderly with dementia or moderate to severe cognitive impairment, institutionalized or in outpatient/home care.
translations.Expressão triste, voz triste, choro fáci: Sad expression, sad voice, easy crying.
translations.A3. Falta de Reatividade a Eventos Agrad: A3. Lack of Reactivity to Pleasant Events
translations.Ausência de resposta emocional positiva: Absence of positive emotional response to pleasant events.
translations.Movimentos lentos, fala lenta, reações l: Slow movements, slow speech, slow reactions.
translations.Pontuar 0 se as queixas forem exclusivam: Score 0 if complaints are exclusively gastrointestinal.
translations.Menor envolvimento em atividades habitua: Less involvement in usual activities (score only if change occurred acutely, in less than 1 month).
translations.Pontuar 2 se maior que ~2,3 kg em um mês: Score 2 if greater than ~2.3 kg in one month.
translations.Fadiga fácil, incapaz de manter atividad: Easy fatigue, unable to maintain activities (score only if change occurred acutely, in less than 1 month).
translations.Mais tarde do que o habitual para este i: Later than usual for this individual.
translations.Interrupções frequentes do sono ao longo: Frequent sleep interruptions throughout the night.
translations.Mais cedo do que o habitual para este in: Earlier than usual for this individual.
translations.Sente que a vida não vale a pena (1), te: Feels life is not worth living (1), has active desire for death or recent attempt (2).
Delusions of poverty, illness, or loss.
translations.Pontuação de 0 a 10 pontos, compatível c: Score 0 to 10 points, consistent with absence of clinically significant depressive symptoms.
translations.• Manter acompanhamento de rotina\n• Rea:
• Maintain routine follow-up
• Reassess periodically and after relevant changes in clinical or cognitive status
translations.Pontuação entre 11 e 18 pontos sugere pr: Score between 11 and 18 points suggests probable major depressive episode.
translations.• Encaminhar para avaliação psiquiátrica:
• Refer for specialized psychiatric/geriatric evaluation
• Investigate reversible causes and associated comorbidities
• Consider therapeutic interventions (pharmacological and non-pharmacological) as per specialized evaluation
translations.Pontuação de 19 pontos ou mais, compatív: Score of 19 points or more, consistent with definite major depressive episode.
translations.• Encaminhamento psiquiátrico prioritári:
• Priority psychiatric referral
• Carefully assess suicide risk (item E1)
• Consider antidepressant treatment and intensive multidisciplinary follow-up
translations.Suscetibilidade a autolesão com intenção: Susceptibility to self-harm with intent to die, identified especially by the suicidal ideation item of the scale.
translations.Estado subjetivo em que a pessoa vê alte: Subjective state in which the individual sees limited or no personal alternatives and is unable to mobilize energy for their own benefit.
translations.Interrupções por tempo limitado da quant: Time-limited interruptions in the quantity and quality of sleep, related to items of cyclic functions on the scale.
translations.Ingestão de nutrientes insuficiente para: Insufficient nutrient intake to meet metabolic needs, related to loss of appetite and weight identified on the scale.
translations.Risco de suicídio avaliado e monitorado: Suicide risk assessed and monitored when ideation is present
translations.Aplicar a CSDD por meio de entrevista es: Administer the CSDD through a structured interview with the caregiver and a brief interview with the patient
translations.Investigar e minimizar a interferência d: Investigate and minimize interference of physical illnesses and disabilities in the scoring of somatic items
translations.Avaliar cuidadosamente qualquer ideação: Carefully evaluate any identified suicidal ideation and notify the medical team immediately
translations.Encaminhar para avaliação psiquiátrica/g: Refer for psychiatric/geriatric evaluation according to obtained score
translations.Reaplicar a escala periodicamente para m: Reapply the scale periodically to monitor response to interventions (e.g., antidepressants)
translations.A CSDD avalia 19 itens em 5 domínios — s: The CSDD assesses 19 items in 5 domains — mood signs (anxiety, sadness, lack of reactivity, irritability), behavioral disturbance (agitation, retardation, physical complaints, loss of interest), physical signs (appetite, weight, energy), cyclic functions (diurnal variation, sleep), and ideational disturbance (suicide, self-esteem, pessimism, delusions) — each scored from 0 to 2, totaling 0 to 38 points.
translations.Desenvolvida por Alexopoulos, Abrams, Yo: Developed by Alexopoulos, Abrams, Young, and Shamoian in 1988 at Cornell University, it was the first depression scale specifically validated for use in patients with dementia, combining caregiver information and direct patient observation.
translations.Amplamente validada internacionalmente p: Widely validated internationally for use in patients with mild to severe dementia, with good sensitivity and specificity. Scores above 10 suggest probable major depression, and above 18, definite major depression.
translations.Itens somáticos (queixas físicas, perda: Somatic items (physical complaints, loss of appetite/weight, lack of energy) may be confounded by physical illnesses or disabilities unrelated to depression — it is recommended to score 0 on these items when the symptom is clearly attributable to a physical cause. Requires training for reliable application of the structured interview.
translations.A CSDD é a escala de depressão mais util: The CSDD is the most widely used depression scale specifically for patients with dementia, combining caregiver interview and brief patient interview.
translations.Itens somáticos (queixas físicas, apetit: Somatic items (physical complaints, appetite, weight, energy) may be confounded by physical illnesses — score 0 when the symptom is clearly due to a physical cause, not emotional.
translations.O item de ideação suicida deve ser inves: The suicidal ideation item should be investigated with special care: a score of 1 indicates passive ideation (feels life is not worth living), a score of 2 indicates active ideation or recent attempt.
translations.Reaplique a escala periodicamente para m: Reapply the scale periodically to monitor response to therapeutic interventions, especially antidepressant treatment.
translations.Quantos itens compõem a Escala de Cornel: How many items make up the Cornell Scale (CSDD) and what is the maximum score?
translations.A CSDD avalia 19 itens, cada um pontuado: The CSDD assesses 19 items, each scored from 0 to 2, totaling a maximum score of 38 points.
translations.Como deve ser pontuado um item somático: How should a somatic item (e.g., loss of appetite) be scored when clearly caused by physical illness, and not by depression?
translations.Excluir o paciente da avaliação: Exclude the patient from the assessment
translations.Para evitar pontuações falsamente elevad: To avoid falsely elevated scores, somatic items clearly attributable to disability or physical illness should be scored as 0.
translations.Qual pontuação de corte sugere um episód: Which cutoff score suggests a major depressive episode defined by the CSDD?
translations.Pontuações acima de 18 sugerem um episód: Scores above 18 suggest a defined major depressive episode; above 10 already suggest a probable major depressive episode.
translations.A CSDD pode ser aplicada sem a colaboraç: Can the CSDD be applied without the caregiver's collaboration?
translations.Não é recomendado. A CSDD foi desenhada: It is not recommended. The CSDD was designed as a structured interview that combines the caregiver's report (observing the patient's behavior over the previous week) with a brief direct interview with the patient, and this combination is essential for its validity in patients with cognitive impairment.
translations.Doenças físicas podem inflar artificialm: Can physical illnesses artificially inflate the CSDD score?
translations.Sim. Idosos frequentemente têm doenças f: Yes. Elderly individuals often have physical illnesses with symptoms similar to depression (loss of appetite, weight, energy). To minimize falsely high scores, somatic items should be scored as 0 when clearly attributable to a physical condition, not a depressive disorder.
translations.<p>A CSDD soma a pontuação (0 a 2) de 19:
The CSDD sums the score (0 to 2) of 19 items distributed across 5 domains — mood signs, behavioral disturbance, physical signs, cyclic functions, and ideational disturbance — totaling 0 to 38 points.

Quick guide: 0-10 = No significant depression | 11-18 = Probable major depression | 19-38 = Definite major depression.

Indicated for depression screening in elderly patients with mild to severe dementia, especially when patient self-report is unreliable.

translations.SOCIEDADE BRASILEIRA DE GERIATRIA E GERO: SOCIEDADE BRASILEIRA DE GERIATRIA E GERONTOLOGIA. Diagnosis and management of depression in the elderly with dementia. São Paulo: SBGG, 2020.
translations.A CSDD soma a pontuação (0 a 2) de 19 it: The CSDD sums the scores (0 to 2) of 19 items distributed across 5 domains—mood signs, behavioral disturbance, physical signs, cyclic functions, and ideational disturbance—totaling 0 to 38 points.
translations.0-10 = Sem depressão significativa | 11-: 0-10 = No significant depression | 11-18 = Probable major depression | 19-38 = Definite major depression.
translations.CPC - Categoria de Performance Cerebral: CPC - Cerebral Performance Category Post-Cardiac Arrest
translations.Classifique o desfecho neurológico após: Classify neurological outcome after cardiac arrest using the Cerebral Performance Category (CPC). Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Classificar o desfecho neurológico funci: Classify the patient's functional neurological outcome after cardiac arrest and resuscitation on a standardized scale from 1 (good performance) to 5 (death/brain death).
translations.Indicada para pacientes sobreviventes de: Indicated for survivors of cardiac arrest, for neurological outcome assessment during hospitalization and post-discharge follow-up.
translations.Pacientes adultos sobreviventes de parad: Adult survivors of cardiac arrest, focusing on functional neurological assessment.
translations.Selecione a categoria que melhor descrev: Select the category that best describes the patient's current functional neurological outcome.
translations.Consciente, alerta, capaz de trabalhar e: Conscious, alert, able to work and lead a normal life; may have mild neurological or psychological deficit.
translations.Consciente, com incapacidade suficiente: Conscious, with disability sufficient to impair usual activities but able to perform independent activities of daily living; may work in a sheltered environment.
translations.Consciente, mas dependente de outros par: Conscious but dependent on others for daily activities due to brain impairment; ranges from ambulation to severe dementia or paralysis.
translations.Inconsciente, sem interação com o ambien: Unconscious, no interaction with environment, no evidence of cortical function (coma or persistent vegetative state).
translations.Desfecho neurológico favorável, com paci: Favorable neurological outcome, patient conscious, alert, and able to live independently.
translations.• Seguimento ambulatorial de rotina\n• A:
• Routine outpatient follow-up
• Assess need for rehabilitation for residual mild deficits, if present
translations.Incapacidade neurológica moderada, mas c: Moderate neurological disability but preservation of independence for basic activities.
translations.• Encaminhar para reabilitação neurológi:
• Refer for neurological rehabilitation as needed
• Multidisciplinary follow-up
• Assess support for return to adapted work activities
translations.Incapacidade neurológica grave, com depe: Severe neurological disability, dependent on others for activities of daily living.
translations.• Plano de cuidados de longo prazo com s:
• Long-term care plan with caregiver support
• Intensive neurological rehabilitation as indicated
• Support for family/caregivers
translations.Paciente inconsciente, sem interação com: Unconscious patient, no interaction with environment, no evidence of cortical function.
translations.• Cuidados de suporte prolongado\n• Disc:
• Prolonged supportive care
• Multidisciplinary discussion on prognosis and goals of care with family
• Prevention of complications of immobility
translations.Morte encefálica confirmada ou óbito do: Confirmed brain death or patient death.
translations.• Seguir protocolos institucionais para:
• Follow institutional protocols for brain death confirmation and family communication
• Consider evaluation for organ donation, per legislation and family wishes
translations.Diminuição da circulação sanguínea cereb: Decreased cerebral blood flow, which may compromise health, related to the cardiac arrest event.
translations.Funcionamento familiar que não consegue: Family functioning that fails to support the well-being of its members, related to the impact of the patient's neurological outcome.
translations.Processo normal e complexo que inclui re: A normal and complex process that includes emotional, physical, spiritual, social, and intellectual responses and behaviors by which individuals, families, and communities incorporate an actual, anticipated, or perceived loss into their daily lives.
translations.Nível de consciência compatível com a ca: Level of consciousness compatible with the identified category
translations.Classificar o desfecho neurológico com a: Classify neurological outcome using the CPC scale throughout hospitalization and follow-up
translations.Encaminhar para reabilitação neurológica: Refer for neurological rehabilitation according to identified category
translations.Apoiar a família na compreensão do progn: Support the family in understanding the neurological prognosis
translations.Documentar a evolução da categoria CPC a: Document the evolution of the CPC category over time
translations.A Categoria de Performance Cerebral (CPC: The Cerebral Performance Category (CPC) was developed in the context of the Brain Resuscitation Clinical Trial (BRCT), classifying the functional neurological outcome of survivors of cardiorespiratory arrest into 5 categories, from CPC 1 (good cerebral performance) to CPC 5 (death/brain death).
translations.Amplamente utilizada em pesquisa e práti: Widely used in research and clinical practice on cardiopulmonary resuscitation and post-cardiac arrest care, being one of the most used standardized neurological outcomes in studies on therapeutic hypothermia and other post-resuscitation interventions.
translations.Validada e amplamente adotada como desfe: Validated and widely adopted as a standard neurological outcome in resuscitation studies, recommended by international cardiopulmonary resuscitation guidelines (ILCOR/AHA).
translations.É uma escala relativamente simples, com: It is a relatively simple scale, with only 5 broad categories, which may not capture specific nuances of neurological outcome. Definitive assessment of neurological prognosis after cardiorespiratory arrest should consider multiple complementary exams and the time of clinical evolution.
translations.A avaliação definitiva do prognóstico ne: Definitive assessment of neurological prognosis after cardiorespiratory arrest is usually performed after 72 hours, combining clinical examination, electroencephalogram, and other complementary exams.
translations.Reavalie a categoria CPC ao longo do tem: Reassess the CPC category over time, as the neurological condition may evolve favorably or unfavorably in the following days/weeks.
translations.Utilize a escala para comunicação padron: Use the scale for standardized communication among the team and for documenting outcomes in institutional records.
translations.Envolva a família na discussão sobre pro: Involve the family in the discussion about prognosis, with clear and welcoming language.
translations.Quantas categorias compõem a escala CPC?: How many categories make up the CPC scale?
translations.A escala CPC possui 5 categorias, de CPC: The CPC scale has 5 categories, from CPC 1 (good cerebral performance) to CPC 5 (death/brain death).
translations.Coma ou estado vegetativo, sem evidência: Coma or vegetative state, with no evidence of cortical function
translations.CPC 4 representa coma ou estado vegetati: CPC 4 represents coma or persistent vegetative state, with no evidence of interaction with the environment or cortical function.
translations.Em que contexto a escala CPC é mais util: In what context is the CPC scale most used?
translations.Avaliação de desfecho neurológico pós-pa: Assessment of neurological outcome after cardiorespiratory arrest
translations.A CPC é utilizada especificamente para c: The CPC is used specifically to classify the functional neurological outcome of survivors of cardiorespiratory arrest.
translations.A escala CPC pode ser usada logo após a: Can the CPC scale be used immediately after resuscitation?
translations.Pode ser utilizada ao longo da internaçã: It can be used throughout hospitalization for monitoring, but definitive prognostic assessment is usually performed after at least 72 hours, combining clinical examination and complementary exams, according to resuscitation guidelines.
translations.A CPC substitui a Escala de Glasgow?: Does the CPC replace the Glasgow Coma Scale?
translations.Não. São ferramentas complementares: a E: No. They are complementary tools: the Glasgow Coma Scale assesses the level of consciousness in the acute moment, while the CPC classifies the global functional neurological outcome, usually used in later assessments or as a study outcome.
translations.Como funciona a Categoria de Performance: How the Cerebral Performance Category (CPC) works
translations.<p>A CPC classifica o desfecho neurológi:
The CPC classifies functional neurological outcome after cardiac arrest into 5 categories: CPC 1 (good cerebral performance), CPC 2 (moderate disability), CPC 3 (severe disability), CPC 4 (coma/vegetative state), and CPC 5 (death/brain death).

Quick guide: CPC 1-2 = Good neurological outcome | CPC 3-5 = Poor neurological outcome.
translations.CPC - Categoria de Performance Cerebral: CPC - Cerebral Performance Category Post-Cardiac Arrest | Nursing Calculators
translations.Categoria de Performance Cerebral: cálcu: Cerebral Performance Category: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Classificar o desfecho neurológico funci: Classify the patient's functional neurological outcome after cardiac arrest and resuscitation on a standardized scale from 1 (good performance) to 5 (death/brain death). Indicated for survivors of cardiac arrest, for neurological outcome assessment during hospitalization and post-discharge follow-up.
translations.BRAIN RESUSCITATION CLINICAL TRIAL I STU: BRAIN RESUSCITATION CLINICAL TRIAL I STUDY GROUP. A randomized clinical study of cardiopulmonary-cerebral resuscitation: design, methods, and patient characteristics. American Journal of Emergency Medicine, v. 4, n. 1, p. 72-86, 1986.
translations.PANCHAL, A. R. et al. 2020 American Hear: PANCHAL, A. R. et al. 2020 American Heart Association Guidelines for CPR and Emergency Cardiovascular Care. Circulation, v. 142, n. 16_suppl_2, p. S366-S468, 2020.
translations.A CPC classifica o desfecho neurológico: The CPC classifies functional neurological outcome after cardiac arrest into 5 categories: CPC 1 (good cerebral performance), CPC 2 (moderate disability), CPC 3 (severe disability), CPC 4 (coma/vegetative state), and CPC 5 (death/brain death).
translations.CPC 1-2 = Bom desfecho neurológico | CPC: CPC 1-2 = Good neurological outcome | CPC 3-5 = Poor neurological outcome.
translations.Calcule a Escala CRIES para avaliar a do: Calculate the CRIES Scale to assess postoperative pain in newborns through physiological and behavioral indicators. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Avaliar a dor pós-operatória em recém-na: Assess postoperative pain in newborns using 5 indicators: crying, need for supplemental oxygen, increased vital signs, facial expression, and sleeplessness.
translations.Indicada na avaliação de dor pós-operató: Indicated for postoperative pain assessment in newborns with gestational age ≥ 32 weeks admitted to the neonatal ICU.
translations.Recém-nascidos com 32 semanas de idade g: Newborns with gestational age ≥ 32 weeks in the postoperative period.
translations.Sem choro, ou choro não é agudo: No cry, or cry is not high-pitched
translations.Observar alterações na oxigenação; recém: Observe changes in oxygenation; newborns in pain may have drops in oxygen saturation.
translations.Comparar FC e PA com os valores basais p: Compare HR and BP with preoperative baseline values (stress-free period). Measure BP last, as it may awaken the newborn.
translations.FC e PA inalteradas ou menores que o bas: HR and BP unchanged or lower than baseline
translations.Aumento de FC ou PA em menos de 20% do b: Increase in HR or BP less than 20% from baseline
translations.Aumento de FC ou PA em mais de 20% do ba: Increase in HR or BP more than 20% from baseline
translations.A expressão facial mais associada à dor: The facial expression most associated with pain is grimacing (brow furrowing, eye squeezing, deepening of nasolabial furrow, open lips and mouth).
translations.Avaliar o estado do recém-nascido na hor: Assess the newborn's state in the hour prior to scoring.
translations.Pontuação de 0 a 3 pontos, compatível co: Score 0-3 points, consistent with no pain or minimal pain.
translations.• Manter conduta atual\n• Reavaliar conf:
• Maintain current management
• Reassess per institutional protocol
translations.Pontuação de 4 a 5 pontos sugere dor pre: Score 4-5 points suggests pain present, requiring further evaluation.
translations.• Realizar avaliação adicional da dor\n•:
• Perform additional pain assessment
• Consider non-pharmacologic comfort measures (non-nutritive sucking, facilitated tucking, holding)
• Reassess at short interval
translations.Pontuação de 6 pontos ou mais indica dor: Score ≥6 points indicates significant pain, with indication for analgesic administration.
translations.• Comunicar imediatamente à equipe médic:
• Immediately notify medical team
• Prioritize pharmacologic analgesic intervention per prescription
• Combine non-pharmacologic comfort measures
• Reassess at short interval after intervention
translations.Experiência sensorial e emocional desagr: Unpleasant sensory and emotional experience associated with actual tissue damage (postoperative), identified by physiological and behavioral indicators in the newborn.
translations.Escore CRIES avaliado de forma sistemáti: CRIES score systematically assessed in the postoperative period
translations.Utiliza medidas de alívio farmacológicas: Uses pharmacologic and non-pharmacologic relief measures as indicated
translations.Redução do escore CRIES após intervenção: Reduction in CRIES score after analgesic intervention
translations.Aplicar a CRIES regularmente no período: Apply CRIES regularly in the postoperative period per institutional protocol
translations.Comparar os sinais vitais com os valores: Compare vital signs with the newborn's own preoperative baseline values
translations.Utilizar medidas não farmacológicas de c: Use non-pharmacologic comfort measures (non-nutritive sucking, oral glucose, facilitated tucking)
translations.Administrar analgesia conforme prescriçã: Administer analgesia per prescription for high scores (≥6)
translations.Registrar a pontuação CRIES e a resposta: Record CRIES score and response to intervention in the medical record
translations.A CRIES avalia 5 indicadores — choro, ne: CRIES assesses 5 indicators — crying, need for supplemental oxygen, increased vital signs, facial expression, and sleeplessness — each scored from 0 to 2, totaling a score from 0 to 10 points, specific for postoperative pain in newborns.
translations.Desenvolvida por Krechel e Bildner em 19: Developed by Krechel and Bildner in 1995, CRIES was one of the first scales specifically constructed for postoperative pain assessment in neonates, combining objective physiological indicators with behavioral indicators.
translations.Validada para recém-nascidos com idade g: Validated for newborns with gestational age of 32 weeks or more in neonatal intensive care units, with good reliability and validity in detecting postoperative pain.
translations.Requer acesso a parâmetros basais pré-op: Requires access to preoperative baseline parameters (HR, BP, saturation) of the newborn for adequate comparison. Other causes of changes in vital signs and oxygenation (atelectasis, pneumothorax, excessive sedation) should be considered before attributing the change solely to pain.
translations.O nome CRIES é um acrônimo em inglês: Cr: The name CRIES is an acronym: Crying, Requires O2, Increased vital signs, Expression, and Sleeplessness.
translations.É essencial comparar a frequência cardía: It is essential to compare heart rate and blood pressure with the newborn's own preoperative baseline values, obtained during a stress-free period.
translations.Afira a pressão arterial por último, poi: Measure blood pressure last, as the procedure may awaken the newborn and hinder assessment of other items.
translations.Considere outras causas de alteração da: Consider other causes of oxygenation changes (atelectasis, pneumothorax, sedation) before attributing the drop in saturation solely to pain.
translations.O que significa o acrônimo CRIES?: What does the acronym CRIES stand for?
translations.Crying, Requires O2, Increased vital sig: Crying, Requires O2, Increased vital signs, Expression, Sleeplessness
translations.Color, Respiration, Increased HR, Expres: Color, Respiration, Increased HR, Expression, Sedation
translations.CRIES é a sigla para Crying (choro), Req: CRIES stands for Crying, Requires O2, Increased vital signs, Expression, and Sleeplessness.
translations.A partir de qual pontuação a administraç: From what score is administration of analgesia considered indicated in CRIES?
translations.Apenas com pontuação máxima de 10: Only with a maximum score of 10
translations.Pontuações de 4 ou mais já indicam neces: Scores of 4 or more already indicate need for additional evaluation, and from 6 points administration of analgesia is indicated.
translations.Para avaliar o item 'sinais vitais', com: To assess the 'vital signs' item, what should current HR and BP be compared with?
translations.Com valores de referência padrão para a: With standard reference values for age
translations.Com os valores basais pré-operatórios do: With the newborn's own preoperative baseline values
translations.Com os valores do último plantão: With values from the last shift
translations.Com os valores de outro recém-nascido da: With values from another newborn of the same gestational age
translations.A CRIES utiliza os valores basais pré-op: CRIES uses the newborn's own preoperative baseline values (obtained during a stress-free period) as a reference for comparison.
translations.A CRIES pode ser usada em qualquer recém: Can CRIES be used in any newborn?
translations.A CRIES foi validada para recém-nascidos: CRIES was validated for newborns with gestational age of 32 weeks or more, being specific for the context of postoperative pain in the neonatal ICU.
translations.Uma queda na saturação de oxigênio sempr: Does a drop in oxygen saturation always indicate pain?
translations.Não necessariamente. Outras causas como: Not necessarily. Other causes such as atelectasis, pneumothorax, or excessive sedation can also cause drops in oxygenation and should be considered in clinical assessment before attributing the change solely to pain.
translations.Como funciona a Escala CRIES: How does the CRIES Scale work?
translations.<p>A CRIES soma a pontuação (0 a 2) de 5:
CRIES sums the score (0 to 2) of 5 indicators — crying, need for O2, increased vital signs, facial expression, and sleeplessness — totaling 0 to 10 points.

Quick guide: 0-3 = No/minimal pain | 4-5 = Pain present, reassess | 6-10 = Significant pain, analgesia indicated.

translations.0 — FC e PA inalteradas ou menores que o: 0 — HR and BP unchanged or less than baseline
translations.1 — Aumento de FC ou PA em menos de 20%: 1 — Increase in HR or BP less than 20% from baseline
translations.2 — Aumento de FC ou PA em mais de 20% d: 2 — Increase in HR or BP more than 20% from baseline
translations.Avaliar a dor pós-operatória em recém-na: Assess postoperative pain in newborns using 5 indicators: crying, need for supplemental oxygen, increased vital signs, facial expression, and sleeplessness/alertness. Indicated for postoperative pain assessment in newborns with gestational age ≥ 32 weeks admitted to the neonatal ICU.

translations.A CRIES soma a pontuação (0 a 2) de 5 in: CRIES sums the score (0 to 2) of 5 indicators — crying, need for O2, increased vital signs, facial expression, and sleeplessness — totaling 0 to 10 points.
translations.0-3 = Sem dor/mínima | 4-5 = Dor present: 0-3 = No/minimal pain | 4-5 = Pain present, reassess | 6-10 = Significant pain, analgesia indicated.

translations.O Escore CURB-65 é uma ferramenta para a: The CURB-65 Score is a tool to assess pneumonia severity. For each criterion present, select "Yes" to add one point to the total score.

Primary care, emergency care, and inpatient settings.

translations.Calcule o escore CURB-65 para avaliar a: Calculate the CURB-65 score to assess the severity of community-acquired pneumonia and guide admission decisions. Includes interpretation, nursing care plan, and evidence.
translations.Avaliar a gravidade da pneumonia adquiri: To assess the severity of community-acquired pneumonia and guide the decision on the need for hospital admission or outpatient treatment.
translations.Indicada para pacientes adultos com diag: Indicated for adult patients with a diagnosis of community-acquired pneumonia in the emergency department or primary care.
translations.Pacientes adultos com pneumonia adquirid: Adult patients with confirmed or strongly suspected community-acquired pneumonia.
translations.Novo episódio de confusão mental / desor: New episode of confusion/disorientation in time, space, or person.
translations.Menor ou Igual a 50 mg/dL (ou 7 mmol/L): Less than or equal to 50 mg/dL (or 7 mmol/L)
translations.Ureia sérica elevada, acima de 50 mg/dL: Elevated serum urea, above 50 mg/dL (7 mmol/L).
translations.Frequência respiratória igual ou superio: Respiratory rate equal to or greater than 30 breaths per minute.
translations.Pressão arterial sistólica menor que 90: Systolic blood pressure less than 90 mmHg or diastolic less than or equal to 60 mmHg.
translations.Pressão arterial sistólica ≥ 90 mmHg e d: Systolic BP ≥ 90 mmHg and diastolic BP > 60 mmHg.
translations.PA sistólica < 90 mmHg ou PA diastólica: Systolic BP < 90 mmHg or diastolic BP ≤ 60 mmHg.
translations.Idade do paciente igual ou superior a 65: Patient age equal to or greater than 65 years.
translations.Baixo risco de mortalidade (menor que 3%: Low mortality risk (less than 3%). Generally suitable for outpatient treatment.
translations.• Considerar tratamento ambulatorial\n•:
• Consider outpatient treatment
• Educate on warning signs for return
• Clinical reassessment in 48-72h
translations.Risco intermediário de mortalidade (cerc: Intermediate mortality risk (about 9%). Consider short-stay hospital admission or supervised outpatient treatment.
translations.• Considerar internação hospitalar breve:
• Consider brief hospitalization or observation
• Close clinical reassessment
• Assess comorbidities and social support
translations.Alto risco de mortalidade (15% a mais de: High mortality risk (15% to over 40%). Indicates need for hospital admission, with higher scores (4-5) suggesting evaluation for intensive care.
translations.• Internação hospitalar\n• Considerar av:
• Hospital admission
• Consider ICU evaluation if score 4-5
• Initiate early antibiotic therapy per protocol
• Strict vital sign monitoring
translations.Excesso ou déficit na oxigenação e/ou el: Excess or deficit in oxygenation and/or carbon dioxide elimination at the alveolar-capillary membrane.
translations.Avaliar frequência respiratória e padrão: Assess respiratory rate and breathing pattern
translations.Comunicar escore elevado à equipe médica: Report elevated score to medical team
translations.O CURB-65 foi desenvolvido por Lim e col: CURB-65 was developed by Lim et al., validated and recommended by the British Thoracic Society, assessing 5 criteria: Confusion, Urea, Respiratory rate, Blood pressure, and age ≥65 years.
translations.Amplamente utilizado em serviços de emer: Widely used in emergency departments to stratify severity of community-acquired pneumonia and guide the decision between outpatient treatment and hospital admission.
translations.Validado em múltiplos estudos internacio: Validated in multiple international studies, with good correlation between score and 30-day mortality. A simplified version without urea measurement (CRB-65) can be used in primary care when lab tests are unavailable.
translations.Não deve ser o único critério para decis: Should not be the sole criterion for hospitalization decision — factors such as hypoxemia, decompensated comorbidities, social conditions, and ability to adhere to outpatient treatment should also be considered.
translations.O CRB-65 (sem ureia) pode ser usado na a: CRB-65 (without urea) can be used in primary care when laboratory tests are not readily available.
translations.Considere sempre fatores sociais e comor: Always consider social factors and associated comorbidities in the final decision, in addition to the numerical score.
translations.Escores 4-5 sugerem avaliação para terap: Scores 4-5 suggest evaluation for intensive care, not just ward admission.
translations.Reavalie a resposta ao tratamento nas pr: Reassess treatment response within the first 48-72 hours.
translations.Quantos critérios compõem o CURB-65?: How many criteria make up CURB-65?
translations.O CURB-65 avalia 5 critérios: Confusão m: CURB-65 evaluates 5 criteria: Confusion, Urea, Respiratory rate, Blood pressure, and age ≥ 65 years.
translations.Um escore CURB-65 de 4 a 5 pontos sugere: A CURB-65 score of 4 to 5 points suggests which management?
translations.Escores de 4-5 pontos indicam risco muit: Scores of 4-5 indicate very high mortality risk, suggesting evaluation for intensive care unit admission.
translations.Qual versão do escore pode ser usada qua: Which version of the score can be used when urea measurement is unavailable?
translations.O CRB-65 é uma versão simplificada que e: CRB-65 is a simplified version that excludes serum urea, useful when laboratory tests are not readily available, such as in primary care.
translations.Não. É uma ferramenta de apoio à decisão: No. It is a decision support tool that should be combined with individualized clinical assessment, including hypoxemia, comorbidities, and the patient's social conditions.
translations.Qual a diferença entre CURB-65 e CRB-65?: What is the difference between CURB-65 and CRB-65?
translations.O CRB-65 é uma versão simplificada sem a: CRB-65 is a simplified version without serum urea measurement, useful in settings without rapid access to laboratory tests, such as primary care.
translations.<p>O CURB-65 soma 1 ponto para cada um d:
CURB-65 adds 1 point for each of the 5 criteria present: Confusion, elevated Urea, Respiratory rate ≥30 breaths/min, low Blood pressure, and age ≥65 years, totaling 0 to 5 points.

Quick guide: 0-1 = Low risk (outpatient) | 2 = Moderate risk (consider admission) | 3-5 = High risk (admission, consider ICU if 4-5).

translations.0-1 = Baixo risco (ambulatorial) | 2 = R: 0-1 = Low risk (outpatient) | 2 = Moderate risk (consider admission) | 3-5 = High risk (admit, consider ICU if 4-5).
translations.Consulte definições, fatores relacionado: View definitions, related factors, NIC and NOC.
translations.Calcule o quadro de pessoal de enfermage: Calculate the nursing staff (QP) according to COFEN Resolution No. 543/2017, based on the Patient Classification System (SCP) and Marinho's Constant (KM).
translations.Calcular o quantitativo de profissionais: Calculate the number of nursing professionals (QP) required for a unit, based on the total nursing hours (THE) demanded by the patient profile (SCP) and Marinho's Constant (KM), according to the methodology of COFEN Resolution No. 543/2017.
translations.Indicado para gestores de enfermagem no: Indicated for nursing managers in planning and dimensioning teams in inpatient units, considering patient classification by care complexity.
translations.Unidades de internação hospitalar (clíni: Hospital inpatient units (medical, surgical, pediatric, obstetric, among others).
translations.Pacientes estáveis, sob cuidados de roti: Stable patients under routine care — 4 nursing hours/patient/day.
translations.Pacientes estáveis com dependência parci: Stable patients with partial dependence on the team — 6 nursing hours/patient/day.
translations.Pacientes crônicos estáveis, totalmente: Stable chronic patients, totally dependent on team actions — 10 nursing hours/patient/day.
translations.Pacientes graves, porém estáveis, com ri: Severe but stable patients at risk of instability — 10 nursing hours/patient/day.
translations.Pacientes graves e instáveis, com risco: Severe and unstable patients at imminent risk of death — 18 nursing hours/patient/day.
translations.Número de dias por semana em que a unida: Number of days per week the unit operates (7 for inpatient units with continuous operation).
translations.Percentual para cobrir ausências previst: Percentage to cover planned absences (vacations, absences, leaves). COFEN Resolution 543/2017 requires at least 15%.
translations.Carga horária semanal de trabalho do pro: Weekly workload of the nursing professional (commonly 30, 36, or 40 hours, according to legislation/institution).
translations.Quadro de pessoal compatível com unidade: Staff compatible with small units or low care complexity.
translations.• Distribuir 33% da equipe como Enfermei:
• Distribute 33% of the team as Nurses and the rest as Nursing Technicians/Auxiliaries (minimal/intermediate care), respecting the minimum of 6 nurses per unit
• Reassess the dimensioning at each relevant change in patient profile
translations.• Distribuir a equipe conforme o percent:
• Distribute the team according to the required percentage of nurses for the predominant complexity profile
• Consider seasonality of absences when planning schedules
• Reassess periodically with new SCP patient classification
translations.Quadro de pessoal compatível com unidade: Staff compatible with large units or higher care complexity.
translations.• Considerar necessidade de coordenação/:
• Consider the need for additional coordination/supervision for large teams
• Reassess the dimensioning at each relevant change in patient profile (SCP)
• Verify adequacy of nurse/technician ratio according to current legislation
translations.Quadro de pessoal compatível com unidade: Staff compatible with high-complexity units and large patient volume (e.g., large ICU).
translations.• Avaliar a divisão da unidade em subuni:
• Evaluate the division of the unit into subunits/stations to facilitate management
• Ensure nursing leadership and supervision on all shifts
• Periodically reassess staffing according to changes in the care profile
translations.Risco de infecção (relacionado à sobreca: Risk for infection (related to team overload)
translations.Risco aumentado de eventos adversos e in: Increased risk of adverse events and infections related to inadequate staffing in the face of care demand.
translations.Quadro de pessoal calculado conforme met: Staff calculated according to COFEN 543/2017 methodology
translations.Proporção enfermeiro/técnico respeitada: Nurse/technician ratio respected according to care complexity
translations.Reavaliação periódica do dimensionamento: Periodic reassessment of staffing according to changes in patient profile (PCS)
translations.Classificar os pacientes da unidade conf: Classify patients in the unit according to the Patient Classification System (PCS)
translations.Calcular o Total de Horas de Enfermagem: Calculate the Total Nursing Hours (TNH) demanded by the patient profile
translations.Aplicar a Constante de Marinho (KM) cons: Apply the Marinho Constant (KM) considering days of operation, IST, and team workload
translations.Distribuir o quadro de pessoal (QP) entr: Distribute the staff (QP) among nurses and technicians/aides according to predominant complexity
translations.Reavaliar o dimensionamento periodicamen: Periodically reassess staffing and at each relevant change in the care profile
translations.O dimensionamento segue três etapas: (1): Staffing follows three steps: (1) calculation of Total Nursing Hours (TNH), summing the product of the number of patients in each PCS category by the respective care time (Minimum=4h, Intermediate=6h, High Dependency=10h, Semi-Intensive=10h, Intensive=18h); (2) calculation of the Marinho Constant (KM = DS × (1+IST)/CHS); (3) calculation of Staff (QP = TNH × KM).
translations.A metodologia foi originalmente proposta: The methodology was originally proposed by Maria José Trevizan Marinho and incorporated into the regulations of the Federal Nursing Council, being consolidated in COFEN Resolution No. 543/2017 (later revised by COFEN Resolution No. 743/2024).
translations.Metodologia oficial adotada nacionalment: Official methodology adopted nationally for nursing staff sizing in health services, referenced by audits of the Regional Nursing Councils (COREN).
translations.O resultado representa o quantitativo to: The result represents the total theoretical number of professionals (nurses + technicians/aides); the percentage distribution between these categories depends on the predominant complexity and must follow current regulatory parameters. The weekly workload (CHS) and IST may vary according to labor legislation, collective bargaining, and institutional policy.
translations.A fórmula tem 3 etapas: THE (horas totai: The formula has 3 steps: TNH (total care hours demanded), KM (Marinho constant, which adjusts for 7-day coverage, absences, and workload) and QP = TNH × KM.
translations.O IST (Índice de Segurança Técnica) nunc: The IST (Technical Safety Index) can never be less than 15%, according to COFEN Resolution No. 543/2017.
translations.Para cuidados mínimos e intermediários,: For minimum and intermediate care, it is recommended that at least 33% of the team (minimum of 6) be nurses, with the remainder being nursing technicians/aides.
translations.Em unidades de maior complexidade (alta: In units of higher complexity (high dependency, semi-intensive, intensive), the proportion of nurses should be higher, according to current regulatory parameters.
translations.Clínica médica: 12 cuidados mínimos, 15: Medical clinic: 12 minimum care, 15 intermediate, 5 high dependency
translations.Quais são as 5 categorias do Sistema de: What are the 5 categories of the Patient Classification System (PCS) used in TNH?
translations.Mínimos, intermediários, alta dependênci: Minimum, intermediate, high dependency, semi-intensive, intensive
translations.O SCP classifica os pacientes em 5 categ: The SCP classifies patients into 5 categories: minimal care (4h), intermediate care (6h), high dependency (10h), semi-intensive (10h), and intensive (18h) nursing hours per patient/day.
translations.Qual o valor mínimo exigido para o Índic: What is the minimum value required for the Technical Safety Index (TSI) by COFEN Resolution 543/2017?
translations.A Resolução COFEN nº 543/2017 exige um Í: COFEN Resolution No. 543/2017 requires a Technical Safety Index (TSI) of no less than 15%, to cover planned absences such as vacations, absences, and leaves.
translations.Como é calculado o Quadro de Pessoal (QP: How is the Staffing Table (ST) calculated?
translations.O Quadro de Pessoal é calculado multipli: The Staffing Table is calculated by multiplying the Total Nursing Hours (THE) by the Marinho Constant (KM).
translations.O resultado do QP já vem dividido entre: Does the ST result already come divided between nurses and technicians?
translations.Não. O QP representa o quantitativo tota: No. The ST represents the total number of nursing professionals. The percentage distribution between nurses and technicians/assistants must follow regulatory parameters according to the predominant care complexity of the unit.
translations.Com que frequência o dimensionamento dev: How often should staffing be recalculated?
translations.Recomenda-se reavaliação periódica (gera: Periodic reassessment is recommended (usually semiannually or annually) and whenever there is a relevant change in the complexity profile of patients served by the unit.
translations.<p>Calcula o Total de Horas de Enfermage:
Calculates the Total Nursing Hours (THE) from the number of patients in each SCP category, applies the Marinho Constant (KM), and obtains the Staffing Table (ST = THE × KM).

Formula: THE = (PCM×4 + PCI×6 + PCAD×10 + PCSI×10 + PCIt×18) | KM = DS×(1+IST)/CHS | ST = THE × KM.
translations.Calculadora de Dimensionamento de Equipe: Nursing Staffing Calculator: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Calcular o quantitativo de profissionais: Calculate the number of nursing professionals (ST) required for a unit, based on the total nursing hours (THE) demanded by the patient profile (SCP) and the Marinho Constant (KM), according to the methodology of COFEN Resolution No. 543/2017. Recommended for nursing managers in planning and staffing of inpatient units, considering patient classification by care complexity.

translations.Indicada para avaliação seriada do desco: Indicated for serial assessment of respiratory distress in term and preterm newborns, aiding in decision-making regarding the need for ventilatory support.
translations.Recém-nascidos com sinais de desconforto: Newborns with signs of respiratory distress.
Cyanosis of lips and oral mucosa.
translations.Presente em ar ambiente, melhora com FiO: Present in room air, improves with FiO2 40%
translations.Presente e persiste mesmo com FiO2 40%: Present and persists even with FiO2 40%
translations.Presença e audibilidade do gemido expira: Presence and audibility of expiratory grunt.
translations.Qualidade da entrada de ar à ausculta pu: Quality of air entry on lung auscultation.
translations.• Manter monitorização de rotina\n• Reav:
• Maintain routine monitoring
• Reassess per institutional protocol
translations.Pontuação de 1 a 4 pontos, compatível co: Score 1 to 4 points, consistent with mild respiratory distress.
translations.• Monitorização contínua dos sinais vita:
• Continuous monitoring of vital signs and oxygen saturation
• Reassess periodically
• Consider supplemental oxygen therapy per medical evaluation
translations.Pontuação de 5 a 7 pontos, compatível co: Score 5 to 7 points, consistent with moderate respiratory distress.
translations.• Comunicar à equipe médica\n• Considera:
• Notify medical team
• Consider non-invasive ventilatory support (e.g., CPAP) per evaluation
• Continuous monitoring and frequent reassessment
translations.Pontuação acima de 7 pontos, compatível: Score above 7 points, consistent with severe respiratory distress, with risk of impending respiratory failure.
translations.• Comunicar imediatamente à equipe médic:
• Immediately notify medical team
• Prepare for possible need for invasive ventilatory support
• Intensive and continuous monitoring
translations.Inspiração e/ou expiração que não propor: Inspiration and/or expiration that does not provide adequate ventilation, identified by the parameters assessed in the Downes Score.
translations.Excesso ou déficit na oxigenação e/ou el: Excess or deficit in oxygenation and/or carbon dioxide elimination at the alveolar-capillary membrane, related to cyanosis and auscultation changes identified on the scale.
translations.Risco de entrada de secreções gastrintes: Risk of entry of gastrointestinal, oropharyngeal, solid, or liquid secretions into the tracheobronchial airways, related to the newborn's respiratory distress.
translations.Necessidade de suporte ventilatório iden: Need for ventilatory support identified early
translations.Melhora progressiva do escore após inter: Progressive improvement in score after intervention
translations.Aplicar o Escore de Downes de forma seri: Apply the Downes Score serially per institutional protocol
translations.Comunicar imediatamente à equipe médica: Immediately notify medical team of elevated or progressively worsening scores
translations.Preparar material para suporte ventilató: Prepare equipment for ventilatory support (CPAP, invasive ventilation) as needed
translations.Monitorar saturação de oxigênio, frequên: Monitor oxygen saturation, heart rate, and blood gases per prescription
translations.Registrar o escore e a resposta às inter: Record the score and response to interventions in the medical record
translations.O Escore de Downes avalia 5 parâmetros —: The Downes Score evaluates 5 parameters — cyanosis, respiratory rate, retractions, expiratory grunt, and air entry on auscultation — each scored 0 to 2, totaling a score of 0 to 10 points, for assessment of neonatal respiratory distress.
translations.Descrito por Downes e colaboradores, é u: Described by Downes and colleagues, it is one of the classic scores for assessing respiratory distress in newborns, used as a complement or alternative to the Silverman-Andersen Score.
translations.Correlaciona-se bem com parâmetros gasom: It correlates well with blood gas parameters (pH, PaO2, alveolar-arterial oxygen gradient) and is used in both term and preterm newborns for serial monitoring of respiratory distress.
translations.Como toda escala observacional, está suj: Like any observational scale, it is subject to inter-examiner variability. It should be used in conjunction with complete clinical assessment and, when available, arterial blood gas analysis.
translations.O Escore de Downes avalia 5 parâmetros:: The Downes Score evaluates 5 parameters: cyanosis, respiratory rate, retractions, expiratory grunt, and air entry, each scored from 0 to 2 points.
translations.É frequentemente utilizado de forma comp: It is often used as a complement or alternative to the Silverman-Andersen Score in the assessment of neonatal respiratory distress.
translations.Escores acima de 7 sugerem desconforto r: Scores above 7 suggest severe respiratory distress and risk of impending respiratory failure, requiring immediate communication to the medical team.
translations.Reavalie o escore de forma seriada para: Reassess the score serially to monitor response to interventions, such as non-invasive ventilatory support.
translations.Quantos parâmetros compõem o Escore de D: How many parameters make up the Downes Score and what is the maximum score?
translations.O Escore de Downes avalia 5 parâmetros (: The Downes Score evaluates 5 parameters (cyanosis, respiratory rate, retractions, expiratory grunt, and air entry), each from 0 to 2 points, totaling a maximum of 10 points.
translations.Uma pontuação acima de 7 no Escore de Do: What does a score above 7 on the Downes Score indicate?
translations.Desconforto respiratório grave, com risc: Severe respiratory distress, with risk of respiratory failure
translations.Pontuações acima de 7 no Escore de Downe: Scores above 7 on the Downes Score indicate severe respiratory distress, with risk of impending respiratory failure.
translations.O Escore de Downes é semelhante a qual o: The Downes Score is similar to which other neonatal respiratory assessment scale?
translations.O Escore de Downes é frequentemente util: The Downes Score is often used as a complement or alternative to the Silverman-Andersen Score for assessing neonatal respiratory distress.
translations.O Escore de Downes substitui a gasometri: Does the Downes Score replace arterial blood gas analysis?
translations.Não. Embora se correlacione bem com parâ: No. Although it correlates well with blood gas parameters, the Downes Score is an observational clinical assessment tool and should be used in conjunction with arterial blood gas analysis and other clinical data, when available and indicated.
translations.Qual a diferença entre o Escore de Downe: What is the difference between the Downes Score and the Silverman-Andersen Score?
translations.Ambos avaliam o desconforto respiratório: Both assess neonatal respiratory distress but use partially different parameters. The Downes includes cyanosis and respiratory rate as scored items, while the Silverman-Andersen focuses more on signs of respiratory effort (nasal flaring, xiphoid retraction, among others).
translations.<p>O Escore de Downes soma a pontuação (:
The Downes Score sums the score (0 to 2) of 5 parameters — cyanosis, respiratory rate, retractions, expiratory grunt, and air entry — totaling 0 to 10 points.

Quick guide: 0 = No distress | 1-4 = Mild | 5-7 = Moderate | 8-10 = Severe.

translations.Calcule o Índice de Downton para avaliaç: Calculate the Downton Index for fall risk assessment in elderly patients. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence for nursing practice.
translations.Avaliar o risco de queda do paciente, es: Assess the patient's fall risk, especially elderly, based on 5 domains: previous falls, use of risk medications, sensory deficit, mental state, and gait pattern.
translations.Indicada na admissão hospitalar, especia: Indicated at hospital admission, especially for elderly patients, and periodically according to institutional fall prevention protocol.
translations.Pacientes idosos hospitalizados ou em ac: Elderly hospitalized patients or those in outpatient/home care.
History of falls in the last 12 months.
translations.Presença de queda anterior nos últimos 1: Presence of previous fall in the last 12 months.
translations.Uso de diuréticos, anti-hipertensivos, a: Use of diuretics, antihypertensives, antiparkinsonians, antidepressants, or sedatives/hypnotics.
translations.Não faz uso de medicamentos associados a: Does not use medications associated with increased fall risk.
translations.Uso de um ou mais medicamentos associado: Use of one or more medications associated with increased fall risk.
translations.Presença de deficiência visual, auditiva: Presence of visual, auditory, or limb deficit that compromises environmental perception.
translations.Presença de déficit visual, auditivo ou: Presence of visual, auditory, or limb deficit.
translations.Nível de orientação e consciência do pac: Patient's level of orientation and consciousness.
translations.Paciente orientado em tempo, espaço e pe: Patient oriented to time, place, and person.
translations.Padrão de marcha e necessidade de auxíli: Gait pattern and need for assistance to walk.
translations.Segura, com ou sem Auxílio de Locomoção: Safe, with or without Mobility Aid
translations.Marcha segura, mesmo se utilizar disposi: Safe gait, even if using assistive device, or bedridden/chair-bound patient without attempting to walk.
translations.Marcha segura apenas quando faz uso de d: Safe gait only when using support device (cane, walker).
translations.Marcha insegura, instável, independentem: Unsafe, unstable gait, regardless of use of support device.
translations.Baixo risco de queda segundo os critério: Low fall risk according to assessed criteria.
translations.• Orientação geral sobre segurança e pre:
• General guidance on safety and fall prevention
• Reassessment according to institutional protocol
• Obstacle-free environment
translations.Escore igual ou superior a 3 indica alto: Score equal to or greater than 3 indicates high fall risk, according to the validated cutoff point of the Downton Index.
translations.• Implementar protocolo intensivo de pre:
• Implement intensive fall prevention protocol
• Visual risk signage at bedside/room
• Raised bed rails and bed in low position
• Frequent supervision and assistance with ambulation
• Reassessment every shift
translations.Vulnerabilidade aumentada a quedas, que: Increased vulnerability to falls, which can cause physical harm and compromise health.
translations.Identificar déficits cognitivos, sensori: Identify cognitive, sensory, and motor deficits
translations.Revisar medicamentos em uso associados a: Review medications associated with fall risk with medical team
translations.Sinalizar risco de queda no leito e pron: Flag fall risk at bedside and in medical record
translations.Orientar paciente e família sobre medida: Educate patient and family about safety measures
translations.Manter grades elevadas e leito em posiçã: Keep bed rails raised and bed in low position when indicated
translations.O Índice de Downton foi desenvolvido par: The Downton Index was developed to assess fall risk in the elderly, considering 5 domains: history of previous falls, use of risk medications, sensory deficit, mental state, and gait pattern, with a total score ranging from 0 to 7 (or 11, depending on the version used).
translations.Amplamente utilizado em serviços de geri: Widely used in geriatric services and patient safety protocols for fall risk screening, especially in hospitalized elderly populations.
translations.Estudos de validação relatam boa sensibi: Validation studies report good sensitivity for identifying patients at risk of falling, with a cutoff of 3 points or more indicating high risk.
translations.Como a maioria das escalas de risco de q: Like most fall risk scales, it has limited specificity and may classify a significant number of patients as high risk. It should be complemented by individualized clinical assessment and evaluation of the physical environment.
translations.Um escore igual ou superior a 3 já é suf: A score of 3 or more is sufficient to classify the patient as high risk.
translations.Revise a lista de medicamentos em uso, p: Review the list of medications in use, as several common drugs in older adults increase fall risk.
translations.Combine a avaliação com o exame físico d: Combine the assessment with physical examination of gait and balance.
translations.Reavalie após qualquer mudança no estado: Reassess after any change in the patient's clinical status or medication regimen.
translations.Quantos domínios compõem o Índice de Dow: How many domains make up the Downton Index?
translations.O Downton avalia 5 domínios: quedas ante: The Downton assesses 5 domains: previous falls, medications, sensory deficit, mental state, and ambulation.
translations.A partir de qual pontuação o Índice de D: From what score does the Downton Index indicate high fall risk?
translations.Um escore de 3 pontos ou mais é o ponto: A score of 3 points or more is the validated cutoff to classify the patient as high fall risk.
translations.Quais classes de medicamentos são consid: Which classes of medications are considered risk factors in the Downton Index?
translations.Diuréticos, anti-hipertensivos, antipark: Diuretics, antihypertensives, antiparkinsonians, antidepressants, and sedatives
Diuretics, antihypertensives, antiparkinsonians, antidepressants, and sedatives/hypnotics are associated with increased fall risk in older adults.
translations.O Índice de Downton substitui a avaliaçã: Does the Downton Index replace individual clinical assessment?
translations.Não. É uma ferramenta de triagem que dev: No. It is a screening tool that should be combined with clinical judgment, assessment of the physical environment, and detailed patient history.
translations.Com que frequência devo reavaliar o Índi: How often should I reassess the Downton Index?
translations.Recomenda-se reavaliação na admissão, ap: Reassessment is recommended on admission, after any fall episode, change in medication regimen, and periodically according to institutional protocol.
translations.<p>O Índice de Downton soma pontos de 5:
The Downton Index sums points from 5 domains — previous falls, use of risk medications, sensory deficit, mental state, and ambulation pattern — totaling 0 to 7 points.

Quick guide: 0-2 = Low risk | ≥3 = High risk, implement intensive fall prevention protocol.

translations.0 — Segura, com ou sem Auxílio de Locomo: 0 — Safe, with or without walking aid
translations.Avaliar o risco de queda do paciente, es: Assess the patient's fall risk, especially older adults, based on 5 domains: previous falls, use of risk medications, sensory deficit, mental state, and ambulation pattern. Indicated on hospital admission, especially for elderly patients, and periodically according to institutional fall prevention protocol.

translations.O Índice de Downton soma pontos de 5 dom: The Downton Index sums points from 5 domains — previous falls, use of risk medications, sensory deficit, mental state, and gait pattern — totaling 0 to 7 points.
translations.0-2 = Baixo risco | ≥3 = Alto risco, imp: 0-2 = Low risk | ≥3 = High risk, implement intensive fall prevention protocol.
translations.ECOG Performance Status - Capacidade Fun: ECOG Performance Status - Oncologic Functional Capacity
translations.Classifique a capacidade funcional do pa: Classify the functional capacity of the oncologic patient using the ECOG Performance Status scale (0 to 5). Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Classificar objetivamente a capacidade f: Objectively classify the functional capacity of the oncologic patient, guiding therapeutic and prognostic decisions according to the degree of autonomy for activities of daily living.
translations.Indicada para pacientes oncológicos, em: Indicated for oncologic patients at any stage of treatment, and in palliative care, as a parameter for therapeutic decision-making and follow-up.
translations.Pacientes adultos com diagnóstico oncoló: Adult patients with oncologic diagnosis or in palliative care.
translations.Selecione a categoria que melhor descrev: Select the category that best describes the patient's current functional capacity.
translations.Capaz de realizar todas as atividades no: Able to carry out all normal activities without restriction, as before the disease.
translations.Restrito a atividades fisicamente extenu: Restricted in physically strenuous activities, but able to do light/sedentary work (e.g., light housework, office work).
translations.Deambula e é capaz de todo o autocuidado: Ambulatory and capable of all self-care, but unable to carry out any work activities; up and about more than 50% of waking hours.
translations.Capacidade de autocuidado apenas limitad: Capable of only limited self-care; confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours.
translations.Completamente incapacitado; não é capaz: Completely disabled; cannot carry out any self-care; totally confined to bed or chair.
translations.Paciente com boa capacidade funcional, g: Patient with good functional capacity, generally a candidate for curative-intent oncologic treatments at full dose.
translations.• Avaliar elegibilidade para tratamento:
• Assess eligibility for active oncologic treatment per protocol
• Reassess periodically during treatment
translations.Paciente deambula e mantém autocuidado,: Patient ambulates and maintains self-care, but with limitation for work activities. Requires individualized assessment regarding tolerance to more aggressive treatments.
translations.• Avaliação multiprofissional para defin:
• Multidisciplinary assessment for therapeutic definition
• Consider dose/intensity adjustment of treatment according to tolerance
translations.Capacidade de autocuidado limitada, com: Limited self-care capacity, with more than half of time in bed/chair. Aggressive oncologic treatments are generally not well tolerated.
translations.• Priorizar discussão sobre objetivos de:
• Prioritize discussion of care goals
• Consider associated palliative approach
• Individualized risk-benefit assessment of active treatments
translations.Paciente completamente dependente para a: Patient completely dependent for self-care, confined to bed/chair. Active oncologic treatments are generally not indicated.
translations.• Priorizar cuidados paliativos e confor:
• Prioritize palliative care and comfort
• Discussion of care goals with patient/family
• Intensive multidisciplinary support
translations.Sensação avassaladora e sustentada de ex: Overwhelming and sustained sense of exhaustion and decreased capacity for physical and mental work.
translations.Padrão de bem-estar em dimensões física,: A pattern of well-being in physical, psychospiritual, environmental, cultural, and/or social dimensions that can be strengthened.
translations.Nível de energia adequado à condição clí: Energy level adequate to clinical condition
translations.Participação em decisões sobre o plano t: Participation in decisions about the therapeutic plan
translations.Avaliar a capacidade funcional com escal: Assess functional capacity with validated scale (ECOG)
translations.Planejar cuidados conforme o grau de dep: Plan care according to identified degree of dependence
translations.Envolver equipe multiprofissional na def: Involve multidisciplinary team in therapeutic definition
translations.A escala ECOG Performance Status foi des: The ECOG Performance Status scale was developed by the Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group, classifying the functional capacity of cancer patients on a scale from 0 (fully active) to 5 (dead), being one of the most widely used instruments worldwide in oncology for therapeutic and prognostic decisions.
translations.Amplamente adotada em ensaios clínicos o: Widely adopted in oncology clinical trials as an eligibility criterion and risk stratification tool, and in daily clinical practice to guide decisions on treatment intensity.
translations.Apresenta boa correlação com prognóstico: It shows good correlation with prognosis and tolerance to oncological treatments, being validated and extensively used in international oncology literature.
translations.É uma escala relativamente simples e pod: It is a relatively simple scale and may not capture specific nuances of certain conditions; the Karnofsky Scale, more granular (0-100), is often used as a complement or alternative in certain contexts.
translations.Avalie a capacidade funcional real do pa: Assess the patient's actual functional capacity, not their perceived or desired capacity.
translations.Reavalie o ECOG a cada consulta ou ciclo: Reassess ECOG at each visit or oncological treatment cycle to monitor progression.
translations.Use em conjunto com a Escala de Karnofsk: Use in conjunction with the Karnofsky Scale when greater granularity is needed for therapeutic decisions.
translations.O ECOG é frequentemente utilizado como c: ECOG is often used as an eligibility criterion for clinical trials and treatment protocols.
translations.Qual a faixa de valores do ECOG Performa: What is the range of values for ECOG Performance Status?
translations.Um paciente ECOG 4 é geralmente candidat: Is an ECOG 4 patient generally a candidate for aggressive oncological treatment?
translations.Geralmente não, prioriza-se abordagem pa: Generally not, palliative approach is prioritized
translations.Pacientes ECOG 4 (completamente incapaci: ECOG 4 patients (completely disabled) generally do not tolerate aggressive oncological treatments, with palliative care and comfort approach being prioritized.
translations.Para que é frequentemente utilizado o EC: What is ECOG often used for in clinical research?
translations.Critério de elegibilidade em ensaios clí: Eligibility criterion in oncology clinical trials
translations.O ECOG é amplamente utilizado como crité: ECOG is widely used as an eligibility criterion and risk stratification tool in oncology clinical trials.
translations.Qual a diferença entre ECOG e Escala de: What is the difference between ECOG and the Karnofsky Scale?
translations.Ambas avaliam capacidade funcional em on: Both assess functional capacity in oncology, but ECOG uses a simpler scale from 0 to 5, while Karnofsky uses a more granular scale from 0 to 100 (increments of 10), allowing greater precision in certain contexts.
translations.O ECOG substitui a avaliação clínica com: Does ECOG replace the complete clinical assessment of the cancer patient?
translations.Não. É uma ferramenta padronizada de com: No. It is a standardized communication tool about functional capacity, but should be complemented by individualized clinical assessment, including comorbidities and patient care goals.
translations.<p>O ECOG classifica a capacidade funcio:
ECOG classifies the functional capacity of cancer patients into 6 levels, from 0 (fully active, no restrictions) to 5 (dead), guiding decisions on eligibility and intensity of oncological treatment.

Quick guide: 0-1 = Good functional capacity | 2 = Intermediate | 3 = Reduced | 4 = Severe disability | 5 = Dead.

translations.Respeito e Compromisso com o paciente: Respect and Commitment to the patient
translations.“Aqui, você encontra escuta, compreensão: “Here, you find listening, understanding, and paths to reorganize your emotional life.”
translations.Não precisa saber o nome do que sente. E: You don't need to know the name of what you feel. We are here to welcome you.
translations.Recursos de regulação para momentos de i: Regulation resources for moments of emotional intensity
translations.Ferramentas de sono e descanso mental re: Sleep and restorative mental rest tools
translations.Não sei o que sinto: I don't know what I feel
translations.Cada pessoa é única. Nossos serviços se: Each person is unique. Our services adapt to your need.
translations.Encontros semanais de 50 minutos em ambi: Weekly 50-minute sessions in a welcoming and confidential environment.
translations.Sessões por vídeo com a mesma qualidade: Video sessions with the same quality as in-person, in the comfort of your space.
translations.Investigação científica das funções cogn: Scientific investigation of cognitive and emotional functions.
translations.Não há pressa. Não há julgamento. Apenas: There is no rush. There is no judgment. Just a safe space for you to reorganize emotionally.
No commitment. Cancel whenever you want.
translations.Espaço terapêutico dedicado à saúde ment: Therapeutic space dedicated to integrative mental health, combining science, care, and technology.
translations.© 2026 Elisabeth Marques. Seu espaço de: © 2026 Elisabeth Marques. Your space for emotional reorganization.
translations.\"Atendimento interativo voltado ao equi: "Interactive care focused on emotional balance, self-knowledge, and human development."
translations.Acolher, compreender e auxiliar pessoas: To welcome, understand, and assist people in the process of emotional reconstruction, promoting awareness, balance, and development through neuropsychoanalysis and humanized listening.
translations.Ética, empatia, respeito à individualida: Ethics, empathy, respect for individuality, non-judgmental listening, emotional support, professional responsibility, and commitment to human transformation.
translations.Ajudar pessoas a compreenderem suas dore: To help people understand their emotional pains, reframe their stories, and develop a more conscious, healthy, and emotionally safe life.
translations.Atendimento terapêutico integrativo volt: Integrative therapeutic care focused on emotional development, understanding behavioral patterns, and strengthening mental health, uniting neuroscience, psychoanalysis, and behavioral analysis in a humanized way.
