translations.GOSNELL, D. J. Gosnell pressure sore ris: GOSNELL, D. J. Gosnell pressure sore risk assessment instrument revision. Decubitus, v. 2, n. 3, p. 32-38, 1989.
translations.A Escala de Gosnell soma a pontuação de: The Gosnell Scale sums the scores of 5 domains — mental status (1-5), continence, mobility, and activity (1-4 each) and nutrition (1-3) — totaling 5 to 20 points. Lower scores indicate higher risk.
translations.5-10 = Alto risco | 11-15 = Risco modera: 5-10 = High risk | 11-15 = Moderate risk | 16-20 = Low risk.
translations.Calcule o gotejamento de soluções intrav: Calculate the drip rate (drops/min) of intravenous solutions based on volume, infusion time, and drop factor. Free nursing tool.
translations.Calcule com precisão o gotejamento (gota: Accurately calculate the drip rate (drops/min) of intravenous solutions from volume, infusion time, and drop factor.
translations.Indicada sempre que uma solução intraven: Indicated whenever an intravenous solution is administered via macro or micro drip set, without an electronic infusion pump.
translations.Pacientes em uso de terapia intravenosa: Patients receiving gravity intravenous therapy, across all age groups.
Total volume of the prescribed solution.
translations.Gotejamento muito lento. Confira o volum: Very slow drip rate. Check the volume, prescribed time, and consider whether an infusion pump would be more appropriate for this rate.
translations.Valor dentro da faixa prática mais comum: Value within the most common practical range in adults. Always check the patient and follow institutional protocols.
translations.Gotejamento muito rápido. Revise o volum: Very fast drip rate. Review the volume, drop factor, and infusion time — consider using an infusion pump.
translations.O cálculo de gotejamento fundamenta-se n: The drip rate calculation is based on the relationship between the prescribed solution volume, the drop factor of the drip set used (macrodrip or microdrip), and the total infusion time. The standard formula — Drops/min = (Volume × Factor) ÷ Time in minutes — derives from the definition of the drip set itself: each mL of solution corresponds to a fixed number of drops, defined by the drip set manufacturer (20 drops/mL for macrodrip, 60 drops/mL for microdrip).
translations.Equipos de macrogotas são utilizados na: Macrodrip sets are used for most routine infusions in adults. Microdrip sets offer greater precision and are indicated in pediatrics, neonatology, and low-volume or high-precision infusions.
translations.Protocolos institucionais e sociedades d: Institutional protocols and infusion therapy societies recommend double-checking the drip rate calculation before administration, especially in vulnerable populations (pediatrics, elderly, renal or cardiac patients), due to the risk of fluid overload associated with infusion errors.
translations.Embora não exista um limite universal, v: Although there is no universal limit, values between 5 and 120 drops/minute are often cited as a practical verification range in adults — values outside this range usually indicate a need to review the prescription, the selected drip set, or to use an infusion pump.
translations.Confirme o fator de gotejamento do equip: Confirm the drop factor of the drip set before calculation.
translations.Utilize bomba de infusão quando disponív: Use an infusion pump when available.
translations.Registre o cálculo e a resposta do pacie: Record the calculation and the patient's response.
translations.Quantas gotas equivalem a 1 mL em um equ: How many drops equal 1 mL in a macrodrip set?
translations.Macrogotas administram 20 gotas por mL —: Macrodrip delivers 20 drops per mL — it is the standard set for most routine infusions.
translations.Qual é a fórmula do gotejamento em gotas: What is the formula for drip rate in drops/min?
translations.Gotas/min = (Volume × Fator do equipo) ÷: Drops/min = (Volume × Drop factor) ÷ Total infusion time in minutes.
translations.Um equipo microgotas administra quantas: How many drops per mL does a microdrip set deliver?
translations.Microgotas administram 60 gotas por mL e: Microdrip delivers 60 drops per mL and is indicated when greater control is needed, such as in pediatrics.
translations.Qual a diferença entre macrogotas e micr: What is the difference between macrodrip and microdrip?
translations.Macrogotas administram 20 gotas por mL e: Macrodrops deliver 20 drops per mL and are used in most routine infusions. Microdrops deliver 60 drops per mL and are indicated when greater control and precision are needed, such as in pediatrics or low-volume infusions.
translations.Esta calculadora substitui a checagem cl: Does this calculator replace clinical assessment?
translations.Não. A ferramenta é um apoio ao cálculo: No. The tool supports calculation and does not replace clinical assessment, medical prescription, or institutional protocols. Always verify data before administering any medication or solution.
translations.Como faço o cálculo para uma bomba de in: How do I calculate for an electronic infusion pump?
translations.Bombas de infusão são programadas direta: Infusion pumps are programmed directly in mL/h, without using the drip factor: simply divide the total volume by the total time in hours (mL/h = Volume ÷ Time in hours).
translations.<p>O gotejamento intravenoso é calculado:
Intravenous drip rate is calculated by multiplying the total volume of the solution (in mL) by the drip factor of the administration set (drops/mL) and dividing by the total infusion time in minutes. The result indicates how many drops per minute must be administered so that the entire volume is infused within the prescribed time.

Formula: Drops/min = (Volume × Factor) ÷ Time in minutes.

Indicated whenever an IV solution is administered via macro- or microdrip set without an electronic infusion pump.

The result indicates how many drops per minute must be administered so that the entire volume is infused within the prescribed time.

translations.Guia de bolso do Índice de Apgar — refer: Pocket guide to the Apgar Score — quick reference for delivery room consultation.
translations.Avaliar no 1º e 5º minuto · 0 a 10 ponto: Assess at 1st and 5th minute · 0 to 10 points
translations.NUNCA atrase a reanimação para calcular: NEVER delay resuscitation to calculate the Apgar
translations.Calcule a Escala de Hamilton para Ansied: Calculate the Hamilton Anxiety Rating Scale (HAM-A) and assess the severity of psychic and somatic anxiety symptoms. Includes interpretation, nursing care plan, and evidence.
translations.Avaliar a gravidade dos sintomas de ansi: To assess the severity of anxiety symptoms, both psychic and somatic, through 14 interviewer-rated items.
translations.Indicada para avaliação da gravidade de: Indicated for assessing the severity of anxiety symptoms in adults with anxiety disorders and for monitoring treatment response over time.
translations.Adultos com diagnóstico ou suspeita de t: Adults with a diagnosis or suspected anxiety disorder.
translations.Preocupações, previsão do pior, apreensã: Worries, anticipation of the worst, fearful apprehension, irritability.
translations.Sensação de tensão, fadiga, resposta de: Feelings of tension, fatigue, startle response, easy crying, tremors, feelings of restlessness, inability to relax.
translations.De escuro, de estranhos, de ficar sozinh: Of dark, of strangers, of being alone, of animals, of traffic, of crowds.
translations.Dificuldade para adormecer, sono interro: Difficulty falling asleep, interrupted sleep, unsatisfying sleep with fatigue on waking, dreams, nightmares, night terrors.
translations.Perda de interesse, falta de prazer em p: Loss of interest, lack of pleasure in hobbies, depression, early waking, diurnal variation of mood.
translations.Dores musculares, rigidez, contrações mu: Muscle pains, stiffness, muscle twitching, clonic spasms, teeth grinding, unsteady voice.
translations.Zumbido, visão turva, ondas de calor e f: Tinnitus, blurred vision, hot and cold flashes, feelings of weakness, tingling sensation.
translations.Taquicardia, palpitações, dor no peito,: Tachycardia, palpitations, chest pain, throbbing of vessels, fainting feelings, extrasystoles.
translations.Pressão ou constrição no peito, sensação: Pressure or constriction in chest, choking feelings, sighing, dyspnea.
translations.Dificuldade para engolir, flatulência, d: Difficulty swallowing, flatulence, abdominal pain, burning, fullness, nausea, vomiting, borborygmi, weight loss, constipation.
translations.Micção frequente/urgente, amenorreia, me: Frequent/urgent urination, amenorrhea, menorrhagia, frigidity, premature ejaculation, loss of libido, impotence.
translations.Boca seca, rubor, palidez, tendência à s: Dry mouth, flushing, pallor, tendency to sweat, dizziness, tension headache, piloerection.
translations.Inquietação, agitação motora, tremor das: Fidgeting, restlessness, tremor of hands, furrowed brow, strained face, sighing, facial pallor, tachycardia and tachypnea at rest.
translations.Pontuação de 0 a 17 pontos, compatível c: Score from 0 to 17 points, consistent with mild anxiety.
translations.• Manter acompanhamento clínico de rotin:
• Maintain routine clinical follow-up
• Consider stress management strategies and relaxation techniques
• Reassess periodically
translations.Pontuação entre 18 e 24 pontos, compatív: Score between 18 and 24 points, consistent with mild to moderate anxiety.
translations.• Encaminhar para avaliação psiquiátrica:
• Refer for specialized psychiatric/psychological evaluation
• Consider psychotherapeutic interventions
• Reassess response to interventions over time
translations.Pontuação entre 25 e 30 pontos, compatív: Score between 25 and 30 points, consistent with moderate to severe anxiety.
translations.• Encaminhamento psiquiátrico prioritári:
• Priority psychiatric referral
• Consider pharmacological treatment combined with psychotherapy
• Monitor functional impact and risk of comorbidities
translations.Pontuação acima de 30 pontos, compatível: Score above 30 points, consistent with severe anxiety with significant functional impact.
translations.• Encaminhamento psiquiátrico urgente\n•:
• Urgent psychiatric referral
• Assess need for intensive treatment
• Investigate risk of comorbidities (depression, suicidal ideation) and significant functional impairment
translations.Sensação vaga e incômoda de desconforto: Vague and uncomfortable feeling of discomfort or fear, accompanied by autonomic response, identified by psychic and somatic symptoms assessed on the scale.
translations.Interrupções por tempo limitado da quant: Time-limited interruptions in the quantity and quality of sleep, related to the insomnia item of the scale.
translations.Incapacidade de formar uma apreciação vá: Inability to form a valid appraisal of stressors, inadequate choices of practical responses, and/or inability to use available resources.
translations.Escore HAM-A avaliado e monitorado ao lo: HAM-A score assessed and monitored throughout treatment
translations.Encaminhamento especializado realizado c: Specialized referral performed according to identified severity
translations.Redução do escore HAM-A após intervençõe: Reduction in HAM-A score after therapeutic interventions
translations.Aplicar a HAM-A por meio de entrevista c: Administer the HAM-A through a structured clinical interview
translations.Encaminhar para avaliação psiquiátrica/p: Refer for psychiatric/psychological evaluation according to identified severity
translations.Orientar sobre técnicas de relaxamento e: Provide guidance on relaxation techniques and stress management
translations.Monitorar sintomas somáticos que podem m: Monitor somatic symptoms that may mimic other clinical conditions
translations.Reaplicar a escala periodicamente para m: Reapply the scale periodically to monitor response to pharmacological and/or psychotherapeutic treatment
translations.A HAM-A avalia 14 itens — humor ansioso,: The HAM-A assesses 14 items — anxious mood, tension, fears, insomnia, intellectual functions, depressed mood, muscular and sensory somatic symptoms, cardiovascular symptoms, respiratory symptoms, gastrointestinal symptoms, genitourinary symptoms, autonomic symptoms, and behavior during interview — each scored from 0 to 4, totaling 0 to 56 points.
translations.Desenvolvida por Max Hamilton em 1959, f: Developed by Max Hamilton in 1959, it was one of the first standardized scales for assessing the severity of anxiety symptoms, widely used in clinical research and practice to this day.
translations.Amplamente validada internacionalmente c: Widely validated internationally as a hetero-assessment instrument (administered by the interviewer) for measuring the severity of anxiety symptoms and monitoring treatment response.
translations.É uma escala de heteroavaliação, exigind: It is a hetero-assessment scale, requiring rater training for reliable application. Somatic symptoms may overlap with general medical conditions, requiring careful clinical evaluation to differentiate anxiety from organic causes.
translations.A HAM-A é uma escala de heteroavaliação: The HAM-A is a hetero-assessment scale (administered by a trained interviewer), not a self-report questionnaire.
translations.Avalia tanto sintomas psíquicos (humor a: It assesses both psychic symptoms (anxious mood, tension, fears) and somatic symptoms (cardiovascular, respiratory, gastrointestinal, among others).
translations.Sintomas somáticos podem se sobrepor a c: Somatic symptoms may overlap with general medical conditions — always consider the complete clinical context before attributing symptoms solely to anxiety.
translations.Reaplique a escala periodicamente para m: Reapply the scale periodically to monitor response to pharmacological and/or psychotherapeutic treatment.
translations.Quantos itens compõem a HAM-A e qual a p: How many items make up the HAM-A and what is the maximum score?
translations.A HAM-A avalia 14 itens, cada um pontuad: The HAM-A assesses 14 items, each scored from 0 to 4, totaling a maximum score of 56 points.
translations.A HAM-A é um instrumento de autorrelato: Is the HAM-A a self-report or hetero-assessment instrument?
translations.Pode ser ambos, sem diferença: It can be both, no difference
translations.A HAM-A é uma escala de heteroavaliação,: The HAM-A is a hetero-assessment scale, administered by a trained interviewer/clinician, not a self-report questionnaire.
translations.A HAM-A avalia apenas sintomas psíquicos: Does the HAM-A assess only psychic symptoms of anxiety?
translations.Não, avalia tanto sintomas psíquicos qua: No, it assesses both psychic and somatic symptoms
translations.Avalia apenas o comportamento durante a: Assesses only behavior during the interview
translations.A HAM-A avalia tanto sintomas psíquicos: The HAM-A assesses both psychic symptoms (anxious mood, tension, fears, intellectual functions) and somatic symptoms (cardiovascular, respiratory, gastrointestinal, among others).
translations.Qual a diferença entre a HAM-A e o GAD-7: What is the difference between the HAM-A and the GAD-7?
translations.O GAD-7 é um questionário breve de autor: The GAD-7 is a brief self-report questionnaire (7 items), while the HAM-A is a more extensive scale (14 items) of hetero-assessment, administered by a trained interviewer, and evaluates somatic symptoms in more detail in addition to psychic symptoms.
translations.A HAM-A pode ser usada para diagnosticar: Can the HAM-A be used to diagnose an anxiety disorder?
translations.Não. A HAM-A avalia a gravidade dos sint: No. The HAM-A assesses the severity of anxiety symptoms and is useful for monitoring treatment response, but the diagnosis of a specific anxiety disorder requires a complete clinical evaluation with formal diagnostic criteria (e.g., DSM-5 or ICD-11).
translations.Como funciona a Escala de Hamilton para: How the Hamilton Anxiety Rating Scale (HAM-A) works
translations.<p>A HAM-A soma a pontuação (0 a 4) de 1:
The HAM-A sums the score (0 to 4) of 14 items assessing psychic and somatic symptoms of anxiety, totaling 0 to 56 points.

Quick guide: 0-17 = Mild | 18-24 = Mild to moderate | 25-30 = Moderate to severe | 31-56 = Severe.

translations.Escala de Hamilton para Ansiedade: cálcu: Hamilton Anxiety Rating Scale: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Avaliar a gravidade dos sintomas de ansi: Assess the severity of anxiety symptoms, both psychic and somatic, through 14 items rated by the interviewer. Indicated for assessing the severity of anxious symptoms in adults with anxiety disorders and for monitoring treatment response over time.
translations.HAMILTON, M. The assessment of anxiety s: HAMILTON, M. The assessment of anxiety states by rating. British Journal of Medical Psychology, v. 32, n. 1, p. 50-55, 1959.
translations.MAIER, W. et al. The Hamilton Anxiety Sc: MAIER, W. et al. The Hamilton Anxiety Scale: reliability, validity and sensitivity to change in anxiety and depressive disorders. Journal of Affective Disorders, v. 14, n. 1, p. 61-68, 1988.
translations.ASSOCIAÇÃO BRASILEIRA DE PSIQUIATRIA. Di: ASSOCIAÇÃO BRASILEIRA DE PSIQUIATRIA. Diretrizes para o diagnóstico e tratamento dos transtornos de ansiedade. São Paulo: ABP, 2020.
translations.A HAM-A soma a pontuação (0 a 4) de 14 i: The HAM-A sums the score (0 to 4) of 14 items assessing psychic and somatic symptoms of anxiety, totaling 0 to 56 points.
translations.0-17 = Leve | 18-24 = Leve a moderada |: 0-17 = Mild | 18-24 = Mild to moderate | 25-30 = Moderate to severe | 31-56 = Severe.
translations.Calcule o escore HAS-BLED para estimar o: Calculate the HAS-BLED score to estimate bleeding risk in patients on anticoagulation, especially in atrial fibrillation. Includes interpretation and evidence.
translations.Estimar o risco de sangramento maior em: To estimate the risk of major bleeding in patients on or with an indication for anticoagulant therapy, especially in atrial fibrillation, guiding monitoring and management of modifiable risk factors.
translations.Indicada para pacientes com fibrilação a: Indicated for patients with atrial fibrillation on or with an indication for anticoagulation, as a complement to thromboembolic risk assessment (CHA2DS2-VASc).
translations.Pacientes adultos com fibrilação atrial: Adult patients with atrial fibrillation on or candidates for anticoagulant therapy.
translations.Pressão arterial sistólica maior que 160: Systolic blood pressure greater than 160 mmHg.
translations.Diálise, transplante renal, ou creatinin: Dialysis, renal transplant, or serum creatinine greater than 2.26 mg/dL.
translations.Cirrose, bilirrubina maior que 2x o limi: Cirrhosis, bilirubin greater than 2x the upper limit of normal, or AST/ALT/alkaline phosphatase greater than 3x normal.
translations.História de sangramento maior prévio, an: History of previous major bleeding, significant anemia, or known predisposition to bleeding.
translations.INR instável ou elevado, ou tempo em fai: Unstable or elevated INR, or time in therapeutic range less than 60% (applicable to patients on warfarin).
translations.Uso concomitante de antiplaquetários (AA: Concomitant use of antiplatelets (aspirin, clopidogrel) or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs).
translations.Consumo de álcool igual ou superior a 8: Alcohol consumption of 8 or more drinks per week.
translations.Baixo risco de sangramento maior associa: Low risk of major bleeding associated with anticoagulant therapy.
translations.• Manter anticoagulação conforme indicaç:
• Maintain anticoagulation as indicated, with routine monitoring
• Periodically reassess modifiable risk factors
translations.Alto risco de sangramento. Isso não cont: High risk of bleeding. This does not automatically contraindicate anticoagulation but indicates a need for more careful monitoring and attention to modifiable risk factors.
translations.• Não suspender anticoagulação automatic:
• Do not automatically discontinue anticoagulation based solely on a high score — reassess risk-benefit with the medical team
• Correct modifiable risk factors (blood pressure control, review of concomitant medications, reduction of alcohol consumption)
• More frequent monitoring, especially of INR if on warfarin
translations.Ausência ou deficiência de informação co: Absence or deficiency of cognitive information related to anticoagulant therapy and its risks.
translations.Adere ao monitoramento laboratorial pres: Adheres to prescribed laboratory monitoring (e.g., INR)
translations.Evita uso concomitante de medicamentos d: Avoids concomitant use of high-risk medications without medical guidance
translations.Calcular o escore HAS-BLED na avaliação: Calculate the HAS-BLED score at initial assessment and periodically
translations.Identificar e corrigir fatores de risco: Identify and correct modifiable risk factors (blood pressure, concomitant medications, alcohol consumption)
translations.Orientar o paciente sobre sinais de alar: Educate the patient about warning signs of bleeding
translations.Monitorar exames laboratoriais relaciona: Monitor laboratory tests related to anticoagulation (e.g., INR)
translations.O escore HAS-BLED foi desenvolvido por P: The HAS-BLED score was developed by Pisters and colleagues to estimate the risk of major bleeding in patients with atrial fibrillation on anticoagulant therapy, assessing 9 risk factors, most of which are modifiable.
translations.Recomendado pelas diretrizes europeias d: Recommended by European cardiology guidelines as a complementary tool to CHA2DS2-VASc, not to contraindicate anticoagulation, but to identify and correct modifiable risk factors and determine the need for closer monitoring.
translations.Validado extensivamente na literatura ca: Extensively validated in the cardiology literature, with good predictive ability for major bleeding, and widely used in conjunction with thromboembolic risk scores.
translations.Um escore elevado não deve, isoladamente: A high score should not, by itself, contraindicate anticoagulation when clinically indicated—it should prompt correction of modifiable factors and closer monitoring, not automatic treatment suspension.
translations.Um HAS-BLED elevado não é motivo automát: A high HAS-BLED is not an automatic reason to stop anticoagulation—use it to identify and correct modifiable risk factors.
translations.Avalie sempre em conjunto com o CHA2DS2-: Always assess together with CHA2DS2-VASc for a balanced therapeutic decision between stroke risk and bleeding risk.
translations.Fatores modificáveis incluem controle pr: Modifiable factors include blood pressure control, review of concomitant medications, and reduction of alcohol consumption.
translations.Reavalie periodicamente, especialmente a: Reassess periodically, especially after changes in renal or hepatic function or medication regimen.
translations.Quantos fatores de risco compõem o HAS-B: How many risk factors make up HAS-BLED?
translations.O HAS-BLED avalia 9 fatores de risco, a: HAS-BLED assesses 9 risk factors, most worth 1 point each.
translations.Um HAS-BLED elevado (≥3) deve levar à su: Should a high HAS-BLED (≥3) lead to automatic anticoagulation suspension?
translations.Não, deve motivar correção de fatores mo: No, it should prompt correction of modifiable factors and closer monitoring
translations.Apenas se o paciente tiver mais de 80 an: Only if the patient is over 80 years old
translations.Sim, é uma contraindicação absoluta: Yes, it is an absolute contraindication
translations.Um escore elevado não contraindica autom: A high score does not automatically contraindicate anticoagulation—it should prompt identification and correction of modifiable risk factors, as well as closer monitoring.
translations.Com qual outro escore o HAS-BLED é frequ: With which other score is HAS-BLED often used together?
translations.O HAS-BLED é usado em conjunto com o CHA: HAS-BLED is used together with CHA2DS2-VASc to balance the decision between stroke (thromboembolic) risk and bleeding risk in atrial fibrillation.
translations.O HAS-BLED contraindica a anticoagulação: Does HAS-BLED contraindicate anticoagulation in high scores?
translations.Não diretamente. Um escore elevado indic: Not directly. A high score indicates a greater need for monitoring and correction of modifiable risk factors, but the final decision on anticoagulation should also weigh the thromboembolic risk (CHA2DS2-VASc) and be individualized.
translations.Quais fatores de risco do HAS-BLED são m: Which HAS-BLED risk factors are modifiable?
translations.Hipertensão não controlada, uso concomit: Uncontrolled hypertension, concomitant use of antiplatelets/NSAIDs, labile INR, and excessive alcohol consumption are examples of potentially modifiable factors with clinical intervention.
translations.<p>O HAS-BLED soma pontos de 9 fatores d:
HAS-BLED sums points from 9 risk factors—hypertension, abnormal renal and liver function, prior stroke, prior bleeding, labile INR, advanced age, use of risk medications, and excessive alcohol consumption—each worth 1 point, totaling 0 to 9 points.

Quick guide: 0-2 = Low risk | ≥3 = High bleeding risk (does not contraindicate anticoagulation but requires closer monitoring).
translations.HAS-BLED - Risco de Sangramento em Antic: HAS-BLED - Bleeding Risk in Anticoagulation | Nursing Calculators
translations.Estimar o risco de sangramento maior em: To estimate the risk of major bleeding in patients on or with indication for anticoagulant therapy, especially in atrial fibrillation, guiding monitoring and management of modifiable risk factors. Indicated for patients with atrial fibrillation on or with indication for anticoagulation, as a complement to thromboembolic risk assessment (CHA2DS2-VASc).
translations.PISTERS, R. et al. A novel user-friendly: PISTERS, R. et al. A novel user-friendly score (HAS-BLED) to assess 1-year risk of major bleeding in patients with atrial fibrillation. Chest, v. 138, n. 5, p. 1093-1100, 2010.
translations.O HAS-BLED soma pontos de 9 fatores de r: HAS-BLED sums points from 9 risk factors — hypertension, abnormal renal and liver function, prior stroke, prior bleeding, labile INR, advanced age, use of risk medications, and harmful alcohol consumption — each worth 1 point, totaling 0 to 9 points.
translations.0-2 = Baixo risco | ≥3 = Alto risco de s: 0-2 = Low risk | ≥3 = High risk of bleeding (does not contraindicate anticoagulation but requires closer monitoring).
translations.HEART Score - Risco de Eventos Cardíacos: HEART Score - Risk of Cardiac Events in Chest Pain
translations.Calcule o HEART Score para estratificar: Calculate the HEART Score to stratify the risk of major adverse cardiac events in patients with chest pain in the emergency department. Includes interpretation and evidence.
translations.Estratificar o risco de eventos cardíaco: Stratify the risk of major adverse cardiac events (MACE) in patients presenting to the emergency department with chest pain, guiding the decision between safe discharge or further investigation/hospitalization.
translations.Indicada para pacientes adultos com dor: Indicated for adult patients with acute chest pain in the emergency department, with suspected acute coronary syndrome.
translations.Pacientes adultos atendidos em unidade d: Adult patients seen in the emergency department with a complaint of chest pain.
translations.Grau de suspeição da história clínica pa: Degree of suspicion of the clinical history for acute coronary syndrome.
translations.Hipertensão, diabetes, tabagismo, obesid: Hypertension, diabetes, smoking, obesity, dyslipidemia, family history, or known atherosclerotic disease.
translations.3 ou Mais Fatores de Risco / Doença Ater: 3 or More Risk Factors / Known Atherosclerotic Disease
translations.Valor da troponina em relação ao limite: Troponin value relative to the upper limit of normal of the method used.
translations.Menor ou Igual ao Limite Normal: Less than or Equal to Normal Limit
translations.Entre 1 e 3 Vezes o Limite Normal: Between 1 and 3 Times the Normal Limit
translations.Maior que 3 Vezes o Limite Normal: Greater than 3 Times the Normal Limit
translations.Baixo risco de eventos cardíacos adverso: Low risk of major adverse cardiac events (MACE) within 6 weeks (approximately 1-2%).
translations.• Considerar alta segura com acompanhame:
• Consider safe discharge with outpatient follow-up
• Educate on warning signs for immediate return
• Clinical reassessment if symptoms persist or worsen
translations.Risco moderado de eventos cardíacos adve: Moderate risk of major adverse cardiac events, generally indicating need for observation and further investigation.
translations.• Observação em unidade de dor torácica:
• Observation in chest pain unit or brief hospitalization
• Further investigation (serial troponin, stress test, or coronary CT angiography)
• Communicate to cardiology team
translations.Alto risco de eventos cardíacos adversos: High risk of major adverse cardiac events, often indicating need for invasive investigation.
translations.• Internação hospitalar\n• Avaliação car:
• Hospital admission
• Urgent cardiology evaluation
• Consider early invasive strategy (cardiac catheterization) as per medical assessment
translations.Diminuição na circulação sanguínea coron: Decrease in coronary blood circulation, which may compromise cardiac function.
translations.Sensação vaga e incômoda de desconforto: Vague and uncomfortable sensation of discomfort or fear, related to diagnostic uncertainty and fear of a serious cardiac event.
translations.Marcadores de necrose miocárdica dentro: Myocardial necrosis markers within normal limits
translations.Aplicar o HEART Score de forma sistemáti: Apply the HEART Score systematically in chest pain assessment
translations.Coletar troponina seriada conforme proto: Collect serial troponin per institutional protocol
translations.Monitorar sinais vitais e ritmo cardíaco: Monitor vital signs and cardiac rhythm continuously
translations.Comunicar escore de alto risco imediatam: Communicate high-risk score immediately to medical team
translations.O HEART Score foi desenvolvido por Backu: The HEART Score was developed by Backus et al., combining 5 components — History, ECG, Age, Risk factors, and Troponin — to stratify the risk of major adverse cardiac events (MACE) in patients with chest pain in the emergency department.
translations.Amplamente adotado em protocolos de dor: Widely adopted in chest pain protocols in emergency services, allowing safe identification of low-risk patients eligible for early discharge, optimizing hospital resources.
translations.Validado extensivamente em estudos multi: Extensively validated in multicenter studies, demonstrating good discriminative ability for MACE at 6 weeks, superior to strategies based solely on troponin or ECG alone.
translations.Não substitui o julgamento clínico, espe: Does not replace clinical judgment, especially in atypical presentations. Troponin should be interpreted according to the laboratory method available at the institution (conventional versus high-sensitivity).
translations.O HEART Score deve ser calculado após EC: The HEART Score should be calculated after ECG and at least one troponin measurement are available.
translations.Escores de baixo risco (0-3) têm alto va: Low-risk scores (0-3) have high negative predictive value for cardiac events at 6 weeks, supporting safe discharge decision.
translations.Reavalie o escore se novos dados clínico: Reassess the score if new clinical or laboratory data (serial troponin) become available.
translations.Combine sempre com o julgamento clínico,: Always combine with clinical judgment, especially in atypical presentations of acute coronary syndrome.
translations.O que significa o 'T' no acrônimo HEART: What does the 'T' stand for in the HEART Score acronym?
translations.O 'T' representa Troponina, um dos 5 com: The 'T' stands for Troponin, one of the 5 components of the HEART Score, along with History, ECG, Age, and Risk factors.
translations.Qual a principal utilidade clínica do HE: What is the main clinical utility of a low-risk HEART Score (0-3)?
translations.Apoiar decisão de alta segura do pronto-: Support safe discharge from the emergency department
translations.Pacientes com HEART Score de 0 a 3 têm b: Patients with a HEART Score of 0 to 3 have a low risk of major adverse cardiac events at 6 weeks, supporting the decision for safe discharge with outpatient follow-up.
translations.Um HEART Score de 8 pontos geralmente in: A HEART Score of 8 points generally indicates which management?
translations.Escores de 7 a 10 pontos indicam alto ri: Scores of 7 to 10 indicate high risk, generally prompting hospital admission and urgent cardiology evaluation with possible invasive investigation.
translations.O HEART Score substitui o ECG e a tropon: Does the HEART Score replace ECG and troponin?
translations.Não. Pelo contrário, o HEART Score depen: No. On the contrary, the HEART Score depends on these two tests as essential components of the calculation, combining them with clinical history, age, and risk factors.
translations.O HEART Score pode ser usado em qualquer: Can the HEART Score be used in any patient with chest pain?
translations.É validado especificamente para dor torá: It is specifically validated for chest pain suggestive of acute coronary syndrome in the emergency department. It should not be applied in isolation to clearly non-cardiac presentations or in contexts very different from the original validation.
translations.<p>O HEART Score soma pontos de 5 compon:
The HEART Score sums points from 5 components — History, ECG, Age, Risk factors, and Troponin — each scored from 0 to 2, totaling 0 to 10 points, which stratify the risk of major adverse cardiac events in patients with chest pain.

Quick guide: 0-3 = Low risk | 4-6 = Moderate risk | 7-10 = High risk.
translations.HEART Score - Risco de Eventos Cardíacos: HEART Score - Risk of Cardiac Events in Chest Pain | Nursing Calculators
translations.HEART Score: cálculo, fundamentação e ev: HEART Score: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.2 — 3 ou Mais Fatores de Risco / Doença: 2 — 3 or More Risk Factors / Known Atherosclerotic Disease
translations.0 — Menor ou Igual ao Limite Normal: 0 — Less than or Equal to Normal Limit
translations.Estratificar o risco de eventos cardíaco: Stratify the risk of major adverse cardiac events (MACE) in patients presenting to the emergency department with chest pain, guiding the decision between safe discharge or further investigation/admission. Indicated for adult patients with acute chest pain in the emergency department with suspected acute coronary syndrome.
translations.BACKUS, B. E. et al. A prospective valid: BACKUS, B. E. et al. A prospective validation of the HEART score for chest pain patients at the emergency department. International Journal of Cardiology, v. 168, n. 3, p. 2153-2158, 2013.
SIX, A. J.; BACKUS, B. E.; KELDER, J. C. Chest pain in the emergency room: value of the HEART score. Netherlands Heart Journal, v. 16, n. 6, p. 191-196, 2008.
translations.O HEART Score soma pontos de 5 component: The HEART Score sums points from 5 components — History, ECG, Age, Risk factors, and Troponin — each scored from 0 to 2, totaling 0 to 10 points, which stratify the risk of major adverse cardiac events in patients with chest pain.
translations.0-3 = Baixo risco | 4-6 = Risco moderado: 0-3 = Low risk | 4-6 = Moderate risk | 7-10 = High risk.
translations.Hendrich II Fall Risk Model - Risco de Q: Hendrich II Fall Risk Model - Fall Risk in Adults
translations.Calcule o Hendrich II Fall Risk Model pa: Calculate the Hendrich II Fall Risk Model to assess fall risk in hospitalized adult patients. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Identificar pacientes adultos hospitaliz: Identify hospitalized adult patients at increased risk of falls based on clinical factors and performance on the Get-Up-and-Go test.
translations.Indicada na triagem de risco de quedas e: Indicated for fall risk screening in hospitalized adult patients, usually on admission and reassessed periodically.
translations.Presença de confusão mental, desorientaç: Presence of confusion, disorientation, or impulsivity.
translations.Incontinência, urgência urinária/intesti: Incontinence, urinary/bowel urgency, or need for frequent elimination.
translations.Presença de tontura ou vertigem relatada: Presence of reported dizziness or vertigo.
translations.Uso de qualquer benzodiazepínico ou tran: Use of any benzodiazepine or tranquilizer.
translations.Observe o paciente se levantando de uma: Observe the patient rising from a chair without using arm support, if possible.
translations.Capaz de levantar em um único movimento,: Able to rise in one single movement, without loss of balance
translations.Impulsiona-se com as mãos, bem-sucedido: Pushes up with hands, successful on first attempt
translations.Pontuação abaixo de 5, associada a baixo: Score below 5, associated with low fall risk.
translations.• Manter precauções padrão de segurança:
• Maintain standard safety precautions (bed in low position, call bell within reach)
• Reassess periodically and after changes in clinical status or medications
translations.Pontuação igual ou superior a 5 indica a: Score of 5 or higher indicates high fall risk.
translations.• Implementar protocolo institucional de:
• Implement institutional fall prevention protocol (visual identification, increased supervision)
• Review medications associated with risk (antiepileptics, benzodiazepines)
• Educate patient and family about safety measures
• Reassess frequently during hospitalization
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a quedas, que: Increased susceptibility to falls, which may cause physical harm and compromise health.
translations.Início abrupto de perturbações reversíve: Abrupt onset of reversible disturbances of consciousness, attention, cognition, and perception, when present as an identified risk factor.
translations.Limitação no movimento físico independen: Limitation in independent, intentional physical movement of the body or one or more extremities, related to performance on the Get-Up-and-Go test.
translations.Escore de Hendrich II avaliado na admiss: Hendrich II score assessed on admission and reassessed periodically
translations.Protocolo de prevenção de quedas impleme: Fall prevention protocol implemented according to identified risk
translations.Aplicar o Hendrich II Fall Risk Model na: Apply the Hendrich II Fall Risk Model on admission and reassess periodically
translations.Implementar identificação visual de risc: Implement visual fall risk identification (wristband, signage) per institutional protocol
translations.Revisar medicações associadas a risco de: Review fall risk-associated medications with medical/pharmacy team
translations.Orientar paciente e acompanhantes sobre: Educate patient and companions about safety measures
translations.Manter cama em posição baixa e campainha: Keep bed in low position and call bell within reach
translations.O Hendrich II Fall Risk Model avalia 7 f: The Hendrich II Fall Risk Model assesses 7 clinical risk factors (confusion/disorientation/impulsivity, symptomatic depression, altered elimination, dizziness, male gender, use of antiepileptics, and use of benzodiazepines) added to performance on the Get-Up-and-Go test, totaling a score from 0 to 16 points.
translations.Desenvolvido por Ann Hendrich e colabora: Developed by Ann Hendrich and collaborators, it is a widely used tool in hospitals for fall risk screening in adult patients, with good predictive validity validated in various hospital settings.
translations.Validado em múltiplos estudos com boa se: Validated in multiple studies with good sensitivity and specificity for predicting falls in hospitalized patients, being one of the recommended tools for systematic fall risk screening.
translations.Como toda ferramenta de triagem, não sub: Like any screening tool, it does not replace individualized clinical judgment. The score should be reassessed whenever there is a change in the patient's clinical status, cognition, or medication.
translations.O teste Get-Up-and-Go pode contribuir co: The Get-Up-and-Go test can contribute up to 4 of the total 16 points — observe the patient's performance carefully when rising from the chair.
translations.O ponto de corte de 5 pontos ou mais par: The cutoff point of 5 points or more for high risk is the most widely used in clinical practice.
translations.Reavalie o escore sempre que houver alte: Reassess the score whenever there is a change in the patient's clinical status, cognition, or medication.
translations.Combine o resultado com a observação de: Combine the result with observation of environmental factors (lighting, footwear, assistive devices) for a more comprehensive prevention plan.
translations.Qual fator de risco tem o maior peso iso: Which risk factor has the highest individual weight in the Hendrich II?
translations.Confusão/desorientação/impulsividade tem: Confusion/disorientation/impulsivity has the highest individual weight among fixed risk factors, worth 4 points.
translations.Qual pontuação de corte é utilizada para: What cutoff score is used to indicate high fall risk?
translations.Uma pontuação igual ou superior a 5 pont: A score of 5 or higher indicates high fall risk in the Hendrich II Fall Risk Model.
translations.Quantos pontos, no máximo, o teste Get-U: How many points can the Get-Up-and-Go test contribute at most to the total score?
translations.O teste Get-Up-and-Go pode contribuir co: The Get-Up-and-Go test can contribute up to 4 points if the patient is unable to stand up without assistance.
translations.O Hendrich II é usado em pacientes de qu: Is the Hendrich II used for patients of any age?
translations.É validado principalmente para pacientes: It is primarily validated for hospitalized adult patients. For the pediatric population, specific scales such as the Humpty Dumpty Falls Scale are used.
translations.Com que frequência o Hendrich II deve se: How often should the Hendrich II be reassessed?
translations.Geralmente é aplicado na admissão e reav: It is usually applied on admission and reassessed periodically according to institutional protocol, as well as whenever there is a significant change in the patient's clinical status, cognition, or medication.
translations.Como funciona o Hendrich II Fall Risk Mo: How the Hendrich II Fall Risk Model works
translations.<p>O Hendrich II soma a pontuação de 7 f:
The Hendrich II sums the scores of 7 risk factors (confusion, depression, altered elimination, dizziness, male sex, antiepileptics, and benzodiazepines) with the performance on the Get-Up-and-Go test, totaling 0 to 16 points.

Quick guide: <5 points = Low risk | ≥5 points = High fall risk.

translations.1 — Impulsiona-se com as mãos, bem-suced: 1 — Pushes up with hands, successful on first attempt
translations.Identificar pacientes adultos hospitaliz: Identify hospitalized adult patients at increased risk of falls based on clinical factors and performance on the Get-Up-and-Go test. Indicated for fall risk screening in hospitalized adult patients, usually on admission and reassessed periodically.

translations.O Hendrich II soma a pontuação de 7 fato: The Hendrich II sums the scores of 7 risk factors (confusion, depression, altered elimination, dizziness, male gender, antiepileptics, and benzodiazepines) with the performance on the Get-Up-and-Go test, totaling 0 to 16 points.
translations.5 pontos = Baixo risco | ≥5 pontos = Alt: <5 points = Low risk | ≥5 points = High fall risk.
translations.Escala de Humpty Dumpty - Risco de Queda: Humpty Dumpty Fall Risk Assessment Scale for Pediatric Patients
translations.Calcule a Escala de Humpty Dumpty para a: Calculate the Humpty Dumpty Scale to assess fall risk in hospitalized pediatric patients. Includes interpretation, nursing diagnosis, and evidence.
translations.Identificar pacientes pediátricos hospit: Identify hospitalized pediatric patients at increased risk of falls based on 7 clinical and developmental factors.
translations.Indicada na triagem de risco de quedas e: Indicated for fall risk screening in hospitalized pediatric patients at admission and periodically reassessed during hospitalization.
translations.Pacientes pediátricos hospitalizados (nã: Hospitalized pediatric patients (not validated for use in adult patients).
translations.Nível de percepção do paciente sobre sua: Patient's level of awareness of their own limitations.
translations.Fatores ambientais e histórico de quedas: Environmental factors and history of falls.
translations.Histórico de quedas restrito ao berço/ca: History of falls restricted to crib/bed/furniture
translations.Tempo desde procedimento cirúrgico, seda: Time since surgical procedure, sedation, or anesthesia.
translations.Uso de medicações associadas a risco de: Use of medications associated with fall risk (sedatives, hypnotics, diuretics, etc.).
translations.Múltiplas medicações de risco ou uso de: Multiple risk medications or use of sedatives
translations.Pontuação entre 7 e 11 pontos indica bai: Score between 7 and 11 indicates low fall risk relative to the baseline pediatric population.
translations.• Manter precauções padrão de segurança:
• Maintain standard pediatric safety precautions (bed in low position, obstacle-free environment)
• Educate patient and family about safety in the hospital environment
• Reassess periodically and after changes in clinical status
translations.Pontuação de 12 pontos ou mais indica al: Score of 12 or more indicates high fall risk, requiring an intensified prevention protocol.
translations.• Implementar protocolo de alto risco (i:
• Implement high-risk protocol (visual identification, fall risk bracelet/signage)
• Consider increased supervision, including possible direct observation
• Thoroughly educate family about risks and importance of not leaving the child unattended
• Review risk medications with medical/pharmacy team
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a quedas, que: Increased susceptibility to falls, which may cause physical harm and compromise health, related to the child's developmental and clinical factors.
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a lesão tecidu: Increased susceptibility to accidental tissue injury, related to age, mobility, and hospital environment.
translations.Funcionamento familiar que não consegue: Family functioning that cannot support the well-being of its members, relevant when intensive caregiver education about child supervision is needed.
translations.Escore de Humpty Dumpty avaliado na admi: Humpty Dumpty score assessed at admission and reassessed periodically
translations.Família orientada sobre medidas de segur: Family educated about safety measures and supervision
translations.Aplicar a Escala de Humpty Dumpty na adm: Apply the Humpty Dumpty Scale at admission and reassess periodically
translations.Implementar identificação visual de risc: Implement visual risk identification (bracelet, door/bed signage) per institutional protocol
translations.Orientar pais/cuidadores sobre a importâ: Educate parents/caregivers about the importance of not leaving the high-risk child unsupervised
translations.Manter grades do leito elevadas e ambien: Keep bed rails raised and environment free of obstacles
translations.Revisar medicações sedativas ou de risco: Review sedative or risk medications with medical/pharmacy team
translations.A Escala de Humpty Dumpty avalia 7 parâm: The Humpty Dumpty Scale assesses 7 parameters — age, sex, diagnosis, cognitive impairment, environmental factors, response to surgery/sedation/anesthesia, and medication use — each scored according to a specific table, summing to a total score between 7 and 23 points.
translations.Desenvolvida especificamente para a popu: Developed specifically for the hospitalized pediatric population, unlike fall risk scales for adults, it incorporates developmental factors inherent to childhood, such as the physical instability characteristic of certain age groups.
translations.Validada em estudos caso-controle em amb: Validated in case-control studies in the pediatric hospital setting, demonstrating good sensitivity in identifying high-risk patients, and is widely adopted in pediatric patient safety protocols.
translations.Não deve ser utilizada em pacientes adul: Should not be used in adult patients, as it was developed and validated specifically for the pediatric population. Revised versions of the scale (HDFS 2.0) removed the sex and medication parameters and adjusted the age score, which may yield differences compared to the original version used here.
translations.A Escala de Humpty Dumpty é específica p: The Humpty Dumpty Scale is specific to pediatric patients — it should not be applied to adults.
translations.O fator 'histórico de quedas prévias' no: The factor 'history of previous falls' in the environmental factors domain represents the single strongest warning sign in that domain.
translations.Crianças menores de 3 anos recebem a mai: Children under 3 years receive the highest score on the age factor, due to the physical instability and lack of risk perception inherent to this age group.
translations.Reavalie o escore sempre que houver muda: Reassess the score whenever there is a change in the child's clinical status, cognitive status, or medication.
translations.Alto risco — criança pequena com diagnós: High risk — young child with neurological diagnosis
translations.A Escala de Humpty Dumpty pode ser usada: Can the Humpty Dumpty Scale be used in adult patients?
translations.Não, é validada especificamente para a p: No, it is validated specifically for the pediatric population
translations.A Escala de Humpty Dumpty foi desenvolvi: The Humpty Dumpty Scale was developed and validated specifically for pediatric patients, considering developmental factors inherent to childhood.
translations.Qual pontuação total indica alto risco d: What total score indicates high fall risk in the original version of the scale?
translations.Uma pontuação de 12 pontos ou mais class: A score of 12 points or more classifies the pediatric patient as high fall risk.
translations.Qual faixa etária recebe a maior pontuaç: Which age group receives the highest score on the age factor?
translations.Crianças menores de 3 anos recebem a pon: Children under 3 years receive the highest score (4 points) on the age factor, due to greater physical instability and lower risk perception.
translations.A Escala de Humpty Dumpty serve para adu: Is the Humpty Dumpty Scale for adults?
translations.Não. É validada especificamente para a p: No. It is validated specifically for the hospitalized pediatric population. For adults, other specific tools are used, such as the Morse Scale or the Hendrich II Fall Risk Model.
translations.Existe uma versão atualizada da escala?: Is there an updated version of the scale?
translations.Sim. A versão revisada (HDFS 2.0) remove: Yes. The revised version (HDFS 2.0) removed the sex and medication parameters and adjusted the age factor score, aiming to improve the predictive performance of the tool. This calculator uses the original 7-item version, widely adopted in institutional protocols.
translations.Como funciona a Escala de Humpty Dumpty: How the Humpty Dumpty Scale works
translations.<p>A Escala de Humpty Dumpty soma a pont:
The Humpty Dumpty Scale sums the scores of 7 parameters — age, sex, diagnosis, cognitive impairment, environmental factors, response to surgery/sedation/anesthesia, and medication use — totaling 7 to 23 points.

Quick guide: 7-11 points = Low risk | ≥12 points = High fall risk.

translations.O fator &#x27;histórico de quedas prévia: The factor 'history of previous falls' in the environmental factors domain represents the greatest isolated warning sign in this domain.
translations.1 — Histórico de quedas restrito ao berç: 1 — History of falls restricted to crib/bed/furniture
translations.3 — Múltiplas medicações de risco ou uso: 3 — Multiple risk medications or use of sedatives

Indicated for fall risk screening in hospitalized pediatric patients at admission and reassessed periodically during hospitalization.

translations.Calcule o Índice de Massa Corporal (IMC): Calculate the Body Mass Index (BMI) during pregnancy and obtain guidance on nutritional classification and recommended weight gain. Includes interpretation and evidence.
translations.Calcular o Índice de Massa Corporal (IMC: Calculate the pregnant woman's Body Mass Index (BMI) and provide guidance on nutritional status classification, assisting in prenatal follow-up and defining gestational weight gain goals.
translations.Indicada no acompanhamento pré-natal, pa: Indicated in prenatal follow-up for assessment and monitoring of the pregnant woman's nutritional status throughout pregnancy.
Current weight of the pregnant woman, in kilograms.
Height of the pregnant woman, in meters.
translations.Baixo peso, associado a maior risco de r: Underweight, associated with increased risk of small for gestational age newborn and higher recommended gestational weight gain.
translations.• Encaminhar para avaliação e acompanham:
• Refer for nutritional assessment and follow-up
• Total recommended gestational weight gain goal generally between 12.5 and 18 kg (according to current guidelines)
• Monitor fetal growth and weight gain throughout prenatal care
translations.Estado nutricional adequado no início da: Adequate nutritional status at the beginning of pregnancy, associated with lower risk of weight-related complications.
translations.• Manter alimentação equilibrada e acomp:
• Maintain balanced diet and routine prenatal follow-up
• Total recommended gestational weight gain goal generally between 11.5 and 16 kg
• Monitor weight gain at each visit
translations.Sobrepeso, associado a maior risco de di: Overweight, associated with increased risk of gestational diabetes, gestational hypertension, and fetal macrosomia.
translations.• Encaminhar para avaliação e acompanham:
• Refer for nutritional assessment and follow-up
• Total recommended gestational weight gain goal generally between 7 and 11.5 kg
• Monitor blood glucose and blood pressure according to prenatal protocol
translations.Obesidade, associada a maior risco de di: Obesity, associated with increased risk of gestational diabetes, gestational hypertension, preeclampsia, fetal macrosomia, and delivery complications.
translations.• Encaminhar para acompanhamento nutrici:
• Refer for nutritional follow-up and high-risk prenatal care as assessed
• Total recommended gestational weight gain goal generally between 5 and 9 kg
• Strict monitoring of blood glucose, blood pressure, and fetal growth
translations.Ingestão de nutrientes insuficiente para: Insufficient nutrient intake to meet metabolic needs, related to gestational underweight.
translations.Risco de nutrição desequilibrada: mais d: Risk of imbalanced nutrition: more than body requirements
translations.Risco de ingestão de nutrientes que exce: Risk of nutrient intake exceeding metabolic needs, related to gestational overweight/obesity.
translations.Padrão de ingestão de nutrientes suficie: Pattern of nutrient intake sufficient to meet metabolic needs, which can be strengthened, applicable to pregnant women with adequate weight seeking healthy maintenance.
translations.IMC gestacional monitorado ao longo do p: Gestational BMI monitored throughout prenatal care
translations.Ganho de peso gestacional dentro da meta: Gestational weight gain within the recommended goal for nutritional classification
translations.Ausência de complicações relacionadas ao: Absence of complications related to nutritional status
translations.Calcular e monitorar o IMC gestacional a: Calculate and monitor gestational BMI at each prenatal visit
translations.Orientar sobre a meta de ganho de peso g: Provide guidance on gestational weight gain goal according to nutritional classification
translations.Encaminhar para acompanhamento nutricion: Refer for specialized nutritional follow-up when indicated
translations.Monitorar sinais de complicações associa: Monitor signs of complications associated with nutritional status (gestational diabetes, hypertension)
translations.Orientar sobre alimentação equilibrada e: Provide guidance on balanced diet and physical activity compatible with pregnancy
translations.O IMC gestacional é calculado da mesma f: Gestational BMI is calculated the same way as conventional BMI (weight in kg divided by height in meters squared), being used at the beginning of pregnancy to classify pre-gestational nutritional status and guide weight gain goals throughout pregnancy.
translations.As recomendações de ganho de peso gestac: Recommendations for gestational weight gain by pre-pregnancy BMI category were established by institutions such as the Institute of Medicine (IOM), and are adopted and adapted by prenatal guidelines, including those of the Brazilian Ministry of Health.
translations.A classificação por IMC e as metas de ga: BMI classification and associated weight gain goals are widely adopted in prenatal protocols, although more accurate monitoring throughout pregnancy uses specific curves (such as the Atalah curve), which consider BMI as a function of gestational age, not just the isolated value.
translations.Esta calculadora aplica a fórmula padrão: This calculator applies the standard BMI formula (weight/height²), useful mainly as an initial assessment. For monitoring the adequacy of weight gain throughout pregnancy, the use of specific BMI-for-gestational-age curves (Atalah curve), adopted by the Ministry of Health, is recommended, which are not incorporated in this simplified calculator.
translations.O IMC gestacional utiliza a mesma fórmul: Gestational BMI uses the same formula as conventional BMI: weight (kg) divided by height (m) squared.
translations.Idealmente, o IMC deve ser calculado com: Ideally, BMI should be calculated using pre-pregnancy weight or weight obtained at the first prenatal visit, to correctly define the weight gain goal.
translations.Para acompanhamento mais preciso ao long: For more accurate monitoring throughout pregnancy, use specific BMI-for-gestational-age curves (Atalah curve), adopted by the Ministry of Health.
translations.Gestantes com baixo peso ou obesidade de: Pregnant women with low weight or obesity should be referred for specialized nutritional follow-up.
translations.Qual a fórmula utilizada para calcular o: What formula is used to calculate gestational BMI?
translations.O IMC é calculado dividindo o peso (kg): BMI is calculated by dividing weight (kg) by height squared (m²), the same formula used outside pregnancy.
translations.Qual instituição estabeleceu as metas de: Which institution established the gestational weight gain goals by BMI category that are widely used?
translations.O Institute of Medicine (IOM) estabelece: The Institute of Medicine (IOM) established the gestational weight gain recommendations by pre-pregnancy BMI category widely adopted in prenatal guidelines.
translations.O IMC isolado é suficiente para acompanh: Is isolated BMI sufficient to monitor the adequacy of weight gain throughout pregnancy?
translations.Não, recomenda-se o uso de curvas especí: No, the use of specific BMI-for-gestational-age curves, such as the Atalah curve, is recommended
translations.Não tem nenhuma utilidade na gestação: It has no utility in pregnancy
translations.Só é válido no terceiro trimestre: It is only valid in the third trimester
translations.Para o acompanhamento da adequação do ga: For monitoring the adequacy of weight gain throughout pregnancy, the use of specific curves that consider BMI as a function of gestational age, such as the Atalah curve adopted by the Ministry of Health, is recommended.
translations.Devo usar o peso atual ou o peso pré-ges: Should I use current weight or pre-pregnancy weight to calculate gestational BMI?
translations.Idealmente, utiliza-se o peso pré-gestac: Ideally, pre-pregnancy weight (or weight obtained at the first prenatal visit) is used to classify initial nutritional status and define the appropriate weight gain goal for the remainder of pregnancy.
translations.Esta calculadora considera a idade gesta: Does this calculator consider gestational age in BMI classification?
translations.Não. Esta calculadora aplica a fórmula p: No. This calculator applies the standard BMI formula, useful primarily as an initial assessment. For monitoring weight adequacy throughout pregnancy, it is ideal to use specific BMI-for-gestational-age curves, such as the Atalah curve adopted by the Ministry of Health.
translations.<p>O IMC Gestacional é calculado dividin:
Gestational BMI is calculated by dividing weight (kg) by height squared (m²), the same as conventional BMI, and is used to classify the pregnant woman's nutritional status and guide weight gain goals during pregnancy.

Quick guide: <18.5 = Underweight | 18.5-24.9 = Adequate | 25-29.9 = Overweight | ≥30 = Obesity.

translations.Calcule o Índice de Choque (Shock Index): Calculate the Shock Index from heart rate and systolic blood pressure for early shock screening. Includes interpretation and evidence.
translations.Estimar precocemente a probabilidade de: Early estimation of the probability of hypovolemic/hemorrhagic shock based on the relationship between heart rate and systolic blood pressure, before frank hypotension manifests.
translations.Indicada em pacientes de trauma, hemorra: Indicated in trauma patients, gastrointestinal bleeding, obstetric hemorrhage, or any suspicion of hypovolemic shock, especially in initial triage.
translations.Pacientes adultos com suspeita de choque: Adult patients with suspected hypovolemic or hemorrhagic shock.
translations.Frequência cardíaca do paciente em batim: Patient's heart rate in beats per minute.
translations.Pressão arterial sistólica do paciente e: Patient's systolic blood pressure in mmHg.
translations.Índice de choque dentro da normalidade (: Shock index within normal range (normal reference value: 0.5 to 0.7).
translations.Valor limítrofe, próximo ao limite super: Borderline value, close to the upper limit of normal. Requires attention and reassessment.
translations.• Reavaliar sinais vitais com maior freq:
• Reassess vital signs more frequently
• Investigate possible sources of bleeding or volume loss
translations.Índice de choque igual ou superior a 1 e: Shock index equal to or greater than 1 is associated with increased risk of hypovolemic/hemorrhagic shock, even in the absence of frank hypotension.
translations.• Comunicar imediatamente à equipe médic:
• Immediately notify the medical team
• Investigate and treat the source of bleeding/volume loss
• Consider volume resuscitation and closer hemodynamic monitoring
translations.Índice de choque muito elevado, associad: Very high shock index, associated with established shock and greater need for intervention (massive transfusion, damage control surgery).
translations.• Acionar protocolo de choque/hemorragia:
• Activate shock/massive hemorrhage protocol per institution
• Volume resuscitation and blood products as per urgent medical evaluation
• Continuous and intensive monitoring
translations.Risco de fluxo sanguíneo inadequado aos: Risk of inadequate blood flow to body tissues, which may lead to potentially fatal cellular dysfunction.
translations.Calcular o Índice de Choque na triagem i: Calculate Shock Index in initial triage of at-risk patients
translations.Obter acesso venoso calibroso e auxiliar: Obtain large-bore venous access and assist with volume resuscitation as prescribed
translations.Comunicar índice elevado imediatamente à: Report elevated index immediately to medical team
translations.Investigar e auxiliar no controle da fon: Investigate and assist in controlling the source of bleeding
translations.O Índice de Choque é calculado pela razã: The Shock Index is calculated as the ratio of heart rate to systolic blood pressure, allowing early identification of hemodynamic compromise before frank hypotension manifests, since compensatory tachycardia often precedes blood pressure drop in hemorrhagic conditions.
translations.Amplamente utilizado em trauma, obstetrí: Widely used in trauma, obstetrics (postpartum hemorrhage), and clinical emergencies for rapid risk screening of shock, being more sensitive than isolated vital signs in early detection of hemodynamic compromise.
translations.Estudos demonstram associação entre índi: Studies demonstrate an association between elevated shock index (≥1) and increased need for transfusion, surgical intervention, and mortality in various clinical contexts, including trauma and postpartum hemorrhage.
translations.Pode ser influenciado por condições que: May be influenced by conditions that alter baseline heart rate (beta-blocker use, pacemaker, physical conditioning) or baseline blood pressure (chronic hypertensives), and should be interpreted in the complete clinical context of the patient.
translations.O Índice de Choque pode identificar comp: The Shock Index can identify hemodynamic compromise before blood pressure drops noticeably.
translations.Valores iguais ou superiores a 1 devem m: Values equal to or greater than 1 should prompt active investigation for a source of bleeding or volume loss.
translations.Considere o uso de betabloqueadores ou m: Consider the use of beta-blockers or pacemakers, which may mask the expected compensatory tachycardia.
translations.É especialmente útil em obstetrícia (hem: It is especially useful in obstetrics (postpartum hemorrhage) and trauma for rapid bedside triage.
translations.Como é calculado o Índice de Choque?: How is the Shock Index calculated?
translations.O Índice de Choque é a razão entre a fre: The Shock Index is the ratio of heart rate to systolic blood pressure (HR ÷ SBP).
translations.Qual o valor normal de referência do Índ: What is the normal reference value of the Shock Index?
translations.O valor normal de referência do Índice d: The normal reference value of the Shock Index is between 0.5 and 0.7.
translations.Por que o Índice de Choque pode detectar: Why can the Shock Index detect hemodynamic compromise before frank hypotension?
translations.Porque usa apenas a pressão arterial: Because it uses only blood pressure
translations.Porque a taquicardia compensatória frequ: Because compensatory tachycardia often precedes the drop in blood pressure
translations.Não há explicação fisiológica para isso: There is no physiological explanation for this
translations.Porque avalia apenas a temperatura corpo: Because it only assesses body temperature
translations.Em quadros hemorrágicos, mecanismos comp: In hemorrhagic conditions, compensatory mechanisms increase heart rate before blood pressure drops noticeably, making the index sensitive to early changes.
translations.O Índice de Choque substitui a avaliação: Does the Shock Index replace a complete clinical assessment?
translations.Não. É uma ferramenta de triagem rápida: No. It is a rapid triage tool that complements, but does not replace, a complete clinical assessment including physical examination, history, and other vital signs.
translations.O Índice de Choque é confiável em pacien: Is the Shock Index reliable in patients using beta-blockers?
translations.Pode ser menos confiável nesses paciente: It may be less reliable in these patients, as beta-blockers prevent the expected compensatory tachycardia, potentially masking a shock state even with an apparently normal heart rate.
translations.<p>O Índice de Choque é calculado dividi:
The Shock Index is calculated by dividing heart rate (bpm) by systolic blood pressure (mmHg), early identifying signs of hemodynamic compromise.

Formula: Shock Index = Heart Rate ÷ Systolic Blood Pressure.

Quick guide: 0.5-0.7 = Normal | 0.7-0.9 = Caution | 1.0-1.4 = Increased risk | ≥1.5 = High risk/established shock.

translations.Instrumento básico para realização de in: Basic instrument for performing surgical incisions. Used in all specialties to cut skin, subcutaneous tissue, and soft tissues. It comes in different numbers (3, 4, 7) depending on the blade to be used and the type of procedure.
translations.Lâminas descartáveis afiadas que se enca: Disposable sharp blades that fit onto the scalpel handle. Each number has a specific shape:
translations.Nº 10: formato curvilíneo para incisões: No. 10: curvilinear shape for large incisions
translations.Nº 11: formato pontiagudo para punções e: No. 11: pointed shape for punctures and precise incisions
translations.Nº 15: formato pequeno curvilíneo para i: No. 15: small curvilinear shape for small incisions
translations.Nº 20-24: formato para procedimentos mai: No. 20-24: shape for more extensive procedures
translations.Equipamento que utiliza corrente elétric: Equipment that uses high-frequency electrical current to cut tissues and simultaneously promote coagulation of blood vessels. Used in virtually all surgical specialties as an alternative or complement to the conventional scalpel.
translations.Tesoura robusta com lâminas retas e pont: Robust scissors with straight blades and blunt tips. Used for cutting denser tissues such as fascia, muscles, and sutures.
translations.Tesoura com lâminas curvas que permite a: Scissors with curved blades that allow access to areas of difficult visualization. Ideal for cutting tissues in cavities.
translations.Tesoura delicada com lâminas longas e fi: Delicate scissors with long, thin blades. Indicated for dissection and incision of delicate tissues.
translations.Mesma função da Metzenbaum reta, porém c: Same function as straight Metzenbaum, but with curved blades that facilitate access to deep anatomical spaces.
translations.Tesoura pequena e delicada com pontas fi: Small, delicate scissors with fine tips. Used in ophthalmology and microsurgery.
translations.Versão curva da tesoura Iris, permitindo: Curved version of Iris scissors, allowing greater precision in angled procedures.
translations.Tesoura de dissecação com lâminas retas: Dissection scissors with straight or curved blades and fine tips for precise dissection.
translations.Tesoura específica para oftalmologia, pe: Specific scissors for ophthalmology, allowing precise incisions in the cornea and sclera.
translations.Tesoura delicada com mecanismo de mola p: Delicate scissors with spring mechanism for microsurgery.
translations.Tesoura oftalmológica para secção de mús: Ophthalmic scissors for cutting ocular muscles.
translations.Tesoura específica para remoção de ponto: Specific scissors for removing suture stitches.
translations.Tesoura de grande porte para secção de t: Large scissors for cutting dense tissues in extensive procedures.
translations.Serra de fio de aço utilizada para secçã: Steel wire saw used for cutting bones.
translations.Instrumento cortante para remoção de fra: Cutting instrument for removing bone fragments.
translations.Instrumento cilíndrico cortante para rem: Cylindrical cutting instrument for removing corneal discs.
translations.Instrumento que utiliza corrente elétric: Instrument that uses electrical current for cutting and coagulation.
translations.Fresas para modelagem e acabamento de su: Burs for modeling and finishing bone surfaces.
translations.Broca ortopédica para perfuração de osso: Orthopedic drill for perforating long bones.
translations.Broca grossa para perfuração óssea em pr: Thick drill for bone perforation in external fixation procedures.
translations.Pinça hemostática com serrilhado parcial: Hemostatic clamp with partial serration. Used for clamping medium-caliber vessels.
translations.Mesma função da Kelly reta, porém com cu: Same function as straight Kelly, but curved for deep access.
translations.Pinça hemostática robusta com serrilhado: Robust hemostatic clamp with full serration. Indicated for larger vessels.
translations.Versão curva da Crile, maior versatilida: Curved version of Crile, greater versatility.
translations.Pinça hemostática delicada para vasos de: Delicate hemostatic clamp for small vessels.
translations.Versão curva da Mosquito para acesso pre: Curved version of Mosquito for precise access.
translations.Semelhante à Mosquito, design específico: Similar to Mosquito, specific design for greater firmness.
translations.Pinça robusta com serrilhado completo pa: Robust clamp with full serration for large vessels.
translations.Pinça hemostática de grande porte para p: Large hemostatic clamp for extensive procedures.
translations.Pinça robusta com dentes na ponta para t: Robust clamp with teeth at the tip for dense tissues.
translations.Pinça longa e robusta para procedimentos: Long, robust clamp for deep pelvic procedures.
translations.Pinça hemostática curva específica para: Curved hemostatic clamp specific for gynecology (hysterectomy).
translations.Pinça hemostática para procedimentos obs: Hemostatic clamp for obstetric and gynecologic procedures.
translations.Pinça hemostática parcial para suturas v: Partial hemostatic clamp for vascular sutures.
translations.Pinça delicada para oclusão temporária d: Delicate clamp for temporary vessel occlusion.
translations.Pinça com pontas arredondadas e serrilha: Clamp with rounded, serrated tips for delicate grasping (intestine).
translations.Pinça com dentes para preensão de tecido: Clamp with teeth for grasping dense tissues.
translations.Pinça hemostática com dentes na ponta pa: Hemostatic clamp with teeth at the tip for greater firmness.
translations.Pinça delicada para dissecção e isolamen: Delicate clamp for dissection and vascular isolation.
translations.Materiais absorvíveis para hemostasia po: Absorbable materials for hemostasis by compression.
translations.Corte e coagulação por vibração ultrassô: Cutting and coagulation by ultrasonic vibration.

Extraction of calculi from hollow organs.
translations.Extração de raízes dentárias e fragmento: Extraction of dental roots and bone fragments.
translations.Serra motorizada para secção óssea preci: Motorized saw for precise bone sectioning.
translations.Atraumática, para vísceras e tecidos del: Atraumatic, for viscera and delicate tissues.
Sharp, for skin and dense tissues.
Cutting of suture and metal wires.
translations.(Instrumentais para Afastamento e Exposi: (Instruments for Retraction and Exposure of the Surgical Field)
Basic manual retractor with dual use.
translations.Autoestático com garras para tecidos sup: Self-retaining with claws for superficial/deep tissues.
translations.Manual com valvas para cavidades profund: Manual with blades for deep cavities.
Visualization of anal canal and rectum.
Firm grasp of skin and dense tissues.
translations.Delicada, para cirurgia plástica e neuro: Delicate, for plastic surgery and neuro.
translations.Múltiplos dentes finos para preensão fir: Multiple fine teeth for firm and delicate grasp.
Robust for dense and large tissues.
translations.Preensão delicada e atraumática de alças: Delicate and atraumatic grasp of intestinal loops.
