translations.Fórmula de Parkland - Reposição Volêmica: Parkland Formula - Fluid Resuscitation in Burns
translations.Calcule o volume de reposição volêmica n: Calculate the fluid resuscitation volume in the first 24 hours for major burns using the Parkland Formula. Includes interpretation, NANDA-I, and evidence.
translations.Calcular o volume estimado de cristaloid: Calculate the estimated crystalloid volume for fluid resuscitation in the first 24 hours in major burn patients, based on body weight and total body surface area burned (TBSA).
translations.Indicada para adultos e crianças com que: Indicated for adults and children with extensive burns (usually TBSA ≥ 20% in adults) as an initial fluid resuscitation estimate, always adjusted according to clinical response.
translations.Pacientes vítimas de queimaduras extensa: Patients with extensive burns requiring fluid resuscitation in the first 24 hours post-injury.
translations.Peso corporal atual do paciente, em quil: Current body weight of the patient, in kilograms.
translations.Percentual de superfície corporal queima: Percentage of total body surface area burned, estimated e.g., by the Rule of Nines.
translations.Volume estimado de reposição relativamen: Relatively low estimated fluid volume, consistent with less extensive burns.
translations.• Administrar metade do volume calculado:
• Administer half of the calculated volume in the first 8 hours from the time of injury (not from arrival at the facility)
• Administer the other half over the next 16 hours
• Adjust according to urine output and clinical response (general goal 0.5-1 mL/kg/h in adults)
translations.Volume estimado de reposição moderado a: Moderate to high estimated fluid volume, usually associated with extensive burns requiring strict hemodynamic monitoring.
translations.• Garantir dois acessos venosos calibros:
• Ensure two large-bore IV access sites
• Monitor hourly urine output, vital signs, and perfusion continuously
• Adjust infusion rate according to clinical response, not just the calculated value
translations.Volume estimado elevado, compatível com: High estimated volume, consistent with very extensive burns, with risk of complications related to aggressive fluid resuscitation (abdominal compartment syndrome, pulmonary edema).
translations.• Internação em centro de queimados/tera:
• Admission to burn center/intensive care unit
• Invasive hemodynamic monitoring as indicated
• Vigilance for signs of abdominal compartment syndrome and fluid overload
• Reassess and adjust volume according to clinical response hourly
translations.Volume estimado muito elevado, compatíve: Very high estimated volume, consistent with critical burns and high risk of complications related to massive fluid resuscitation.
translations.• Cuidados intensivos especializados em:
• Specialized intensive care in a burn center
• Continuous invasive hemodynamic monitoring
• Multidisciplinary assessment for fine-tuning volume, avoiding both under-resuscitation and over-resuscitation
• Intensive surveillance for abdominal compartment syndrome and other complications
translations.Risco de diminuição do volume de líquido: Risk for decreased intravascular, interstitial, and/or intracellular fluid volume related to fluid loss from the burned area.
translations.Risco de diminuição, aumento ou rápida m: Risk for decrease, increase, or rapid shift from one to the other of intravascular, interstitial, and/or intracellular fluids related to aggressive fluid resuscitation.
translations.Quantidade inadequada de sangue bombeada: Inadequate amount of blood pumped by the heart to meet the metabolic demands of the body, related to hypovolemia from fluid loss in the burn.
translations.Diurese dentro da meta estabelecida (0,5: Urine output within established goal (0.5-1 mL/kg/h in adults)
translations.Ausência de sinais de sobrecarga hídrica: Absence of signs of fluid overload or hypovolemia
translations.Calcular o volume de reposição volêmica: Calculate fluid resuscitation volume according to the Parkland Formula
translations.Administrar metade do volume nas primeir: Administer half of the volume in the first 8 hours from the time of injury and the other half over the next 16 hours
translations.Monitorar diurese horária e ajustar a ta: Monitor hourly urine output and adjust infusion rate according to clinical response
translations.Vigiar sinais de sobrecarga hídrica (ede: Monitor for signs of fluid overload (pulmonary edema) and abdominal compartment syndrome
translations.Registrar volumes infundidos e diurese a: Record infused volumes and urine output every hour
translations.A Fórmula de Parkland estima o volume de: The Parkland formula estimates the volume of crystalloid (Lactated Ringer's) required in the first 24 hours post-burn by multiplying body weight (kg) by total body surface area burned (% TBSA) by 4 mL, with half of the volume administered in the first 8 hours from the time of injury and the other half over the next 16 hours.
translations.Desenvolvida no Parkland Memorial Hospit: Developed at Parkland Memorial Hospital, it is one of the most widely used fluid resuscitation formulas worldwide in the initial management of major burns, integrated into protocols of various burn societies.
translations.Amplamente utilizada e validada na práti: Widely used and validated in clinical practice for decades, although recent studies suggest that rigid application of the formula may lead to over-resuscitation ('fluid creep'), reinforcing the need for individualized adjustment according to clinical response (urine output, vital signs, perfusion).
translations.O valor calculado é uma estimativa inici: The calculated value is an initial estimate, not a fixed prescription — the infusion rate must be continuously adjusted according to hourly urine output and other clinical parameters. Overestimating volume can cause serious complications (abdominal compartment syndrome, pulmonary edema). The start time for counting the 8 hours is the time of injury, not the time of arrival at the healthcare facility.
translations.A contagem das 8 horas iniciais começa n: The count of the first 8 hours begins at the time of injury, not at the time of arrival at the healthcare facility — subtract the elapsed time when calculating the infusion rate.
translations.O volume calculado é apenas uma estimati: The calculated volume is only an initial estimate; the actual infusion rate must be continuously adjusted according to hourly urine output (target generally 0.5-1 mL/kg/h in adults).
translations.Evite a ressuscitação volêmica excessiva: Avoid excessive fluid resuscitation ('fluid creep'), associated with complications such as abdominal compartment syndrome and pulmonary edema.
translations.O fluido de escolha tradicional é o Ring: The traditional fluid of choice is Lactated Ringer's, but institutional protocols may vary.
translations.Qual é a fórmula de cálculo do volume de: What is the formula for calculating Parkland volume?
translations.A Fórmula de Parkland calcula o volume (: The Parkland formula calculates the volume (in mL) by multiplying body weight (kg) by total body surface area burned (%) by 4.
translations.Como o volume calculado deve ser adminis: How should the calculated volume be administered over 24 hours?
translations.Todo de uma vez, no início: All at once at the beginning
translations.Metade nas primeiras 8 horas (da hora da: Half in the first 8 hours (from time of injury) and half in the next 16 hours
translations.Metade do volume total é administrada na: Half of the total volume is administered in the first 8 hours counted from the time of injury, and the other half in the remaining 16 hours of the 24-hour period.
translations.O volume calculado pela Fórmula de Parkl: Is the volume calculated by the Parkland formula a fixed prescription?
translations.Sim, deve ser seguido rigidamente: Yes, it must be strictly followed
translations.Não, é uma estimativa inicial que deve s: No, it is an initial estimate that must be adjusted according to clinical response and urine output
translations.Não tem relação com a diurese: It has no relation to urine output
translations.O volume calculado é apenas uma estimati: The calculated volume is only an initial estimate; the actual infusion rate must be continuously adjusted according to hourly urine output and other clinical parameters, avoiding both under- and over-resuscitation.
translations.A partir de quando se conta o início das: From when is the start of the 8 hours counted in the Parkland formula?
translations.A partir da hora da lesão (do momento da: From the time of injury (the moment of the burn), not from the time of arrival at the healthcare facility. This means that if the patient arrives hours after the injury, the elapsed time must be subtracted when calculating the initial infusion rate.
translations.O volume calculado deve ser seguido rigi: Should the calculated volume be strictly followed throughout the entire resuscitation?
translations.Não. O valor é uma estimativa inicial de: No. The value is an initial reference estimate. The actual infusion rate must be continuously adjusted according to the patient's clinical response, especially hourly urine output, to avoid both under-resuscitation and fluid overload.
translations.Como funciona a Fórmula de Parkland: How does the Parkland Formula work?
translations.<p>A Fórmula de Parkland estima o volume:
The Parkland Formula estimates the crystalloid volume needed in the first 24 hours post-burn: Volume (mL) = Weight (kg) × TBSA (%) × 4.

Administration: half of the volume in the first 8 hours (counted from the time of injury) and the other half over the next 16 hours, always adjusted according to urine output and clinical response.

translations.Calcule o PEWS (Pediatric Early Warning: Calculate the PEWS (Pediatric Early Warning Score) to detect early clinical deterioration in pediatric patients. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Detectar precocemente sinais de deterior: Early detection of signs of clinical deterioration in hospitalized pediatric patients, through assessment of 3 domains: behavior, cardiovascular system, and respiratory system.
translations.Indicado para monitorização seriada de p: Indicated for serial monitoring of hospitalized pediatric patients (outside the ICU), to early identify signs of deterioration and guide the need for reassessment or escalation of care.
translations.Pacientes pediátricos hospitalizados, do: Hospitalized pediatric patients, from the neonatal period to adolescence.
translations.Nível de atividade e resposta comportame: Level of activity and behavioral response of the child.
translations.3 - Letárgico, confuso ou com resposta r: 3 - Lethargic, confused, or reduced response to pain
translations.Cor da pele, tempo de enchimento capilar: Skin color, capillary refill time, and heart rate.
translations.2 - Acinzentada, enchimento capilar de 4: 2 - Gray, capillary refill 4 seconds, or tachycardia 20 bpm above normal for age
translations.3 - Acinzentada e mosqueada, enchimento: 3 - Gray and mottled, capillary refill ≥5 seconds, or tachycardia ≥30 bpm above normal (or bradycardia) for age
translations.Frequência respiratória, uso de musculat: Respiratory rate, use of accessory muscles, and need for supplemental oxygen.
translations.0 - FR normal para idade, sem retrações,: 0 - RR normal for age, no retractions, no supplemental O2
translations.1 - FR >10 acima do normal, retrações, u: 1 - RR >10 above normal, retractions, use of O2 (FiO2 up to 30% or ≥3 L/min)
translations.2 - FR >20 acima do normal, batimento de: 2 - RR >20 above normal, nasal flaring and retractions, use of O2 (FiO2 up to 40% or 6 L/min)
translations.3 - FR abaixo do normal com retrações e: 3 - RR below normal with retractions and grunting, or use of O2 >50%/>8L/min, or apnea
translations.Pontuação de 0 a 2 pontos, sem indicação: Score 0 to 2 points, no additional notification indicated.
translations.• Reavaliar a cada 4 horas conforme roti:
• Reassess every 4 hours per routine
• Maintain standard monitoring
translations.Pontuação total de 3 pontos, ou qualquer: Total score of 3 points, or any single parameter scoring 2 or more.
translations.• Reavaliar a cada 3 horas até a pontuaç:
• Reassess every 3 hours until score reduces to ≤2
• Bedside assessment by team within 30 minutes
translations.• Enfermagem notifica o médico residente:
• Nursing notifies resident/attending team
• Reassess every 2 hours until score reduces to ≤2
• Bedside assessment within 30 minutes
translations.Alto Risco - Considerar Equipe de Respos: High Risk - Consider Rapid Response Team
translations.• Considerar acionamento da equipe de re:
• Consider activating rapid response team (e.g., MET)
• Notify senior resident, who communicates with attending physician
• Bedside assessment within 15 minutes
• Reassess every 2 hours until score reduces to ≤2
Total score of 6 points or more.
translations.• Acionar imediatamente a equipe de resp:
• Immediately activate institutional rapid response team
• Notify senior resident and attending physician immediately
• Reassess every 1 hour until score reduces to ≤2
translations.Inspiração e/ou expiração que não propor: Inspiration and/or expiration that does not provide adequate ventilation, identified by the respiratory domain of PEWS.
translations.Quantidade de sangue bombeada pelo coraç: Amount of blood pumped by the heart inadequate to meet the body's metabolic demands, identified by the cardiovascular domain of PEWS.
translations.Início súbito de distúrbios reversíveis: Sudden onset of reversible disturbances in consciousness, attention, cognition, and perception, identified by the behavioral domain of PEWS.
translations.PEWS avaliado sistematicamente conforme: PEWS assessed systematically per institutional protocol
translations.Aplicar o PEWS de forma seriada conforme: Apply PEWS serially per institutional protocol
translations.Somar a pontuação dos 3 domínios (compor: Sum the scores of the 3 domains (behavior, cardiovascular, respiratory) to obtain the total score
translations.Escalonar a frequência de reavaliação e: Escalate the frequency of reassessment and team notification according to the obtained score
translations.Considerar acionamento da equipe de resp: Consider activating the rapid response team for scores ≥5, especially ≥6
translations.Registrar o escore, a hora da avaliação: Record the score, assessment time, and actions taken in the medical record
translations.O PEWS avalia 3 domínios — comportamento: PEWS assesses 3 domains — behavior, cardiovascular, and respiratory — each scored from 0 to 3, totaling a score from 0 to 9 points, for early detection of clinical deterioration in hospitalized pediatric patients.
translations.Desenvolvido a partir dos escores de ale: Developed from Early Warning Scores used in adults, PEWS was adapted for the pediatric population considering normal heart and respiratory rates by age group, being adopted by various pediatric services and children's hospitals.
translations.Amplamente utilizado como ferramenta de: Widely used as a screening tool for early detection of clinical deterioration in pediatric wards, aiding in the decision for reassessment and escalation of care, including activation of rapid response teams.
translations.Existem múltiplas versões do PEWS (ex: B: There are multiple versions of PEWS (e.g., Brighton, CHEWS, CHAM-PEWS), with small variations in parameters and cutoff points — it is important to follow the version adopted by the institution. A recent study indicated that PEWS has limited ability to predict ICU transfers before a rapid response.
translations.O PEWS avalia 3 domínios: comportamento,: PEWS assesses 3 domains: behavior, cardiovascular, and respiratory, each scored from 0 to 3.
translations.Um único parâmetro pontuando 2 ou mais j: A single parameter scoring 2 or more already justifies more frequent reassessment, even if the total does not reach the general threshold.
translations.Existem diferentes versões do PEWS (Brig: There are different versions of PEWS (Brighton, CHEWS, CHAM-PEWS, among others) — always follow the specific protocol adopted by the institution.
translations.Escores ≥5-6 geralmente indicam necessid: Scores ≥5-6 generally indicate the need to activate the pediatric rapid response team.
translations.Quais os 3 domínios avaliados pelo PEWS?: What are the 3 domains assessed by PEWS?
translations.Temperatura, pressão arterial e diurese: Temperature, blood pressure, and urine output
translations.O PEWS avalia 3 domínios: comportamento,: PEWS assesses 3 domains: behavior, cardiovascular system, and respiratory system, each scored from 0 to 3.
translations.Qual a pontuação máxima possível no PEWS: What is the maximum possible score on PEWS (in this version)?
translations.O PEWS soma 3 domínios pontuados de 0 a: PEWS sums 3 domains scored from 0 to 3 each, totaling a maximum score of 9 points.
translations.O que deve ser feito se um único parâmet: What should be done if a single PEWS parameter scores 2 or more, even with a low total score?
translations.Justifica reavaliação mais frequente e a: It justifies more frequent reassessment and bedside evaluation
translations.Reduz automaticamente o escore total: It automatically reduces the total score
translations.Só é relevante em pacientes de UTI: It is only relevant in ICU patients
translations.Um parâmetro isolado pontuando 2 ou mais: An isolated parameter scoring 2 or more already justifies more frequent reassessment and bedside evaluation by the team, even if the total score does not reach the general threshold.
translations.O PEWS é padronizado internacionalmente,: Is PEWS internationally standardized, with a single version?
translations.Não. Existem diferentes versões do PEWS: No. There are different versions of PEWS (Brighton, CHEWS, CHAM-PEWS, among others), with small variations in the assessed parameters and cutoff points. It is essential to follow the specific version and protocol adopted by the healthcare institution.
translations.O PEWS substitui o julgamento clínico da: Does PEWS replace the clinical judgment of the nursing team?
translations.Não. O PEWS é uma ferramenta complementa: No. PEWS is a complementary screening tool. Concerns from the family or the team about the child's clinical status should always be valued and may justify escalation of care, regardless of the numerical score.
translations.<p>O PEWS soma a pontuação (0 a 3) de 3:
The PEWS sums the score (0 to 3) from 3 domains — behavior, cardiovascular, and respiratory — totaling 0 to 9 points.

Quick guide: 0-2 = No alert | 3 = Frequent reassessment | 4 = Notify team | 5 = Consider rapid response | 6-9 = Activate rapid response immediately.

translations.• Observação clínica ativa\n• Reavaliar:

translations.Sintomas depressivos moderados, com poss: Moderate depressive symptoms, with possible indication for therapeutic intervention.
translations.Sintomas depressivos moderadamente grave: Moderately severe depressive symptoms, with likely significant functional impact.
translations.• Encaminhamento prioritário para avalia:
• Priority referral for psychiatric/mental health evaluation

translations.Sintomas depressivos graves, com alto im: Severe depressive symptoms, with high functional impact and need for immediate intervention.
translations.• Encaminhamento urgente para avaliação:

translations.Suscetibilidade a lesão autoinfligida, c: Susceptibility to self-inflicted injury with intent to die, which may be fatal.

translations.Orientar sobre rede de apoio e recursos: Provide guidance on support network and available resources
translations.O PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire-9): The PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire-9) was developed by Spitzer, Kroenke, and Williams as the depression module of the PRIME-MD instrument, covering the 9 diagnostic criteria for major depressive episode according to the DSM, allowing both screening and monitoring of symptom severity over time.
translations.Amplamente utilizado mundialmente na ate: Widely used worldwide in primary care and mental health services as a screening and treatment response monitoring tool, being in the public domain and free for clinical, educational, and research use.
translations.Extensivamente validado em diversas popu: Extensively validated in diverse populations and languages, including Brazilian Portuguese, with good sensitivity and specificity for identifying major depressive episode.
translations.É um instrumento de rastreio, não substi: It is a screening instrument, not replacing formal clinical diagnosis, which must be performed by a qualified professional. Any score on item 9, even minimal, requires immediate clinical evaluation of suicide risk.
translations.Qualquer resposta positiva no item 9 (id: Any positive response on item 9 (thoughts of death/self-harm), even 'several days', requires immediate clinical evaluation of suicide risk.
translations.O PHQ-9 é útil tanto para rastreio inici: The PHQ-9 is useful both for initial screening and for monitoring treatment response over time.
translations.Quantos itens compõem o PHQ-9?: How many items make up the PHQ-9?
translations.O PHQ-9 possui 9 itens, correspondentes: The PHQ-9 has 9 items, corresponding to the 9 diagnostic criteria for major depressive episode according to the DSM.
translations.Qual conduta é indicada diante de qualqu: What action is indicated for any positive score on item 9?
translations.Ignorar se o escore total for baixo: Ignore if total score is low
translations.Avaliação clínica imediata do risco de s: Immediate clinical evaluation of suicide risk
translations.Aguardar a próxima consulta agendada: Wait for the next scheduled appointment
translations.Qualquer resposta positiva no item 9, qu: Any positive response on item 9, which addresses thoughts of death/self-harm, requires immediate clinical evaluation of suicide risk, regardless of total score.
translations.Um escore de 12 pontos no PHQ-9 é classi: A score of 12 on the PHQ-9 is classified as which severity?
translations.Escores entre 10 e 14 pontos são classif: Scores between 10 and 14 are classified as moderate depressive symptoms.
translations.O PHQ-9 é um instrumento de diagnóstico: Is the PHQ-9 a diagnostic instrument for depression?
translations.Não. É um instrumento de rastreio e acom: No. It is a screening and monitoring instrument for depressive symptom severity. Formal diagnosis must be made by a qualified mental health professional based on a complete clinical evaluation.
translations.O que fazer se o paciente pontuar no ite: What to do if the patient scores on item 9 (thoughts of death/self-harm)?
translations.Qualquer pontuação nesse item, mesmo mín: Any score on this item, even minimal, should prompt immediate clinical evaluation of suicide risk, following the institution's safety protocol, with referral for specialized mental health evaluation.
translations.<p>O PHQ-9 soma pontos de 9 itens relaci:
The PHQ-9 sums points from 9 items related to the diagnostic criteria for major depressive episode, each scored from 0 (not at all) to 3 (nearly every day), referring to the past 2 weeks, totaling 0 to 27 points.

Quick guide: 0-4 = Minimal | 5-9 = Mild | 10-14 = Moderate | 15-19 = Moderately severe | 20-27 = Severe.

This questionnaire sensitively addresses the topic of mental health. If you or someone you know is experiencing emotional difficulties, it is important to seek support from a mental health professional.

translations.Princípios do SUS - Sistema Único de Saú: Principles of SUS - Unified Health System | Nursing Calculators
Principles of SUS - Unified Health System | Nursing Calculators - Nursing Calculators
Principles of SUS - Unified Health System
translations.Princípios Filosóficos e Organizacionais: Philosophical and Organizational Principles of SUS
translations.Breve Infográfico dos Princípios do Sist: Brief Infographic of the Principles of the Unified Health System
translations.Art. 2º A saúde é um direito fundamental: Art. 2 Health is a fundamental human right, and the State must provide the indispensable conditions for its full exercise.
translations.§ 1º O dever do Estado de garantir a saú: § 1 The duty of the State to guarantee health consists of formulating and implementing economic and social policies aimed at reducing the risk of diseases and other health problems and establishing conditions that ensure universal and equal access to actions and services for its promotion, protection, and recovery.
translations.§ 2º O dever do Estado não exclui o das: § 2 The duty of the State does not exclude that of individuals, families, companies, and society.
translations.Um breve infográfico sobre os fundamento: A brief infographic on the foundations of the Brazilian Unified Health System, based on Law 8.080/1990
translations.A saúde é um direito de todos e um dever: Health is a right of all and a duty of the State. The principle of Universality ensures that any citizen, regardless of their social, economic, or geographic condition, has access to health actions and services at all levels of care. No one can be excluded from the system. Legal basis: Art. 7, item I of Law 8.080/1990.
translations.O princípio da Integralidade compreende: The principle of Comprehensiveness understands the human being as a whole, ensuring integrated actions for health promotion, protection, and recovery. It means that SUS must offer an articulated and continuous set of preventive and curative actions and services, individual and collective, at all levels of complexity. Legal basis: Art. 7, item II of Law 8.080/1990.
translations.O princípio da Equidade busca reduzir as: The principle of Equity seeks to reduce inequalities, ensuring that each person receives treatment according to their need. It is not about treating everyone equally, but recognizing differences and prioritizing those who need it most, promoting social justice in access to health services. Legal basis: Art. 7, item IV of Law 8.080/1990 (equality of health care, without prejudice or privilege).
translations.A população tem o direito e o dever de p: The population has the right and duty to participate in the management of SUS through Health Councils and Health Conferences. This principle ensures social control over public health policies, ensuring that decisions are made transparently and with the active participation of civil society. Legal basis: Art. 7, item VIII and Law 8.142/1990.
translations.A Regionalização organiza os serviços de: Regionalization organizes health services into regions to ensure adequate care for the population's needs. Health actions and services are organized in a regionalized and hierarchical manner at increasing levels of complexity, allowing integration between municipalities, states, and the Union to optimize resources and ensure access. Legal basis: Art. 8 of Law 8.080/1990.
translations.A Descentralização político-administrati: Political-administrative Decentralization redistributes responsibilities among the three spheres of government (Union, States, and Municipalities). With a single direction in each sphere, there is an emphasis on decentralization of services to municipalities, which are closer to the population and can better meet local needs. Legal basis: Art. 7, item IX of Law 8.080/1990.
translations.O SUS é organizado em níveis de complexi: The SUS is organized into increasing levels of complexity: Primary Care (Basic Health Units), Secondary Care (specialized outpatient services), and Tertiary Care (high-complexity hospitals). Hierarchization ensures that the patient is treated at the most appropriate level for their needs, with orderly referral between services through healthcare regulation. Legal basis: Article 8 of Law 8.080/1990.

translations.O PRISM (Pediatric Risk of Mortality) é: The PRISM (Pediatric Risk of Mortality) is a severity assessment scale used in pediatric intensive care units (PICU) to estimate the risk of mortality in hospitalized children. Developed to assist in assessing disease severity and continuous monitoring of intensive care quality, PRISM uses routinely collected physiological data to calculate a score reflecting the child's mortality risk.
translations.Esta é uma versão\nsimplificada para fin: This is a simplified version for educational purposes and does not replace the full application of the PRISM scale or clinical judgment.

translations.A Calculadoras de Enfermagem Global Plat: The Nursing Calculators Global Platform values your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data in compliance with the Brazilian General Data Protection Law (LGPD – Law No. 13,709/2018) and other applicable legislation.
translations.Coletamos informações que você nos forne: We collect information you provide directly, such as:
translations.Nome, e-mail e dados de contato ao criar: Name, email, and contact information when creating an account;
translations.Dados de uso da plataforma, como ferrame: Platform usage data, such as tools used and preferences;
translations.Informações de dispositivos e navegador,: Device and browser information through cookies and similar technologies.
translations.Fornecer, manter e melhorar a plataforma: Provide, maintain, and improve the platform and its features;
translations.Comunicar atualizações, conteúdos e novi: Communicate updates, content, and news (with your consent);
translations.Cumprir obrigações legais e regulatórias: Comply with legal and regulatory obligations.
translations.Não vendemos ou alugamos seus dados pess: We do not sell or rent your personal data. We may share information with:
translations.Prestadores de serviços que nos auxiliam: Service providers who assist us in operating the platform;
translations.Autoridades competentes, quando exigido: Competent authorities, when required by law;
translations.Parceiros estratégicos, sempre com seu c: Strategic partners, always with your consent.
translations.Acessar, corrigir ou excluir seus dados: Access, correct, or delete your personal data;
translations.Obter informações sobre o tratamento de: Obtain information about the processing of your data.
translations.Para exercer seus direitos, entre em con: To exercise your rights, contact us via email:
translations.Adotamos medidas técnicas e organizacion: We adopt appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or improper disclosure.
translations.Esta Política de Privacidade pode ser at: This Privacy Policy may be updated periodically. We will post changes on this page and, when applicable, send notifications.
translations.Utilizamos cookies e tecnologias similar: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience, remember preferences, and analyze platform usage. You can manage your cookie preferences at any time.
translations.Cookies são pequenos arquivos de texto a: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit the platform, used to recognize your browser and remember information about your visit.
translations.Necessários para o funcionamento básico: Necessary for the basic functioning of the platform, such as login and security.
translations.Ajudam a entender como os visitantes usa: Help understand how visitors use the platform to continuously improve it.
translations.Lembram preferências como idioma, região: Remember preferences such as language, region, and font size.
translations.Usados de forma agregada para entender t: Used in aggregate form to understand usage trends, always with consent.
translations.Você pode aceitar, recusar ou personaliz: You can accept, refuse, or customize the use of non-essential cookies at any time in your account or browser privacy settings, or through the floating cookie button available on all pages.
translations.Ao acessar e utilizar a Calculadoras de: By accessing and using the Nursing Calculators Global Platform, you agree to the terms and conditions described below.
translations.O uso da plataforma implica a aceitação: Use of the platform implies full acceptance of these Terms of Use and our Privacy Policy.
translations.A plataforma deve ser utilizada exclusiv: The platform must be used exclusively for educational, care, and management purposes in nursing, in a lawful and ethical manner.
translations.Você é responsável por manter a confiden: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account access credentials and for all activities carried out under your account.
translations.Todo o conteúdo da plataforma — textos,: All content on the platform—texts, calculators, educational materials, and branding—is protected by copyright and may not be reproduced without authorization.
translations.A plataforma é uma ferramenta de apoio e: The platform is a support tool and does not replace professional clinical judgment. See the Disclaimer for more details.
translations.Podemos atualizar estes Termos periodica: We may update these Terms periodically. Continued use of the platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
translations.Entenda os limites de responsabilidade s: Understand the limits of liability regarding the use of information and tools available on the platform.
translations.As calculadoras, escalas e conteúdos dis: The calculators, scales, and content provided are for educational purposes and to support professional practice. They do not replace clinical judgment, individual patient assessment, or current institutional protocols.
translations.O uso das informações da plataforma é de: Use of the platform's information is the sole responsibility of the professional, who must validate calculations and recommendations according to their experience and their institution's guidelines.
translations.A plataforma pode conter links para site: The platform may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the content, accuracy, or privacy practices of those sites.
translations.Empregamos esforços para manter a plataf: We make efforts to keep the platform available and functional, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation.
translations.Utilizamos cookies para melhorar sua exp: We use cookies to improve your experience, remember preferences, and analyze platform usage.
translations.Confira as regras para o uso da platafor: Check the rules for using the platform, rights, duties, and access conditions.
translations.Entenda os limites de responsabilidade s: Understand the limits of liability regarding the use of available information and tools.
translations.Se você tiver dúvidas sobre privacidade: If you have questions about privacy or data protection, please contact us.
translations.Protocolo de reanimação neonatal do Mini: Ministry of Health neonatal resuscitation protocol — step-by-step for the delivery room.
translations.Diretrizes do Ministério da Saúde para r: Ministry of Health guidelines for newborn resuscitation in the delivery room, based on the Apgar score and step-by-step clinical assessment.
translations.Avaliar 3 perguntas-chave imediatamente: Assess 3 key questions immediately after birth:
translations.Está respirando ou chorando?: Is the newborn breathing or crying?
If YES to all → routine care. If NO to any → initiate resuscitation steps.
translations.Secar e aquecer o recém-nascido com lenç: Dry and warm the newborn with a warm sheet
translations.Estimulação tátil nas plantas dos pés e: Tactile stimulation on the soles of the feet and back
translations.Aspirar vias aéreas apenas se obstruídas: Suction airway only if obstructed (avoid routine suctioning)
translations.está regular? Há esforço respiratório?: Is it regular? Is there respiratory effort?
translations.Indicada se: respiração ausente/gasping: Indicated if: absent breathing/gasping OR HR <100 bpm OR persistent central cyanosis.
translations.Máscara facial + balão autoinflável (21%: Face mask + self-inflating bag (21% O₂ for term; 30% for preterm)
translations.Avaliar em 30 segundos — se FC <60, pass: Assess in 30 seconds — if HR <60, proceed to Step 5
translations.Indicada se: FC <60 bpm após 30s de VPP: Indicated if: HR <60 bpm after 30s of effective PPV.
translations.Técnica: dois polegares (encirclamento): Technique: two thumbs (encircling) on the lower third of the sternum
translations.Indicada se: FC <60 bpm após 60s de comp: Indicated if: HR <60 bpm after 60s of compressions + PPV.
translations.Adrenalina 1:10.000 — 0,1-0,3 mL/kg IV (: Epinephrine 1:10,000 — 0.1-0.3 mL/kg IV (cord) or 0.5-1 mL/kg intratracheal
translations.Considerar expansão de volume (10 mL/kg): Consider volume expansion (10 mL/kg) if hypovolemia suspected
translations.Comunicar equipe e registrar todas as in: Communicate with team and record all interventions
translations.PUSH Tool - Escala de Cicatrização de Le: PUSH Tool - Pressure Ulcer Scale for Healing
translations.Calcule o PUSH Tool (Pressure Ulcer Scal: Calculate the PUSH Tool (Pressure Ulcer Scale for Healing) to monitor the healing progress of pressure injuries. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Monitorar objetivamente a evolução da ci: Objectively monitor the healing progress of pressure injuries over time, based on 3 parameters: wound area, amount of exudate, and type of tissue predominant in the wound bed.
translations.Indicada para reavaliação seriada de les: Indicated for serial reassessment of existing pressure injuries, allowing objective tracking of whether the wound is healing, stable, or worsening.
translations.Pacientes com lesão por pressão diagnost: Patients with diagnosed pressure injury, at any stage, in hospital, outpatient, or home care settings.
translations.Meça o maior comprimento e a maior largu: Measure the greatest length and greatest width of the wound (in cm) and multiply to obtain the area in cm².
translations.Quantidade de exsudato observada após a: Amount of exudate observed after dressing removal, before application of any topical product.
translations.Tipo de tecido predominante presente no: Predominant tissue type present in the wound bed.
translations.Tecido epitelial novo, róseo ou brilhant: New epithelial tissue, pink or shiny, covering part or all of the wound.
translations.Tecido róseo/vermelho brilhante, com asp: Pink/ bright red tissue, granular and moist appearance.
translations.Tecido amarelado ou esbranquiçado, aderi: Yellowish or whitish tissue, adherent or loose, fibrinous type.
translations.Tecido enegrecido, endurecido ou amoleci: Blackened tissue, hard or soft, adherent to the wound bed or edges.
translations.Lesão completamente fechada/epitelizada,: Wound completely closed/epithelialized, with no measurable area or exudate.
translations.• Manter proteção da pele recém-cicatriz:
• Maintain protection of newly healed skin
• Continue preventive measures for new injury
translations.Pontuação mais baixa dentro da faixa pos: Lower score within the possible range, usually associated with a wound in the healing process. PUSH should be interpreted by the trend over time (progressive decrease in score), not just by the isolated value.
translations.• Manter o plano de cuidados da ferida v:
• Maintain current wound care plan
• Reassess weekly (or per protocol) to monitor trend
• Document and compare with previous assessments
translations.Pontuação mais alta, indicando lesão mai: Higher score, indicating more extensive wound and/or less favorable tissue. It is essential to monitor the score trend in serial reassessments.
translations.• Reavaliar o plano terapêutico da ferid:
• Reassess wound therapeutic plan with the team
• Investigate factors that may be hindering healing (nutrition, perfusion, infection, persistent pressure)
• More frequent reassessment according to severity
translations.Dano ao tecido mucoso, córneo, subcutâne: Damage to mucous, corneal, subcutaneous, muscle, cartilage, bone, or vascular tissue.
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a ser invadido: Increased susceptibility to invasion by pathogenic organisms, related to skin discontinuity.
translations.Mensurar e documentar área, exsudato e t: Measure and document area, exudate, and tissue type at each assessment
translations.Realizar curativo conforme protocolo e c: Perform dressing according to protocol and wound characteristics
translations.Registrar a evolução do escore PUSH ao l: Record the evolution of the PUSH score over time
translations.Orientar sobre reposicionamento e alívio: Provide guidance on repositioning and pressure relief
translations.O PUSH Tool foi desenvolvido pelo Nation: The PUSH Tool was developed by the National Pressure Ulcer Advisory Panel (NPUAP) as a simple and standardized instrument to monitor the evolution of pressure injury healing over time, assessing area, exudate, and tissue type.
translations.Amplamente utilizado em serviços de esto: Widely used in wound and ostomy care services to objectively document the clinical evolution of pressure injuries, allowing standardized comparison between serial assessments.
translations.Estudos de validação demonstram boa sens: Validation studies demonstrate good sensitivity to detect changes over time, being a reliable tool for monitoring healing trends, although it does not replace a complete clinical wound assessment.
translations.O PUSH Tool foi desenhado para monitorar: The PUSH Tool was designed to monitor trends over time (progressive score reduction indicates healing), not to classify severity at a single isolated moment. It does not assess wound depth or undermining/tunneling.
translations.Reavalie sempre com a mesma técnica de m: Always reassess using the same measurement technique to ensure comparability between evaluations.
translations.O mais importante é a tendência do escor: The most important aspect is the score trend over time, not the isolated value of a single assessment.
translations.Registre fotografias padronizadas junto: Record standardized photographs along with the score for more complete documentation.
translations.Investigue causas de estagnação ou piora: Investigate causes of stagnation or worsening of the score (nutrition, perfusion, persistent pressure, infection).
translations.Quantos parâmetros compõem o PUSH Tool?: How many parameters make up the PUSH Tool?
translations.O PUSH avalia 3 parâmetros: área da lesã: The PUSH assesses 3 parameters: wound area, amount of exudate, and predominant tissue type.
translations.O que uma redução progressiva do escore: What does a progressive reduction in the PUSH score over reassessments indicate?
translations.O PUSH Tool foi desenhado para monitorar: The PUSH Tool was designed to monitor trends: a progressive reduction in the score over time indicates that the wound is evolving toward healing.
translations.O PUSH Tool avalia profundidade da lesão: Does the PUSH Tool assess wound depth?
translations.Sim, é o principal parâmetro: Yes, it is the main parameter
translations.Não, avalia área, exsudato e tipo de tec: No, it assesses area, exudate, and tissue type
translations.Sim, mas apenas em lesões fechadas: Yes, but only in closed wounds
translations.O PUSH Tool não avalia profundidade dire: The PUSH Tool does not directly assess depth; its 3 parameters are area, exudate, and wound bed tissue type.
translations.Com que frequência o PUSH deve ser reapl: How often should the PUSH be reapplied?
translations.Recomenda-se reavaliação semanal, ou con: Weekly reassessment is recommended, or according to institutional protocol, to monitor the healing trend over time.
translations.Um escore PUSH baixo sempre significa le: Does a low PUSH score always mean a small and simple wound?
translations.Não necessariamente isoladamente — o val: Not necessarily in isolation — the value should be interpreted together with the trend of previous assessments and the patient's overall clinical context.
translations.<p>O PUSH Tool soma pontos de 3 parâmetr:
The PUSH Tool sums points from 3 parameters — wound area (0-10 points), amount of exudate (0-3 points), and predominant tissue type (0-4 points) — totaling 0 to 17 points. The value should be tracked over serial assessments: a progressive reduction in the score indicates favorable healing evolution.

Quick guide: 0 = Healed wound | Lower scores = favorable trend | Higher scores = more extensive wound, requires closer monitoring.
translations.PUSH Tool - Escala de Cicatrização de Le: PUSH Tool - Pressure Injury Healing Scale | Nursing Calculators
translations.PUSH Tool: cálculo, fundamentação e evid: PUSH Tool: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Monitorar objetivamente a evolução da ci: Objectively monitor the evolution of pressure injury healing over time, based on 3 parameters: wound area, exudate amount, and predominant tissue type in the wound bed. Indicated for serial reassessment of existing pressure injuries, allowing objective tracking of whether the injury is healing, stable, or worsening.
translations.NATIONAL PRESSURE ULCER ADVISORY PANEL (: NATIONAL PRESSURE ULCER ADVISORY PANEL (NPUAP). Pressure Ulcer Scale for Healing (PUSH) Tool. Reston: NPUAP, 1998.
translations.THOMAS, D. R. et al. Pressure ulcer scal: THOMAS, D. R. et al. Pressure ulcer scale for healing: derivation and validation of the PUSH tool. Advances in Wound Care, v. 10, n. 5, p. 96-101, 1997.
translations.O PUSH Tool soma pontos de 3 parâmetros: The PUSH Tool sums points from 3 parameters — wound area (0-10 points), exudate amount (0-3 points), and predominant tissue type (0-4 points) — totaling 0 to 17 points. The value should be monitored over serial assessments: progressive reduction in score indicates favorable healing evolution.
translations.0 = Lesão cicatrizada | Escores mais bai: 0 = Healed wound | Lower scores = favorable trend | Higher scores = more extensive wound, requires closer monitoring.
translations.Calcule o escore qSOFA (quick SOFA) para: Calculate the qSOFA (quick SOFA) score for rapid bedside screening of patients with suspected sepsis. Includes interpretation, nursing care, and evidence.
translations.Realizar triagem rápida à beira do leito: Perform rapid bedside screening to identify patients with suspected infection at risk of unfavorable outcome (sepsis) outside the intensive care unit.
translations.Indicada em pronto-socorro, enfermaria e: Indicated in emergency departments, wards, and any non-ICU setting in adult patients with suspected infection.
translations.Pacientes adultos com suspeita ou confir: Adult patients with suspected or confirmed infectious process, treated outside the ICU.
translations.Escala de Coma de Glasgow menor que 15 (: Glasgow Coma Scale less than 15 (decreased level of consciousness).
translations.Glasgow igual a 15, sem alteração do est: GCS equal to 15, no altered mental status.
translations.Glasgow menor que 15, com alteração do e: GCS less than 15, with altered mental status.
translations.Frequência respiratória igual ou superio: Respiratory rate equal to or greater than 22 breaths per minute.
translations.Pressão arterial sistólica igual ou infe: Systolic blood pressure equal to or less than 100 mmHg.
translations.Baixo risco de desfecho desfavorável rel: Low risk of unfavorable sepsis-related outcome. Does not exclude infection, but the risk of poor prognosis is lower.
translations.• Manter vigilância clínica\n• Reavaliar:
• Maintain clinical surveillance
• Reassess if clinical worsening occurs
• Investigate and treat infectious focus per protocol
translations.qSOFA ≥ 2 está associado a maior risco d: qSOFA ≥ 2 is associated with higher mortality risk and prolonged length of stay in patients with suspected infection, suggesting possible sepsis.
translations.• Acionar protocolo de sepse institucion:
• Activate institutional sepsis protocol
• Collect lactate and blood cultures
• Initiate early antibiotic therapy per protocol
• Consider transfer to higher complexity unit
• Frequent vital sign reassessment
translations.Verificar estado mental e nível de consc: Check mental status and level of consciousness
translations.Comunicar imediatamente alterações à equ: Immediately report changes to medical team
translations.Administrar antibióticos conforme prescr: Administer antibiotics per prescription and protocol
translations.O qSOFA (quick Sequential Organ Failure: qSOFA (quick Sequential Organ Failure Assessment) was proposed in the Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3, 2016) as a simple bedside screening tool outside the ICU to identify patients with suspected infection at risk of unfavorable outcome.
translations.Desenvolvido como alternativa mais simpl: Developed as a simpler and faster alternative to the full SOFA, which requires laboratory tests. qSOFA uses only 3 clinical criteria, assessable without the need for complementary exams.
translations.Estudos multicêntricos demonstraram que: Multicenter studies have shown that qSOFA ≥ 2 is associated with higher hospital mortality risk and prolonged ICU stay in patients with suspected infection outside the ICU.
translations.O qSOFA tem sensibilidade moderada e não: qSOFA has moderate sensitivity and should not be used alone to rule out sepsis — a low score does not exclude the disease. It is a screening tool, not diagnostic, and should be complemented by clinical judgment and other institutional protocols (e.g., SIRS, serum lactate).
translations.O qSOFA não substitui a avaliação clínic: qSOFA does not replace complete clinical assessment nor institutional sepsis protocols.
translations.Um qSOFA baixo não descarta sepse — mant: A low qSOFA does not rule out sepsis — maintain vigilance if strong clinical suspicion of infection exists.
translations.Use em conjunto com o lactato sérico e c: Use in conjunction with serum lactate and SIRS criteria when available.
translations.Reavalie o escore a cada mudança no quad: Reassess the score with each change in the patient's clinical condition.
translations.Quantos critérios compõem o qSOFA?: How many criteria make up qSOFA?
translations.O qSOFA avalia 3 critérios: alteração do: qSOFA evaluates 3 criteria: altered mental status, respiratory rate ≥22 breaths/min, and systolic blood pressure ≤100 mmHg.
translations.A partir de qual pontuação o qSOFA indic: From which score does qSOFA indicate high risk?
translations.qSOFA ≥ 2 pontos está associado a maior: qSOFA ≥2 points is associated with higher risk of poor outcome in patients with suspected infection.
translations.O qSOFA exige exames laboratoriais para: Does qSOFA require laboratory tests to be calculated?
translations.Não, usa apenas critérios clínicos: No, it uses only clinical criteria
translations.O qSOFA foi desenhado para ser calculado: qSOFA was designed to be quickly calculated at the bedside, using only clinical criteria (mental status, RR, and BP), without the need for laboratory tests.
translations.Não. O qSOFA é uma ferramenta de triagem: No. qSOFA is a rapid screening tool that identifies patients with suspected infection at higher risk of unfavorable outcome. The diagnosis of sepsis requires a complete clinical assessment and specific criteria (Sepsis-3).
translations.Qual a diferença entre qSOFA e SOFA comp: What is the difference between qSOFA and full SOFA?
translations.O SOFA completo avalia disfunção orgânic: Full SOFA assesses organ dysfunction through 6 systems using laboratory tests, used in the ICU setting. qSOFA is a simplified version with only 3 clinical criteria, intended for rapid screening outside the ICU.
translations.<p>O qSOFA (quick SOFA) avalia 3 critéri:
qSOFA (quick SOFA) evaluates 3 clinical criteria at the bedside: altered mental status, respiratory rate ≥22 breaths/min, and systolic blood pressure ≤100 mmHg. Each present criterion adds 1 point, totaling 0 to 3 points.

Quick guide: 0-1 = Low risk | 2-3 = High risk, activate sepsis protocol.
translations.qSOFA - Triagem Rápida de Sepse | Calcul: qSOFA - Rapid Sepsis Screening | Nursing Calculators
translations.Realizar triagem rápida à beira do leito: Perform rapid bedside screening to identify patients with suspected infection at risk of unfavorable outcome (sepsis), outside the intensive care setting. Indicated in emergency departments, wards, and any non-ICU environment, in adult patients with suspected infection.
translations.SINGER, M. et al. The Third Internationa: SINGER, M. et al. The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA, v. 315, n. 8, p. 801-810, 2016.
translations.SEYMOUR, C. W. et al. Assessment of Clin: SEYMOUR, C. W. et al. Assessment of Clinical Criteria for Sepsis: For the Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA, v. 315, n. 8, p. 762-774, 2016.
translations.ILAS - Instituto Latino Americano de Sep: ILAS - Latin American Sepsis Institute. Sepsis treatment and management protocol. São Paulo: ILAS, 2023.
translations.O qSOFA (quick SOFA) avalia 3 critérios: qSOFA (quick SOFA) evaluates 3 clinical criteria at the bedside: altered mental status, respiratory rate ≥22 breaths/min, and systolic blood pressure ≤100 mmHg. Each present criterion adds 1 point, totaling 0 to 3 points.
translations.0-1 = Baixo risco | 2-3 = Alto risco, ac: 0-1 = Low risk | 2-3 = High risk, activate sepsis protocol.
translations.Classifique o nível de sedação do pacien: Classify the patient's sedation level using the Ramsay Scale (1 to 6 points). Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence for nursing practice.
translations.Classificar o nível de sedação do pacien: Classify the patient's sedation level on a 6-point scale, guiding sedoanalgesia adjustment in the intensive care setting.
translations.Indicada para pacientes sedados em UTI,: Indicated for sedated patients in the ICU, especially those on mechanical ventilation, as one of the first standardized sedation scales.
translations.Pacientes adultos criticamente enfermos,: Critically ill adult patients admitted to the ICU under sedation.
translations.Observe o paciente e, se necessário, apl: Observe the patient and, if necessary, apply a loud auditory stimulus or light glabellar tap to classify the level.
translations.Paciente ansioso, agitado ou inquieto, s: Patient anxious, agitated, or restless, indicating undersedation.
translations.Paciente cooperativo, orientado e tranqu: Patient cooperative, oriented, and calm; considered ideal level when awake.
translations.Paciente responde apenas a comandos verb: Patient responds only to verbal commands.
translations.Resposta rápida ao leve toque glabelar o: Brisk response to light glabellar tap or loud auditory stimulus.
translations.Resposta lenta ao leve toque glabelar ou: Sluggish response to light glabellar tap or loud auditory stimulus.
translations.Nenhuma resposta ao toque glabelar ou a: No response to glabellar tap or loud auditory stimulus.
translations.Nível de sedação insuficiente. Paciente: Insufficient sedation level. Patient anxious, agitated, or restless, with risk of self-extubation and device removal.
translations.• Reavaliar causas de agitação (dor, hip:
• Reassess causes of agitation (pain, hypoxemia, delirium)
• Communicate with medical team for sedoanalgesia adjustment
• Ensure patient safety
translations.Nível de sedação considerado ideal para: Sedation level considered ideal for the awake patient: cooperative, oriented, and calm.
translations.• Manter conduta atual\n• Reavaliar conf:
• Maintain current management
• Reassess per sedation protocol
translations.Sedação leve a moderada, paciente respon: Light to moderate sedation; patient responds to commands or stimuli with brisk response.
translations.• Verificar meta de sedação prescrita\n•:
• Verify prescribed sedation target
• Perform daily sedation interruption if indicated
• Monitor vital signs
translations.Sedação profunda, com resposta lenta ou: Deep sedation with sluggish or no response to stimuli. Associated with longer mechanical ventilation time when prolonged without clinical indication.
translations.• Reavaliar necessidade clínica da sedaç:
• Reassess clinical need for deep sedation
• Consider sedation reduction per protocol
• Monitor vital signs and level of consciousness frequently
translations.Risco de deterioração dos sistemas corpo: Risk of deterioration of body systems due to prescribed or unavoidable musculoskeletal inactivity.
translations.Nível de consciência adequado à meta pre: Level of consciousness appropriate to prescribed target
translations.Avaliar o nível de sedação com escala va: Assess sedation level using a validated scale (Ramsay)
translations.Ajustar infusão de sedativos conforme me: Adjust sedative infusion according to prescribed target and protocol
translations.Realizar interrupção diária da sedação q: Perform daily sedation interruption when indicated
translations.A Escala de Ramsay foi descrita por Mich: The Ramsay Scale was described by Michael Ramsay and colleagues in 1974, being one of the first standardized scales for assessing sedation level in critically ill patients, classifying from 1 (agitated) to 6 (no response).
translations.Amplamente utilizada por décadas em UTIs: Widely used for decades in ICUs worldwide, preceding more modern scales such as RASS and SAS (Sedation-Agitation Scale), which offer greater granularity and better psychometric validation.
translations.Apresenta boa aplicabilidade clínica his: It has good historical clinical applicability, but later studies show lower interobserver reliability compared to more recent scales such as RASS, especially at intermediate levels.
translations.Possui menor validação psicométrica form: It has less formal psychometric validation compared to more modern scales (RASS, SAS). Many institutions have migrated to RASS due to its greater reliability and integration with delirium assessment (CAM-ICU).
translations.Aplique o estímulo na sequência correta:: Apply the stimulus in the correct sequence: observe first, then auditory stimulus, then light glabellar tap.
translations.Muitas instituições têm substituído o Ra: Many institutions have replaced Ramsay with RASS for greater reliability — check the local protocol.
translations.Reavalie o escore a cada mudança na infu: Reassess the score with each change in sedative infusion.
translations.Documente sempre o escore junto ao horár: Always document the score along with the assessment time and the prescribed sedation target.
translations.Qual a faixa de valores da Escala de Ram: What is the range of values for the Ramsay Scale?
translations.A Escala de Ramsay varia de 1 (agitado): The Ramsay Scale ranges from 1 (agitated) to 6 (no response to any stimulus).
translations.Qual nível de Ramsay é considerado o est: Which Ramsay level is considered the ideal state for the awake patient?
translations.O nível 2 (cooperativo, orientado e tran: Level 2 (cooperative, oriented, and tranquil) is considered the ideal state for the awake patient.
translations.Qual escala mais moderna tem substituído: Which more modern scale has replaced Ramsay in many ICUs?
translations.A RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scal: The RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale) has greater psychometric validation and is now preferred in many sedation protocols.
translations.A Escala de Ramsay ainda é utilizada nas: Is the Ramsay Scale still used in ICUs?
translations.Sim, embora muitas instituições tenham m: Yes, although many institutions have migrated to RASS, which has greater validation and integrates with delirium assessment (CAM-ICU). The choice of scale follows institutional protocol.
translations.Com que frequência a Escala de Ramsay de: How often should the Ramsay Scale be reassessed?
translations.Recomenda-se reavaliação a cada turno, a: Reassessment is recommended every shift, with each adjustment in sedative infusion, and whenever there is a change in the patient's clinical condition.
translations.<p>A Escala de Ramsay classifica o nível:
The Ramsay Scale classifies the patient's sedation level into 6 levels, from 1 (anxious and agitated) to 6 (no response to any stimulus), with level 2 representing the ideal state for the awake patient (cooperative, oriented, and tranquil).

Quick guide: 1 = Under-sedation/agitation | 2 = Adequate sedation | 3-4 = Light to moderate sedation | 5-6 = Deep sedation.
