translations.Escala de Ramsay: cálculo, fundamentação: Ramsay Scale: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Classificar o nível de sedação do pacien: Classify the patient's sedation level on a 6-point scale, guiding sedoanalgesia adjustment in the intensive care setting. Indicated for sedated patients in the ICU, especially under mechanical ventilation, as one of the first standardized sedation scales.
translations.RAMSAY, M. A. et al. Controlled sedation: RAMSAY, M. A. et al. Controlled sedation with alphaxalone-alphadolone. British Medical Journal, v. 2, n. 5920, p. 656-659, 1974.
translations.SESSLER, C. N. et al. The Richmond Agita: SESSLER, C. N. et al. The Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale: validity and reliability in adult intensive care unit patients. American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine, v. 166, n. 10, p. 1338-1344, 2002.
translations.A Escala de Ramsay classifica o nível de: The Ramsay Scale classifies the patient's sedation level into 6 levels, from 1 (anxious and agitated) to 6 (no response to any stimulus), with level 2 representing the ideal state for the awake patient (cooperative, oriented, and tranquil).
translations.1 = Sub-sedação/agitação | 2 = Sedação a: 1 = Under-sedation/agitation | 2 = Adequate sedation | 3-4 = Light to moderate sedation | 5-6 = Deep sedation.
translations.Escala de Rancho Los Amigos - Níveis de: Rancho Los Amigos Scale - Levels of Cognitive Functioning
translations.Classifique o nível de funcionamento cog: Classify the cognitive functioning level of brain-injured patients using the Rancho Los Amigos Scale (LCFS). Includes interpretation, nursing diagnosis, and evidence.
translations.Classificar o nível de funcionamento cog: Classify the cognitive and behavioral functioning level of patients recovering from traumatic brain injury through 8 progressive levels.
translations.Indicada para pacientes com lesão cerebr: Indicated for patients with traumatic brain injury, especially during the rehabilitation phase, to guide the therapeutic plan and monitor cognitive evolution.
translations.Pacientes com traumatismo cranioencefáli: Patients with traumatic brain injury or other acquired brain injury, in acute or rehabilitation phase.
translations.Selecione o nível que melhor descreve o: Select the level that best describes the patient's behavior and response to stimuli.
translations.Paciente não responde a estímulos extern: Patient does not respond to external stimuli and appears asleep.
translations.Paciente reage a estímulos externos de f: Patient reacts to external stimuli in a nonspecific, inconsistent, and non-purposeful manner, with stereotyped and limited responses.
translations.Paciente responde de forma específica, m: Patient responds specifically but inconsistently and with delay to stimuli; may follow simple motor commands.
translations.Paciente exibe comportamentos bizarros,: Patient exhibits bizarre, non-purposeful, incoherent, or inappropriate behaviors; no recent memory; short and non-selective attention.
translations.Respostas aleatórias, fragmentadas e não: Random, fragmented, and non-purposeful responses to complex or unstructured stimuli; consistently follows simple commands; impaired memory and selective attention; does not retain new information.
translations.Respostas apropriadas ao contexto e dire: Contextually appropriate and goal-directed responses, dependent on external cueing; retention for relearned tasks but not for new tasks; recent memory problems persist.
translations.Comportamento apropriado em ambientes fa: Appropriate behavior in familiar environments, performs daily routines automatically, with retention of new learning at a slower than normal rate; initiates social interactions, but judgment remains impaired.
translations.Respostas corretas e orientadas ao ambie: Correct and environment-oriented responses; abstract reasoning still reduced compared to premorbid level, requiring minimal supervision.
translations.Níveis I e II, compatíveis com estado de: Levels I and II, consistent with coma or minimal and nonspecific response to stimuli.
translations.• Cuidados de suporte e prevenção de com:
• Supportive care and prevention of complications (positioning, skin integrity, airway)
• Structured sensory stimulation per rehabilitation team protocol
• Continuous neurological monitoring
translations.Níveis III e IV, com respostas inconsist: Levels III and IV, with inconsistent responses or agitated and incoherent behavior.
translations.• Garantir segurança do paciente (preven:
• Ensure patient safety (fall and self-injury prevention)
• Calm environment with controlled stimuli
• Involve multidisciplinary rehabilitation team
translations.Níveis V e VI, com respostas ainda depen: Levels V and VI, with responses still dependent on external cueing and impaired recent memory.
translations.• Estruturar rotina e fornecer orientaçã:
• Structure routine and provide frequent orientation
• Reinforce learning through repetition and routines
• Involve family in rehabilitation process
translations.Níveis VII e VIII, com comportamento apr: Levels VII and VIII, with appropriate behavior and progressive return to autonomy, still with limitations in judgment and abstract reasoning.
translations.• Progredir para reabilitação cognitiva:
• Progress to more complex cognitive rehabilitation and return to activities
• Work on judgment, planning, and abstract reasoning
• Plan transition to home/community environment with adequate support
translations.Início súbito ou deterioração progressiv: Sudden onset or progressive deterioration of reversible or irreversible disorders of consciousness, attention, cognition, and perception, identified by the Rancho Los Amigos level.
translations.Risco de deterioração de sistemas corpor: Risk of deterioration of body systems due to prescribed or unavoidable musculoskeletal inactivity, relevant at lower levels of the scale.
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a quedas, rela: Increased susceptibility to falls, related to cognitive and behavioral impairment identified at intermediate levels of the scale.
translations.Nível de Rancho Los Amigos avaliado e do: Rancho Los Amigos level assessed and documented regularly
translations.Plano de cuidados ajustado conforme o ní: Care plan adjusted according to identified cognitive level
translations.Classificar o nível de Rancho Los Amigos: Classify the Rancho Los Amigos level regularly during the rehabilitation phase
translations.Ajustar o tipo e a intensidade da estimu: Adjust the type and intensity of sensory stimulation according to the identified level
translations.Garantir segurança do paciente, especial: Ensure patient safety, especially at agitation levels (III-IV)
translations.Fornecer orientação e rotina estruturada: Provide orientation and structured routine at confusion levels (V-VI)
translations.Envolver a família no processo de reabil: Involve the family in the rehabilitation process and in understanding the patient's cognitive level
translations.A Escala de Rancho Los Amigos classifica: The Rancho Los Amigos Scale classifies the patient's cognitive and behavioral functioning into 8 progressive levels, from Level I (no response) to Level VIII (purposeful, appropriate), reflecting sequential stages of recovery after brain injury.
translations.Desenvolvida pela equipe do Rancho Los A: Developed by the staff of the Rancho Los Amigos National Rehabilitation Center (California, USA) in the 1970s, it became one of the most widely used tools to classify cognitive recovery after traumatic brain injury. A revised version with 10 levels was later published.
translations.Amplamente utilizada na prática clínica: Widely used in neurological rehabilitation clinical practice, with good acceptance among multidisciplinary teams, although its formal psychometric validation is more limited compared to other neurological scales.
translations.É uma escala categórica e descritiva, co: It is a categorical and descriptive scale, with some degree of subjectivity in level classification. The original 8-level version was later expanded to 10 levels (revised version), which better details the more advanced stages of recovery.
translations.A Escala de Rancho Los Amigos classifica: The Rancho Los Amigos Scale classifies cognitive level into 8 sequential stages, from Level I (no response) to Level VIII (purposeful, appropriate).
translations.É amplamente utilizada em unidades de re: It is widely used in rehabilitation units to guide the type of stimulation and communication appropriate for each recovery stage.
translations.Níveis IV e V frequentemente representam: Levels IV and V often represent behavioral management challenges, making it important to ensure a safe and controlled environment.
translations.Existe uma versão revisada com 10 níveis: There is a revised version with 10 levels, which more specifically details the advanced stages of recovery (Levels VIII to X).
translations.Quantos níveis compõem a versão original: How many levels make up the original version of the Rancho Los Amigos Scale?
translations.A versão original da Escala de Rancho Lo: The original version of the Rancho Los Amigos Scale has 8 levels, from Level I (no response) to Level VIII (purposeful, appropriate). A revised version with 10 levels was later published.
translations.O que caracteriza o Nível IV (Confuso, A: What characterizes Level IV (Confused, Agitated) of the scale?
translations.Ausência total de resposta a estímulos: Total absence of response to stimuli
translations.Comportamentos bizarros, não intencionai: Bizarre, non-purposeful, and incoherent behaviors, without recent memory
translations.Comportamento totalmente apropriado com: Completely appropriate behavior with intact abstract reasoning
translations.Resposta automática e apropriada em ambi: Automatic and appropriate response in familiar environments
translations.O Nível IV é caracterizado por comportam: Level IV is characterized by bizarre, non-purposeful, incoherent, or inappropriate behaviors, with absence of recent memory and short, non-selective attention.
translations.Para que serve principalmente a Escala d: What is the main purpose of the Rancho Los Amigos Scale in clinical practice?
translations.Classificar o funcionamento cognitivo e: Classify cognitive functioning and guide rehabilitation after brain injury
translations.A escala é utilizada para classificar o: The scale is used to classify the level of cognitive and behavioral functioning of patients with brain injury, guiding rehabilitation strategies.
translations.A Escala de Rancho Los Amigos substitui: Does the Rancho Los Amigos Scale replace the Glasgow Coma Scale?
translations.Não. São escalas complementares. A Glasg: No. They are complementary scales. Glasgow primarily assesses the level of consciousness in the acute phase, while Rancho Los Amigos classifies cognitive and behavioral functioning throughout recovery, being particularly useful in the rehabilitation phase.
translations.Existe uma versão com mais de 8 níveis?: Is there a version with more than 8 levels?
translations.Sim. Existe uma versão revisada da escal: Yes. There is a revised version of the scale with 10 levels, which more specifically details the more advanced stages of cognitive recovery (corresponding to the former Levels VII and VIII, now subdivided into more categories).
translations.Como funciona a Escala de Rancho Los Ami: How the Rancho Los Amigos Scale works
translations.<p>A Escala de Rancho Los Amigos classif:
The Rancho Los Amigos Scale classifies the patient's cognitive functioning into 8 sequential levels, from Level I (no response) to Level VIII (purposeful, appropriate).

Quick guide: Levels I-II = No response/generalized | III-IV = Localized/confused-agitated | V-VI = Confused inappropriate/appropriate | VII-VIII = Automatic/purposeful appropriate.
translations.Escala de Rancho Los Amigos - Níveis de: Rancho Los Amigos Scale - Levels of Cognitive Functioning | Nursing Calculators
translations.Cálculo rápido — Escala de Rancho Los Am: Quick calculation — Rancho Los Amigos Scale
translations.Escala de Rancho Los Amigos: cálculo, fu: Rancho Los Amigos Scale: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Classificar o nível de funcionamento cog: Classify the level of cognitive and behavioral functioning of patients recovering from traumatic brain injury, through 8 progressive levels. Indicated for patients with traumatic brain injury, especially during the rehabilitation phase, to guide the therapeutic plan and monitor cognitive evolution.
translations.NIC — Estimulação cognitiva e manejo amb: NIC — Cognitive stimulation and environmental management
translations.HAGEN, C.; MALKMUS, D.; DURHAM, P. Level: HAGEN, C.; MALKMUS, D.; DURHAM, P. Levels of Cognitive Functioning. Downey: Rancho Los Amigos National Rehabilitation Center, 1972.
translations.TRAUMATIC BRAIN INJURY MODEL SYSTEMS (TB: TRAUMATIC BRAIN INJURY MODEL SYSTEMS (TBIMS). Rancho Los Amigos Scale AKA Level of Cognitive Functioning Scale (LCFS). 1997.
translations.ASSOCIAÇÃO BRASILEIRA DE NEUROLOGIA. Dir: ASSOCIAÇÃO BRASILEIRA DE NEUROLOGIA. Diretrizes de reabilitação em traumatismo cranioencefálico. São Paulo: ABN, 2020.
translations.A Escala de Rancho Los Amigos classifica: The Rancho Los Amigos Scale classifies the patient's cognitive functioning into 8 sequential levels, from Level I (no response) to Level VIII (purposeful, appropriate).
translations.Níveis I-II = Sem resposta/generalizada: Levels I-II = No response/generalized | III-IV = Localized/confused-agitated | V-VI = Confused inappropriate/appropriate | VII-VIII = Automatic/purposeful appropriate.
translations.Critérios de Ranson - Prognóstico na Pan: Ranson Criteria - Prognosis in Acute Pancreatitis
translations.Calcule os Critérios de Ranson para esti: Calculate the Ranson Criteria to estimate severity and mortality of acute pancreatitis, considering admission and 48-hour criteria. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Estimar a gravidade e o risco de mortali: To estimate severity and mortality risk of acute pancreatitis based on clinical and laboratory criteria assessed at admission and within the first 48 hours.
translations.Indicada em pacientes com diagnóstico de: Indicated in patients with diagnosis of acute pancreatitis for early prognostic stratification.
translations.Pacientes adultos com pancreatite aguda,: Adult patients with acute pancreatitis, assessed at admission and reassessed after 48 hours.
White blood cell count at admission.
translations.Queda do hematócrito em relação ao valor: Decrease in hematocrit relative to admission value within 48 hours.
translations.Aumento da ureia sérica em relação ao va: Increase in serum urea nitrogen relative to admission value despite hydration within 48 hours.
translations.Pressão parcial de oxigênio arterial em: Arterial partial pressure of oxygen at 48 hours.
translations.Baixo número de critérios positivos, ass: Low number of positive criteria, associated with estimated mortality less than 5% and lower probability of severe course.
translations.• Manter suporte clínico padrão (hidrata:
• Maintain standard clinical support (hydration, analgesia, fasting per protocol)
• Reassess complete criteria at 48 hours if still in early phase
• Routine monitoring
translations.Número intermediário de critérios positi: Intermediate number of positive criteria, associated with estimated mortality around 15-20% and higher risk of complications.
translations.• Considerar monitorização em unidade de:
• Consider monitoring in intermediate/intensive care unit
• Frequent clinical and laboratory reassessment
• Investigate and treat complications early
translations.Número elevado de critérios positivos, a: High number of positive criteria, associated with estimated mortality around 40% and high risk of systemic complications.
translations.• Internação em terapia intensiva\n• Mon:
• Admission to intensive care unit
• Hemodynamic and target organ monitoring
• Multidisciplinary assessment for organ support as needed
translations.Número muito elevado de critérios positi: Very high number of positive criteria, associated with estimated mortality close to 100% in some historical series.
translations.• Cuidados intensivos com suporte avança:
• Intensive care with advanced multi-organ support
• Multidisciplinary discussion about prognosis and care goals with family
• Continuous reassessment of therapeutic response
translations.Diminuição do líquido intravascular, int: Decrease in intravascular, interstitial, and/or intracellular fluid, related to fluid sequestration and systemic inflammatory response in severe pancreatitis.
translations.Experiência sensorial e emocional desagr: Unpleasant sensory and emotional experience associated with actual or potential tissue damage, related to pancreatic inflammation.
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a ser invadido: Increased susceptibility to invasion by pathogenic organisms, related to pancreatic necrosis and possible secondary infection.
translations.Inspiração e/ou expiração que não propor: Inspiration and/or expiration that does not provide adequate ventilation, related to systemic complications of severe pancreatitis (e.g., acute respiratory distress syndrome).
translations.Critérios de Ranson avaliados na admissã: Ranson criteria assessed on admission and at 48 hours
translations.Sinais vitais e função de órgãos monitor: Vital signs and organ function monitored
translations.Ausência ou controle de complicações sis: Absence or control of systemic complications
translations.Coletar exames laboratoriais necessários: Collect laboratory tests necessary for calculating Ranson criteria on admission and at 48 hours
translations.Monitorar sinais vitais, diurese e funçã: Monitor vital signs, urine output, and respiratory function continuously
translations.Auxiliar na reposição volêmica conforme: Assist with volume replacement as prescribed
translations.Comunicar imediatamente resultado com cr: Immediately report results with elevated criteria to the medical team
translations.Avaliar necessidade de transferência par: Assess need for transfer to intensive care unit
translations.Os Critérios de Ranson avaliam 11 parâme: Ranson criteria evaluate 11 clinical and laboratory parameters: 5 on admission (age, leukocytes, glucose, LDH, and AST) and 6 at 48 hours (hematocrit drop, BUN increase, calcium, PaO2, base deficit, and fluid sequestration), summing the number of positive criteria to estimate severity and mortality of acute pancreatitis.
translations.Desenvolvidos por John Ranson em 1974, o: Developed by John Ranson in 1974, originally for alcoholic pancreatitis (with a slightly different set of criteria for biliary pancreatitis), they were one of the first objective prognostic systems for acute pancreatitis.
translations.Amplamente utilizados historicamente, em: Widely used historically, although newer systems such as APACHE II and BISAP have the advantage of being fully calculable on admission without needing to wait 48 hours.
translations.Requer aguardar 48 horas para o cálculo: Requires waiting 48 hours for complete calculation, delaying full risk stratification. Some laboratory parameters (base deficit, fluid sequestration) may not be routinely available in all services. More modern scales (BISAP, APACHE II) are fully calculable on admission.
translations.Os Critérios de Ranson exigem duas avali: Ranson criteria require two assessments: one on admission (5 criteria) and another at 48 hours (6 additional criteria).
translations.O escore completo só está disponível apó: The complete score is only available after 48 hours, which is a limitation compared to scales like BISAP and APACHE II.
translations.Cada critério positivo soma 1 ponto; o r: Each positive criterion adds 1 point; the result is the total number of positive criteria, not a sum of numerical values.
translations.Considere associar os Critérios de Ranso: Consider associating Ranson criteria with other prognostic scores (BISAP, APACHE II) for a more complete and early assessment.
translations.Quantos critérios compõem o escore de Ra: How many criteria make up the Ranson score?
translations.O escore de Ranson é composto por 11 cri: The Ranson score consists of 11 criteria: 5 assessed on admission and 6 assessed at 48 hours.
translations.Quando o escore de Ranson completo pode: When can the complete Ranson score be calculated?
translations.O escore completo requer critérios avali: The complete score requires criteria assessed on admission and criteria assessed at 48 hours, so it is only fully available after that period.
translations.Qual a principal limitação prática dos C: What is the main practical limitation of Ranson criteria compared to scales like BISAP?
translations.Necessidade de aguardar 48 horas para o: Need to wait 48 hours for the complete score
translations.Diferente do BISAP e do APACHE II, que p: Unlike BISAP and APACHE II, which can be fully calculated on admission, Ranson criteria require a second assessment at 48 hours for the complete score.
translations.Os Critérios de Ranson são específicos p: Are Ranson criteria specific to any type of pancreatitis?
translations.Foram originalmente descritos para pancr: They were originally described for alcoholic pancreatitis. There is a variation of the criteria for biliary pancreatitis, with slight differences in cutoff values.
translations.Por que preciso esperar 48 horas para o: Why do I need to wait 48 hours for the full score?
translations.Seis dos onze critérios (queda do hemató: Six of the eleven criteria (hematocrit drop, increase in BUN, calcium, PaO2, base deficit, and fluid sequestration) can only be assessed by comparing with admission values after 48 hours of clinical evolution.
translations.<p>Os Critérios de Ranson somam o número:
Ranson Criteria sum the number of positive criteria among 11 parameters: 5 assessed on admission (age, leukocytes, glucose, LDH, AST) and 6 assessed at 48 hours (hematocrit drop, increase in BUN, calcium, PaO2, base deficit, fluid sequestration).

Quick guide: 0-2 = Mild | 3-4 = Moderate | 5-6 = Severe | ≥7 = Extremely severe.

translations.Critérios de Ranson: cálculo, fundamenta: Ranson Criteria: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Estimar a gravidade e o risco de mortali: Estimate the severity and mortality risk of acute pancreatitis based on clinical and laboratory criteria assessed on admission and within the first 48 hours. Indicated in patients diagnosed with acute pancreatitis for early prognostic stratification.
translations.RANSON, J. H. et al. Prognostic signs an: RANSON, J. H. et al. Prognostic signs and the role of operative management in acute pancreatitis. Surgery, Gynecology & Obstetrics, v. 139, n. 1, p. 69-81, 1974.
translations.BANKS, P. A. et al. Classification of ac: BANKS, P. A. et al. Classification of acute pancreatitis—2012: revision of the Atlanta classification and definitions by international consensus. Gut, v. 62, n. 1, p. 102-111, 2013.
translations.Os Critérios de Ranson somam o número de: Ranson Criteria sum the number of positive criteria among 11 parameters: 5 assessed on admission (age, leukocytes, glucose, LDH, AST) and 6 assessed at 48 hours (hematocrit drop, increase in BUN, calcium, PaO2, base deficit, fluid sequestration).
translations.Classifique o nível de sedação ou agitaç: Classify the level of sedation or agitation of the critically ill patient using the Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale (RASS). Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Classificar objetivamente o nível de sed: Objectively classify the level of sedation or agitation of the critically ill patient, guiding sedation adjustment and screening for delirium assessment (CAM-ICU).
translations.Indicada para pacientes em unidade de te: Indicated for patients in the intensive care unit, especially those on mechanical ventilation and/or receiving sedatives, for serial monitoring of consciousness level.
translations.Pacientes adultos criticamente enfermos,: Critically ill adult patients admitted to the ICU.
translations.Observe o paciente e, se necessário, est: Observe the patient and, if necessary, stimulate verbally and then physically to classify the level.
translations.Combativo, violento, representa perigo i: Combative, violent, immediate danger to staff.
translations.Ansioso, mas movimentos não são agressiv: Anxious but movements not aggressive or vigorous.
translations.Nível de consciência normal, alerta e ca: Normal level of consciousness, alert and calm.
translations.Não totalmente alerta, mas mantém contat: Not fully alert, but maintains eye contact to voice (>10s).
translations.Acorda brevemente ao chamado, com contat: Briefly awakens to voice, with eye contact (<10s).
translations.Movimento ou abertura ocular ao chamado,: Movement or eye opening to voice, but no eye contact.
translations.Sem resposta à voz, mas movimento ou abe: No response to voice, but movement or eye opening to physical stimulation.
translations.Sem resposta à voz ou ao estímulo físico: No response to voice or physical stimulation.
translations.Paciente agitado ou combativo, com risco: Agitated or combative patient, at risk of self-extubation, device removal, and aggression toward staff.
translations.• Reavaliar causas reversíveis de agitaç:
• Reassess reversible causes of agitation (pain, hypoxemia, delirium)
• Adjust sedation per medical prescription
• Assess delirium with CAM-ICU
• Ensure patient and staff safety
translations.Nível de consciência dentro do considera: Consciousness level considered ideal for most critically ill patients, common sedation target.
translations.• Manter conduta atual\n• Reavaliar peri:
• Maintain current management
• Reassess periodically per sedation protocol
translations.Sedação leve a moderada. Pode estar dent: Light to moderate sedation. May be within therapeutic target depending on clinical goal (e.g., light sedation protocol).
translations.• Verificar meta de sedação prescrita\n•:
• Check prescribed sedation target
• Perform daily sedation interruption if indicated
• Assess delirium with CAM-ICU when possible
translations.Sedação profunda, sem resposta ou respos: Deep sedation, unresponsive or only responsive to physical stimulation. Associated with longer mechanical ventilation and worse outcomes when prolonged without clinical indication.
translations.Avaliar o nível de sedação com escala va: Assess sedation level with validated scale (RASS)
translations.A escala RASS foi desenvolvida por Sessl: The RASS was developed by Sessler et al. (2002) to standardize assessment of sedation and agitation levels in critically ill patients, ranging from -5 (unarousable) to +4 (combative), with 0 representing an alert and calm patient.
translations.Amplamente adotada em protocolos de seda: Widely adopted in ICU sedation protocols worldwide, being a prerequisite for CAM-ICU (delirium assessment), which should only be performed in patients with RASS ≥ -3.
translations.Apresenta excelente confiabilidade inter: Excellent interrater reliability and construct validity, being one of the most studied sedation scales and recommended by international guidelines for pain, agitation, and delirium management (PADIS).
translations.Requer treinamento da equipe para aplica: Requires staff training for standardized application and frequent reassessment, especially in goal-directed light sedation protocols.
translations.Avalie primeiro observando o paciente, d: First assess by observing the patient, then stimulate verbally and only then physically, following the scale sequence.
translations.O CAM-ICU só deve ser aplicado se o RASS: The CAM-ICU should only be applied if the RASS is between -3 and +4.
translations.Reavalie o RASS a cada mudança na infusã: Reassess RASS with each change in sedative infusion and according to institutional protocol.
translations.Qual a faixa de valores da escala RASS?: What is the range of values for the RASS scale?
translations.A RASS varia de -5 (não desperta) a +4 (: RASS ranges from -5 (unarousable) to +4 (combative), with 0 representing alert and calm.
translations.A partir de qual valor de RASS o CAM-ICU: From which RASS value can the CAM-ICU be applied?
translations.O CAM-ICU só deve ser aplicado quando o: The CAM-ICU should only be applied when RASS is between -3 and +4, as deeper sedation levels prevent reliable delirium assessment.
translations.Um RASS de +3 indica qual estado do paci: A RASS of +3 indicates which patient state?
translations.RASS +3 corresponde a 'Muito Agitado' —: RASS +3 corresponds to 'Very Agitated' — the patient pulls or removes tubes and catheters, or is aggressive with staff.
translations.Com que frequência o RASS deve ser reava: How often should RASS be reassessed?
translations.Recomenda-se reavaliação a cada turno, a: Reassessment is recommended every shift, with each adjustment in sedative infusion, and whenever there is a change in the patient's clinical condition, according to the institutional sedation protocol.
translations.Não. O RASS avalia nível de sedação/agit: No. RASS assesses level of sedation/agitation. Delirium assessment is done with the CAM-ICU, which should only be applied when RASS is between -3 and +4.
translations.<p>A Escala RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sed:
The RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale) classifies the level of consciousness of critically ill patients on a 10-point scale, from -5 (unarousable to any stimulus) to +4 (combative), through 0 (alert and calm), which represents the usual sedation target in many protocols.

Quick guide: +1 to +4 = Agitation | 0 = Alert and calm | -1 to -3 = Light to moderate sedation | -4 to -5 = Deep sedation.

Indicated for patients in the intensive care unit, especially those on mechanical ventilation and/or receiving sedatives, for serial monitoring of consciousness level.
translations.A Escala RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedati: The RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale) classifies the level of consciousness of critically ill patients on a 10-point scale, from -5 (unarousable to any stimulus) to +4 (combative), through 0 (alert and calm), which represents the usual sedation target in many protocols.
translations.+1 a +4 = Agitação | 0 = Alerta e calmo: +1 to +4 = Agitation | 0 = Alert and calm | -1 to -3 = Light to moderate sedation | -4 to -5 = Deep sedation.
translations.Regra dos Nove - Estimativa de Superfíci: Rule of Nines - Burn Surface Area Estimation
translations.Calcule a superfície corporal queimada (: Calculate the burned body surface area (BBSA) in adults using Wallace's Rule of Nines. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Estimar rapidamente o percentual de supe: Quickly estimate the percentage of burned body surface area (BBSA) in adult patients, guiding burn severity and fluid resuscitation calculation.
translations.Indicada na avaliação inicial de pacient: Indicated in the initial assessment of adult burn victims to estimate the extent of the affected area.
translations.Pacientes adultos vítimas de queimaduras: Adult burn victims (the adult Rule of Nines should not be used in small children, who have different body proportions).
translations.Queimadura envolvendo toda a região da c: Burn involving the entire head and neck region.
translations.Queimadura envolvendo todo o membro supe: Burn involving the entire right upper limb.
Burn involving the entire left upper limb.
translations.Queimadura envolvendo toda a face anteri: Burn involving the entire anterior trunk (chest and abdomen).
translations.Queimadura envolvendo toda a face poster: Burn involving the entire posterior trunk (back).
translations.Queimadura envolvendo todo o membro infe: Burn involving the entire right lower limb.
Burn involving the entire left lower limb.
translations.Extensão pequena de superfície corporal: Small extent of burned body surface area. Actual severity also depends on depth, location (face, hands, genitalia, joints), and patient age.
translations.• Avaliar profundidade da queimadura e n:
• Assess burn depth and need for specialized referral
• Local wound care per protocol
• Analgesia as per medical evaluation
translations.Extensão moderada de superfície corporal: Moderate extent of burned body surface area, usually indicating need for fluid resuscitation and specialized evaluation.
translations.• Calcular reposição volêmica conforme p:
• Calculate fluid resuscitation per protocol (e.g., Parkland formula)
• Consider referral to burn center, especially if special areas affected
• Monitor vital signs and urine output
translations.Extensão grave de superfície corporal qu: Severe extent of burned body surface area, often indicating need for admission to a specialized burn center.
translations.• Encaminhamento para centro de queimado:
• Referral to burn center
• Aggressive fluid resuscitation per protocol
• Intensive monitoring of vital signs, urine output, and respiratory function
translations.Extensão crítica de superfície corporal: Critical extent of burned body surface area, with significant life risk.
translations.• Internação imediata em centro de queim:
• Immediate admission to burn center/intensive care
• Aggressive fluid resuscitation and continuous hemodynamic monitoring
• Assessment of airway and respiratory function (risk of inhalation injury)
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a ser invadido: Increased susceptibility to invasion by pathogenic organisms related to loss of skin barrier.
translations.Experiência sensorial e emocional desagr: Unpleasant sensory and emotional experience associated with actual tissue injury, sudden onset, with intensity from mild to severe.
translations.Reposição volêmica adequada ao percentua: Adequate fluid resuscitation for estimated percentage
translations.Estimar a superfície corporal queimada u: Estimate burned body surface area using the Rule of Nines
translations.Calcular a reposição volêmica conforme p: Calculate fluid resuscitation per institutional protocol
translations.Monitorar sinais vitais, diurese e perfu: Monitor vital signs, urine output, and perfusion continuously
translations.Avaliar via aérea e sinais de lesão por: Assess airway and signs of inhalation injury in facial/thoracic burns
translations.A Regra dos Nove foi desenvolvida por Al: The Rule of Nines was developed by Alexander Wallace, dividing the adult body surface into multiples of 9% (head and neck, each upper limb, anterior trunk, posterior trunk, each lower limb) plus 1% for the genital region, totaling 100% of body surface area.
translations.Amplamente utilizada na avaliação inicia: Widely used in the initial assessment of burns in emergency services due to its speed and simplicity, especially useful in extensive burns. For children, specific charts (such as the Lund-Browder chart) are used because infant body proportions differ significantly from adults.
translations.É uma ferramenta de estimativa rápida am: It is a rapid estimation tool widely accepted in emergency clinical practice, although less accurate than more detailed methods (such as the Lund-Browder chart) for burns with irregular distribution or in children.
translations.A Regra dos Nove desta calculadora é esp: The Rule of Nines in this calculator is specific to adults and should not be applied to small children, who have different body proportions (proportionally larger head). For partial burn areas within a region or irregularly distributed burns, the patient's palm method (approximately 1% of body surface area) can complement the estimate.
translations.A Regra dos Nove é específica para adult: The Rule of Nines is specific to adults — use specific charts (Lund-Browder) for small children.
translations.Para áreas parciais ou de distribuição i: For partial or irregularly distributed areas, use the patient's palm method (approximately 1% of body surface area) to complement the estimate.
translations.A gravidade real da queimadura depende t: The actual severity of the burn also depends on depth (superficial, partial thickness, full thickness) and location (face, hands, joints, genitalia are special areas).
translations.Reavalie a estimativa de SCQ conforme a: Reassess the TBSA estimate as the burn evolves and becomes better demarcated.
translations.Quanto vale cada membro superior na Regr: What is the value of each upper limb in the Rule of Nines for adults?
translations.Cada membro superior corresponde a 9% da: Each upper limb corresponds to 9% of total body surface area in the adult Rule of Nines.
translations.A Regra dos Nove adulta pode ser usada e: Can the adult Rule of Nines be used in small children without adjustment?
translations.Não, crianças pequenas têm proporções co: No, small children have different body proportions and require specific charts
translations.Sim, mas apenas para queimaduras leves: Yes, but only for mild burns
translations.Crianças pequenas têm a cabeça proporcio: Small children have a proportionally larger head and proportionally smaller lower limbs, requiring specific charts such as the Lund-Browder chart.
translations.Qual método pode complementar a Regra do: Which method can complement the Rule of Nines in partial or irregularly distributed areas?
translations.Método da palma da mão do paciente (~1%: Patient's palm method (~1% of body surface area)
translations.O método da palma da mão do próprio paci: The patient's own palm method (including fingers), which corresponds to approximately 1% of body surface area, is useful for estimating small or irregularly distributed areas.
translations.A Regra dos Nove serve para crianças?: Is the Rule of Nines suitable for children?
translations.Não deve ser usada em crianças pequenas: It should not be used in small children without adjustment, as their body proportions differ from adults. The use of specific charts, such as the Lund-Browder chart, is recommended in this population.
translations.A SCQ calculada aqui já indica a gravida: Does the TBSA calculated here already indicate the complete severity of the burn?
translations.Não isoladamente. A gravidade da queimad: Not in isolation. The severity of the burn also depends on the depth of the injury (superficial, partial thickness, or full thickness) and location (face, hands, joints, and genitalia are considered special areas of greater severity), in addition to the percentage extent.
translations.Como funciona a Regra dos Nove: How does the Rule of Nines work?
translations.<p>A Regra dos Nove divide a superfície:
The Rule of Nines divides the adult body surface into segments: head and neck (9%), each upper limb (9%), anterior trunk (18%), posterior trunk (18%), each lower limb (18%), and genitalia/perineum (1%), summing the percentages of affected areas to estimate the total body surface area burned (TBSA).

Quick guide: less than 10% = Minor | 10-19% = Moderate | 20-39% = Severe | ≥40% = Critical.

translations.A segurança no preparo e na administraçã: Safety in the preparation and administration of medications is essential to prevent errors, ensure effective treatment, and patient safety. The

translations.Erros de medicação podem causar danos gr: Medication errors can cause serious harm or death. Following these protocols drastically reduces adverse events and legally protects the healthcare professional.

translations.O escore SAPS 3 (do inglês Simplified Ac: The SAPS 3 score (Simplified Acute Physiology Score 3) is an index developed to predict hospital mortality at ICU admission. The score was developed from a retrospective study of 16,784 patients, aiming to develop a model to assess disease severity and predict vital status at hospital discharge based on ICU admission data. The study was published in 2006, and it is a derivative of SAPS 2 from 1993. The latter had been developed from a study of 13,152 patients, also aiming to develop a model to update and validate a new acute physiology score for hospital mortality probability – updating the SAPS from 1984.
